Package bufferpool provides a pool of bytes buffers.

```go
func GetBuffer() (buf *bytes.Buffer)
```

GetBuffer returns a buffer from the pool.

<a name="PutBuffer"></a>
## func [PutBuffer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/bufferpool/bufpool.go#L35>)

```go
func PutBuffer(buf *bytes.Buffer)
```

PutBuffer returns a buffer to the pool. The buffer is reset before it is put back into circulation.

<a name="Method"></a>
## type [Method](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/codegen/methods.go#L256-L276>)

Method holds enough information about it to recreate it.

```go
type Method struct {
    // The interface we extracted this method from.
    Owner reflect.Type

    // String version of the above, on the form PACKAGE.NAME, e.g.
    // page.Page
    OwnerName string

    // Method name.
    Name string

    // Imports needed to satisfy the method signature.
    Imports []string

    // Argument types, including any package prefix, e.g. string, int, interface{},
    // net.Url
    In  []string

    // Return types.
    Out []string
}
```


<a name="Methods"></a>
## type [Methods](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/codegen/methods.go#L352>)

Methods represents a list of methods for one or more interfaces. The order matches the defined order in their source file\(s\).

```go
type Methods []Method
```

<a name="Methods.Imports"></a>
### func \(Methods\) [Imports](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/codegen/methods.go#L356>)

```go
func (m Methods) Imports() []string
```

Imports returns a sorted list of package imports needed to satisfy the signatures of all methods.

```go
func (m Methods) ToMarshalJSON(receiver, pkgPath string, excludes ...string) (string, []string)
```

ToMarshalJSON creates a MarshalJSON method for these methods. Any method name matching any of the regexps in excludes will be ignored.

<a name="Eq"></a>
## func [Eq](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/compare/compare.go#L44>)

```go
func Eq(v1, v2 any) bool
```

Eq returns whether v1 is equal to v2. It will use the Eqer interface if implemented, which defines equals when two value are interchangeable in the Hugo templates.

## func [LessStrings](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/compare/compare_strings.go#L111>)




<a name="Comparer"></a>
## type [Comparer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/compare/compare.go#L36-L38>)

Comparer can be used to compare two values. This will be used when using the le, ge etc. operators in the templates. Compare returns \-1 if the given version is less than, 0 if equal and 1 if greater than the running version.

```go
type Comparer interface {
    Compare(other any) int
}
```

<a name="Eqer"></a>
## type [Eqer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/compare/compare.go#L19-L23>)

Eqer can be used to determine if this value is equal to the other. The semantics of equals is that the two value are interchangeable in the Hugo templates.

```go
type Eqer interface {
    // Eq returns whether this value is equal to the other.
    // This is for internal use.
    Eq(other any) bool
}
```

<a name="ProbablyEqer"></a>
## type [ProbablyEqer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/compare/compare.go#L27-L30>)

ProbablyEqer is an equal check that may return false positives, but never a false negative.

```go
type ProbablyEqer interface {
    // For internal use.
    ProbablyEq(other any) bool
}
```

## Variables

<a name="DefaultConfigNames"></a>

```go
var (
    // See issue #8979 for context.
    // Hugo has always used config.toml etc. as the default config file name.
    // But hugo.toml is a more descriptive name, but we need to check for both.
    DefaultConfigNames = []string{"hugo", "config"}

    DefaultConfigNamesSet = make(map[string]bool)

    ValidConfigFileExtensions = []string{"toml", "yaml", "yml", "json"}
)
```

<a name="FromFileToMap"></a>
## func [FromFileToMap](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/configLoader.go#L107>)

```go
func FromFileToMap(fs afero.Fs, filename string) (map[string]any, error)
```

FromFileToMap is the same as FromFile, but it returns the config values as a simple map.

<a name="FromMapToTOMLString"></a>
## func [FromMapToTOMLString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/configLoader.go#L71>)

```go
func FromMapToTOMLString(v map[string]any) string
```

FromMapToTOMLString converts the given map to a TOML string. This is useful in tests.

<a name="GetMemoryLimit"></a>
## func [GetMemoryLimit](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/env.go#L47>)

```go
func GetMemoryLimit() uint64
```

GetMemoryLimit returns the upper memory limit in bytes for Hugo's in\-memory caches. Note that this does not represent "all of the memory" that Hugo will use, so it needs to be set to a lower number than the available system memory. It will read from the HUGO\_MEMORYLIMIT \(in Gigabytes\) environment variable. If that is not set, it will set aside a quarter of the total system memory.

<a name="GetNumWorkerMultiplier"></a>
## func [GetNumWorkerMultiplier](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/env.go#L33>)

```go
func GetNumWorkerMultiplier() int
```

GetNumWorkerMultiplier returns the base value used to calculate the number of workers to use for Hugo's parallel execution. It returns the value in HUGO\_NUMWORKERMULTIPLIER OS env variable if set to a positive integer, else the number of logical CPUs.

<a name="GetStringSlicePreserveString"></a>
## func [GetStringSlicePreserveString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/configProvider.go#L117>)

```go
func GetStringSlicePreserveString(cfg Provider, key string) []string
```

GetStringSlicePreserveString returns a string slice from the given config and key. It differs from the GetStringSlice method in that if the config value is a string, we do not attempt to split it into fields.

## func [IsValidConfigFilename](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/configLoader.go#L56>)

```go

```

IsValidConfigFilename returns whether filename is one of the supported config formats in Hugo.

## type [AllProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/configProvider.go#L28-L84>)

AllProvider is a sub set of all config settings.

```go
type AllProvider interface {
    Language() any
    LanguageIndex() int
    Languages() any
    LanguagePrefix() string
    BaseURL() urls.BaseURL
    BaseURLLiveReload() urls.BaseURL
    PathParser() *paths.PathParser
    Environment() string
    IsMultihost() bool
    IsMultilingual() bool
    NoBuildLock() bool
    BaseConfig() BaseConfig
    Dirs() CommonDirs
    Quiet() bool
    DirsBase() CommonDirs
    FileCaches() any
    ContentTypes() ContentTypesProvider
    GetConfigSection(string) any
    GetConfig() any
    CanonifyURLs() bool
    DisablePathToLower() bool
    RemovePathAccents() bool
    IsUglyURLs(section string) bool
    DefaultContentLanguage() string
    DefaultContentLanguageInSubdir() bool
    DefaultContentRoleInSubdir() bool
    DefaultContentVersionInSubdir() bool
    DefaultContentsitesMatrix() *sitesmatrix.IntSets
    AllSitesMatrix() *sitesmatrix.IntSets
    IsKindEnabled(string) bool
    IsLangDisabled(string) bool
    SummaryLength() int
    Pagination() Pagination
    BuildExpired() bool
    BuildFuture() bool
    BuildDrafts() bool
    Running() bool
    Watching() bool
    NewIdentityManager(opts ...identity.ManagerOption) identity.Manager
    FastRenderMode() bool
    PrintUnusedTemplates() bool
    EnableMissingTranslationPlaceholders() bool
    TemplateMetrics() bool
    TemplateMetricsHints() bool
    PrintI18nWarnings() bool
    CreateTitle(s string) string
    IgnoreFile(s string) bool
    NewContentEditor() string
    Timeout() time.Duration
    StaticDirs() []string
    IgnoredLogs() map[string]bool
    WorkingDir() string
    EnableEmoji() bool
    ConfiguredDimensions() *sitesmatrix.ConfiguredDimensions
    CacheDirMisc() string
}
```

<a name="BuildConfig"></a>
## type [BuildConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/commonConfig.go#L98-L114>)

BuildConfig holds some build related configuration.

```go
type BuildConfig struct {
    // When to use the resource file cache.
    // One of never, fallback, always. Default is fallback
    UseResourceCacheWhen string

    // When enabled, will collect and write a hugo_stats.json with some build
    // related aggregated data (e.g. CSS class names).
    // Note that this was a bool <= v0.115.0.
    BuildStats BuildStats

    // Can be used to toggle off writing of the IntelliSense /assets/jsconfig.js
    // file.
    NoJSConfigInAssets bool

    // Can used to control how the resource cache gets evicted on rebuilds.
    CacheBusters []CacheBuster
}
```

<a name="CacheBuster"></a>
## type [CacheBuster](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/commonConfig.go#L392-L401>)

CacheBuster configures cache busting for assets.

```go
type CacheBuster struct {
    // Trigger for files matching this regexp.
    Source string

    // Cache bust targets matching this regexp.
    // This regexp can contain group matches (e.g. $1) from the source regexp.
    Target string
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="CommonDirs"></a>
## type [CommonDirs](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/commonConfig.go#L41-L77>)



```go
type CommonDirs struct {
    // The directory where Hugo will look for themes.
    ThemesDir string

    // Where to put the generated files.
    PublishDir string

    // The directory to put the generated resources files. This directory should in most situations be considered temporary
    // and not be committed to version control. But there may be cached content in here that you want to keep,
    // e.g. resources/_gen/images for performance reasons or CSS built from SASS when your CI server doesn't have the full setup.
    ResourceDir string

    // The project root directory.
    WorkingDir string

    // The root directory for all cache files.
    CacheDir string

    // The content source directory.
    // Deprecated: Use module mounts.
    ContentDir string
    // Deprecated: Use module mounts.
    // The data source directory.
    DataDir string
    // Deprecated: Use module mounts.
    // The layout source directory.
    LayoutDir string
    // Deprecated: Use module mounts.
    // The i18n source directory.
    I18nDir string
    // Deprecated: Use module mounts.
    // The archetypes source directory.
    ArcheTypeDir string
    // Deprecated: Use module mounts.
    // The assets source directory.
    AssetDir string
}
```

<a name="ConfigNamespace"></a>
## type [ConfigNamespace](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/namespace.go#L53-L63>)

ConfigNamespace holds a Hugo configuration namespace. The construct looks a little odd, but it's built to make the configuration elements both self\-documenting and contained in a common structure.

```go
type ConfigNamespace[S, C any] struct {
    // SourceStructure represents the source configuration with any defaults applied.
    // This is used for documentation and printing of the configuration setup to the user.
    SourceStructure any

    // SourceHash is a hash of the source configuration before any defaults gets applied.
    SourceHash string

    // Config is the final configuration as used by Hugo.
    Config C
}
```

<a name="DecodeNamespace"></a>
### func [DecodeNamespace](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/namespace.go#L22>)

```go
func DecodeNamespace[S, C any](configSource any, buildConfig func(any) (C, any, error)) (*ConfigNamespace[S, C], error)
```



<a name="ConfigNamespace[S, C].MarshalJSON"></a>
### func \(\*ConfigNamespace\[S, C\]\) [MarshalJSON](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/namespace.go#L66>)

```go
func (ns *ConfigNamespace[S, C]) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error)
```

MarshalJSON marshals the source structure.

<a name="ConfigNamespace[S, C].Signature"></a>
### func \(\*ConfigNamespace\[S, C\]\) [Signature](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/namespace.go#L72>)

```go
func (ns *ConfigNamespace[S, C]) Signature() S
```

Signature returns the signature of the source structure. Note that this is for documentation purposes only and SourceStructure may not always be cast to S \(it's usually just a map\).

<a name="ContentTypesProvider"></a>
## type [ContentTypesProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/configProvider.go#L88-L93>)

We cannot import the media package as that would create a circular dependency. This interface defines a subset of what media.ContentTypes provides.

```go
type ContentTypesProvider interface {
    IsContentSuffix(suffix string) bool
    IsContentFile(filename string) bool
    IsIndexContentFile(filename string) bool
    IsHTMLSuffix(suffix string) bool
}
```

<a name="PageConfig"></a>
## type [PageConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/commonConfig.go#L499-L505>)

PageConfig configures the behavior of pages.

```go
type PageConfig struct {
    // Sort order for Page.Next and Page.Prev. Default "desc" (the default page sort order in Hugo).
    NextPrevSortOrder string

    // Sort order for Page.NextInSection and Page.PrevInSection. Default "desc".
    NextPrevInSectionSortOrder string
}
```

<a name="Pagination"></a>
## type [Pagination](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/commonConfig.go#L487-L496>)

Pagination configures the pagination behavior.

```go
type Pagination struct {
    //  Default number of elements per pager in pagination.
    PagerSize int

    // The path element used during pagination.
    Path string

    // Whether to disable generation of alias for the first pagination page.
    DisableAliases bool
}
```

<a name="Provider"></a>
## type [Provider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/configProvider.go#L96-L112>)

Provider provides the configuration settings for Hugo.

```go
type Provider interface {
    GetString(key string) string
    GetInt(key string) int
    GetBool(key string) bool
    GetParams(key string) hmaps.Params
    GetStringMap(key string) map[string]any
    GetStringMapString(key string) map[string]string
    GetStringSlice(key string) []string
    Get(key string) any
    Set(key string, value any)
    Keys() []string
    Merge(key string, value any)
    SetDefaults(params hmaps.Params)
    SetDefaultMergeStrategy()
    WalkParams(walkFn func(params ...hmaps.KeyParams) bool)
    IsSet(key string) bool
}
```

<a name="Redirect"></a>
## type [Redirect](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/commonConfig.go#L365-L389>)



```go
type Redirect struct {
    // From is the Glob pattern to match.
    // One of From or FromRe must be set.
    From string

    // FromRe is the regexp to match.
    // This regexp can contain group matches (e.g. $1) that can be used in the To field.
    // One of From or FromRe must be set.
    FromRe string

    // To is the target URL.
    To  string

    // Headers to match for the redirect.
    // This maps the HTTP header name to a Glob pattern with values to match.
    // If the map is empty, the redirect will always be triggered.
    FromHeaders map[string]string

    // HTTP status code to use for the redirect.
    // A status code of 200 will trigger a URL rewrite.
    Status int

    // Forcode redirect, even if original request path exists.
    Force bool
}
```

<a name="Redirect.IsZero"></a>
### func \(Redirect\) [IsZero](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/config/commonConfig.go#L453>)


# create

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/create"
```

Package create provides functions to create new content.

## Index

- [Constants](<#constants>)
- [func NewContent\(h \*hugolib.HugoSites, kind, targetPath string, force bool\) error](<#NewContent>)


## Constants

<a name="DefaultArchetypeTemplateTemplate"></a>

```go
const (
    // DefaultArchetypeTemplateTemplate is the template used in 'hugo new project'
    // and the template we use as a fall back.
    DefaultArchetypeTemplateTemplate = `---
title: "{{ replace .File.ContentBaseName "-" " " | title }}"
date: {{ .Date }}
draft: true
---

`
)
```

<a name="NewContent"></a>
## func [NewContent](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/create/content.go#L52>)

```go
func NewContent(h *hugolib.HugoSites, kind, targetPath string, force bool) error
```

NewContent creates a new content file in h \(or a full bundle if the archetype is a directory\) in targetPath.

```go
type DeferredExecutions struct {
    // A set of filenames in /public that
    // contains a post-processing prefix.
```

<a name="Deps"></a>
## type [Deps](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/deps/deps.go#L57-L127>)

Deps holds dependencies used by many. There will be normally only one instance of deps in play at a given time, i.e. one per Site built.

```go
type Deps struct {
    // The logger to use.
    Log loggers.Logger `json:"-"`

    ExecHelper *hexec.Exec

    // The file systems to use.
    Fs  *hugofs.Fs `json:"-"`

    // The PathSpec to use
    *helpers.PathSpec `json:"-"`

    // The ContentSpec to use
    *helpers.ContentSpec `json:"-"`

    // The SourceSpec to use
    SourceSpec *source.SourceSpec `json:"-"`

    // The Resource Spec to use
    ResourceSpec *resources.Spec

    // The configuration to use
    Conf config.AllProvider `json:"-"`

    // The memory cache to use.
    MemCache *dynacache.Cache

    // The translation func to use
    Translate func(ctx context.Context, translationID string, templateData any) string `json:"-"`

    // The site building.
    Site page.Site

    TemplateStore *tplimpl.TemplateStore

    // Used in tests
    OverloadedTemplateFuncs map[string]any

    TranslationProvider ResourceProvider

    Metrics metrics.Provider

    // BuildStartListeners will be notified before a build starts.
    BuildStartListeners *Listeners[any]

    // BuildEndListeners will be notified after a build finishes.
    BuildEndListeners *Listeners[any]

    // OnChangeListeners will be notified when something changes.
    OnChangeListeners *Listeners[identity.Identity]

    // Resources that gets closed when the build is done or the server shuts down.
    BuildClosers *types.Closers

    // This is common/global for all sites.
    BuildState *BuildState

    // Misc counters.
    Counters *Counters

    // Holds RPC dispatchers for Katex etc.
    // TODO(bep) rethink this re. a plugin setup, but this will have to do for now.
    WasmDispatchers *warpc.Dispatchers

    // The JS batcher client.
    JSBatcherClient js.BatcherClient
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="DepsCfg"></a>
## type [DepsCfg](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/deps/deps.go#L405-L438>)

DepsCfg contains configuration options that can be used to configure Hugo on a global level, i.e. logging etc. Nil values will be given default values.

```go
type DepsCfg struct {
    // The logger to use. Only set in some tests.
    // TODO(bep) get rid of this.
    TestLogger loggers.Logger

    // The logging level to use.
    LogLevel logg.Level

    // Logging output.
    StdErr io.Writer

    // The console output.
    StdOut io.Writer

    // The file systems to use
    Fs  *hugofs.Fs

    // The Site in use
    Site page.Site

    Configs *allconfig.Configs

    // Template handling.
    TemplateProvider ResourceProvider

    // i18n handling.
    TranslationProvider ResourceProvider

    // Build triggered by the IntegrationTest framework.
    IsIntegrationTest bool

    // ChangesFromBuild for changes passed back to the server/watch process.
    ChangesFromBuild chan []identity.Identity
}
```

# helpers

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/helpers"
```

Package helpers implements general utility functions that work with and on content. The helper functions defined here lay down the foundation of how Hugo works with files and filepaths, and perform string operations on content.

  - [func \(p \*PathSpec\) URLizeFilename\(filename string\) string](<#PathSpec.URLizeFilename>)
- [type ProcessingStats](<#ProcessingStats>)
  - [func NewProcessingStats\(name string\) \*ProcessingStats](<#NewProcessingStats>)
  - [func \(s \*ProcessingStats\) Add\(counter \*uint64, amount int\)](<#ProcessingStats.Add>)
  - [func \(s \*ProcessingStats\) Incr\(counter \*uint64\)](<#ProcessingStats.Incr>)


## Constants

<a name="FilePathSeparator"></a>FilePathSeparator as defined by os.Separator.

```go
const FilePathSeparator = string(filepath.Separator)
```

<a name="BytesToHTML"></a>
## func [BytesToHTML](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/content.go#L90>)

```go
func BytesToHTML(b []byte) template.HTML
```

BytesToHTML converts bytes to type template.HTML.

<a name="DirExists"></a>
## func [DirExists](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L363>)

```go
func DirExists(path string, fs afero.Fs) (bool, error)
```

DirExists checks if a path exists and is a directory.

<a name="Emojify"></a>
## func [Emojify](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/emoji.go#L36>)

```go
func Emojify(source []byte) []byte
```

Emojify "emojifies" the input source. Note that the input byte slice will be modified if needed. See http://www.emoji-cheat-sheet.com/

<a name="Exists"></a>
## func [Exists](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L386>)

```go
func Exists(path string, fs afero.Fs) (bool, error)
```

Exists checks if a file or directory exists.

<a name="ExtractAndGroupRootPaths"></a>
## func [ExtractAndGroupRootPaths](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L135>)

```go
func ExtractAndGroupRootPaths(in []string) []string
```

ExtractAndGroupRootPaths extracts and groups root paths from the supplied list of paths. Note that the in slice will be sorted in place.

<a name="ExtractTOC"></a>
## func [ExtractTOC](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/content.go#L95>)

```go
func ExtractTOC(content []byte) (newcontent []byte, toc []byte)
```

ExtractTOC extracts Table of Contents from content.

<a name="FindCWD"></a>
## func [FindCWD](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L202>)

```go
func FindCWD() (string, error)
```

FindCWD returns the current working directory from where the Hugo executable is run.

<a name="FirstUpper"></a>
## func [FirstUpper](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/general.go#L53>)

```go
func FirstUpper(s string) string
```

FirstUpper returns a string with the first character as upper case.

<a name="FormatByteCount"></a>
## func [FormatByteCount](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/general.go#L236>)

```go
func FormatByteCount(bc uint64) string
```

FormatByteCount pretty formats b.

<a name="GetCacheDir"></a>
## func [GetCacheDir](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L288>)

```go
func GetCacheDir(fs afero.Fs, cacheDir string) (string, error)
```

GetCacheDir returns a cache dir from the given filesystem and config. The dir will be created if it does not exist.

<a name="GetDottedRelativePath"></a>
## func [GetDottedRelativePath](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L89>)

```go
func GetDottedRelativePath(inPath string) string
```

GetDottedRelativePath expects a relative path starting after the content directory. It returns a relative path with dots \(".."\) navigating up the path structure.

<a name="GetTempDir"></a>
## func [GetTempDir](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L358>)

```go
func GetTempDir(subPath string, fs afero.Fs) string
```

GetTempDir returns a temporary directory with the given sub path.

<a name="GetTitleFunc"></a>
## func [GetTitleFunc](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/general.go#L135>)

```go
func GetTitleFunc(style string) func(s string) string
```

GetTitleFunc returns a func that can be used to transform a string to title case.

### The supported styles are

\- "Go" \(strings.Title\) \- "AP" \(see https://www.apstylebook.com/\) \- "Chicago" \(see https://www.chicagomanualofstyle.org/home.html\) \- "FirstUpper" \(only the first character is upper case\) \- "None" \(no transformation\)

If an unknown or empty style is provided, AP style is what you get.

<a name="HasStringsPrefix"></a>
## func [HasStringsPrefix](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/general.go#L154>)

```go
func HasStringsPrefix(s, prefix []string) bool
```

HasStringsPrefix tests whether the string slice s begins with prefix slice s.

<a name="HasStringsSuffix"></a>
## func [HasStringsSuffix](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/general.go#L159>)

```go
func HasStringsSuffix(s, suffix []string) bool
```

HasStringsSuffix tests whether the string slice s ends with suffix slice s.

```go
func IsDir(path string, fs afero.Fs) (bool, error)
```

IsDir checks if a given path is a directory.

<a name="IsEmpty"></a>
## func [IsEmpty](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L373>)

```go
func IsEmpty(path string, fs afero.Fs) (bool, error)
```

IsEmpty checks if a given path is empty, meaning it doesn't contain any regular files.

```go
func IsWhitespace(r rune) bool
```

IsWhitespace determines if the given rune is whitespace.

## func [MakePathRelative](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L75>)

```go
func MakePathRelative(inPath string, possibleDirectories ...string) (string, error)
```

MakeTitleInPath converts the path given to a suitable title, trimming whitespace

<a name="MakeTitle"></a>
## func [MakeTitle](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L70>)

```go
func MakeTitle(inpath string) string
```

MakeTitle converts the path given to a suitable title, trimming whitespace and replacing hyphens with whitespace.

<a name="OpenFileForWriting"></a>
## func [OpenFileForWriting](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L268>)

```go
func OpenFileForWriting(fs afero.Fs, filename string) (afero.File, error)
```

OpenFileForWriting opens or creates the given file. If the target directory does not exist, it gets created.

<a name="OpenFilesForWriting"></a>

```go
func OpenFilesForWriting(fs afero.Fs, filenames ...string) (io.WriteCloser, error)
```

OpenFilesForWriting opens all the given filenames for writing.

<a name="PrintFs"></a>
## func [PrintFs](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/general.go#L217>)

```go
func PrintFs(fs afero.Fs, path string, w io.Writer)
```

PrintFs prints the given filesystem to the given writer starting from the given path. This is useful for debugging.

<a name="ProcessingStatsTable"></a>
## func [ProcessingStatsTable](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/processing_stats.go#L72>)

```go
func ProcessingStatsTable(w io.Writer, stats ...*ProcessingStats)
```

ProcessingStatsTable writes a table\-formatted representation of stats to w.

```go
func ReaderContains(r io.Reader, subslice []byte) bool
```

ReaderContains reports whether subslice is within r.

<a name="ReaderToBytes"></a>
## func [ReaderToBytes](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/general.go#L63>)

```go
func ReaderToBytes(lines io.Reader) []byte
```

ReaderToBytes takes an io.Reader argument, reads from it and returns bytes.

<a name="ReaderToString"></a>
## func [ReaderToString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/general.go#L78>)

```go
func ReaderToString(lines io.Reader) string
```

ReaderToString is the same as ReaderToBytes, but returns a string.

<a name="SafeWriteToDisk"></a>
## func [SafeWriteToDisk](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L240>)

```go
func SafeWriteToDisk(inpath string, r io.Reader, fs afero.Fs) (err error)
```

SafeWriteToDisk is the same as WriteToDisk but it also checks to see if file/directory already exists.

<a name="SliceToLower"></a>
## func [SliceToLower](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/general.go#L176>)

```go
func SliceToLower(s []string) []string
```

SliceToLower goes through the source slice and lowers all values.

<a name="StringSliceToList"></a>
## func [StringSliceToList](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/general.go#L192>)

```go
func StringSliceToList(s []string, c string) string
```

StringSliceToList formats a string slice into a human\-readable list. It joins the elements of the slice s with commas, using an Oxford comma, and precedes the final element with the conjunction c.

<a name="TCPListen"></a>
## func [TCPListen](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/general.go#L39>)

```go
func TCPListen() (net.Listener, *net.TCPAddr, error)
```

TCPListen starts listening on a valid TCP port.

<a name="TotalWords"></a>
## func [TotalWords](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/content.go#L151>)

```go
func TotalWords(s string) int
```

TotalWords counts instance of one or more consecutive white space characters, as defined by unicode.IsSpace, in s. This is a cheaper way of word counting than the obvious len\(strings.Fields\(s\)\).

<a name="Walk"></a>
## func [Walk](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L225>)

```go
func Walk(fs afero.Fs, root string, walker hugofs.WalkFunc) error
```

Walk walks the file tree rooted at root, calling walkFn for each file or directory in the tree, including root.

## func [WriteToDisk](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L245>)

```go
func WriteToDisk(inpath string, r io.Reader, fs afero.Fs) (err error)
```

WriteToDisk writes content to disk.

<a name="ContentSpec"></a>
## type [ContentSpec](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/content.go#L40-L44>)

ContentSpec provides functionality to render markdown content.

```go
type ContentSpec struct {
    Converters markup.ConverterProvider

    Cfg config.AllProvider
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewContentSpec"></a>
### func [NewContentSpec](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/content.go#L48>)

```go
func NewContentSpec(cfg config.AllProvider, logger loggers.Logger, contentFs afero.Fs, ex *hexec.Exec) (*ContentSpec, error)
```

NewContentSpec returns a ContentSpec initialized with the appropriate fields from the given config.Provider.

<a name="ContentSpec.ResolveMarkup"></a>
### func \(\*ContentSpec\) [ResolveMarkup](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/content.go#L134>)

```go
func (c *ContentSpec) ResolveMarkup(in string) string
```



<a name="ContentSpec.SanitizeAnchorName"></a>
### func \(\*ContentSpec\) [SanitizeAnchorName](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/content.go#L130>)

```go
func (c *ContentSpec) SanitizeAnchorName(s string) string
```



<a name="ContentSpec.TrimShortHTML"></a>
### func \(\*ContentSpec\) [TrimShortHTML](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/content.go#L167>)

```go
func (c *ContentSpec) TrimShortHTML(input []byte, markup string) []byte
```

TrimShortHTML removes the outer tags from HTML input where \(a\) the opening tag is present only once with the input, and \(b\) the opening and closing tags wrap the input after white space removal.

<a name="NamedSlice"></a>
## type [NamedSlice](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L121-L124>)



```go
type NamedSlice struct {
    Name  string
    Slice []string
}
```

<a name="NamedSlice.String"></a>
### func \(NamedSlice\) [String](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L126>)

```go
func (n NamedSlice) String() string
```



<a name="PathSpec"></a>
## type [PathSpec](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/pathspec.go#L27-L35>)

PathSpec holds methods that decides how paths in URLs and files in Hugo should look like.

```go
type PathSpec struct {
    *paths.Paths
    *filesystems.BaseFs

    ProcessingStats *ProcessingStats

    // The file systems to use
    Fs  *hugofs.Fs
}
```

<a name="NewPathSpec"></a>
### func [NewPathSpec](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/pathspec.go#L39>)

```go
func NewPathSpec(fs *hugofs.Fs, cfg config.AllProvider, logger loggers.Logger, baseBaseFs *filesystems.BaseFs) (*PathSpec, error)
```

NewPathSpec creates a new PathSpec from the given filesystems and language. If an existing BaseFs is provided, parts of that is reused.

<a name="PathSpec.AbsURL"></a>
### func \(\*PathSpec\) [AbsURL](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/url.go#L53>)

```go
func (p *PathSpec) AbsURL(in string, addLanguage bool) string
```

AbsURL creates an absolute URL from the relative path given and the BaseURL set in config.

<a name="PathSpec.IsAbsURL"></a>
### func \(\*PathSpec\) [IsAbsURL](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/url.go#L104>)

```go
func (p *PathSpec) IsAbsURL(in string) (bool, error)
```



<a name="PathSpec.MakePath"></a>
### func \(\*PathSpec\) [MakePath](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L45>)

```go
func (p *PathSpec) MakePath(s string) string
```

MakePath takes a string with any characters and replace it so the string could be used in a path. It does so by creating a Unicode\-sanitized string, with the spaces replaced, whilst preserving the original casing of the string. E.g. Social Media \-\> Social\-Media

<a name="PathSpec.MakePathSanitized"></a>
### func \(\*PathSpec\) [MakePathSanitized](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L61>)

```go
func (p *PathSpec) MakePathSanitized(s string) string
```

MakePathSanitized creates a Unicode\-sanitized string, with the spaces replaced

<a name="PathSpec.MakePathsSanitized"></a>
### func \(\*PathSpec\) [MakePathsSanitized](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/path.go#L54>)

```go
func (p *PathSpec) MakePathsSanitized(paths []string)
```

MakePathsSanitized applies MakePathSanitized on every item in the slice

<a name="PathSpec.PermalinkForBaseURL"></a>
### func \(\*PathSpec\) [PermalinkForBaseURL](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/pathspec.go#L67>)

```go
func (p *PathSpec) PermalinkForBaseURL(link, baseURL string) string
```

PermalinkForBaseURL creates a permalink from the given link and baseURL.

<a name="PathSpec.PrependBasePath"></a>
### func \(\*PathSpec\) [PrependBasePath](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/url.go#L177>)

```go
func (p *PathSpec) PrependBasePath(rel string, isAbs bool) string
```

PrependBasePath prepends any baseURL sub\-folder to the given resource

<a name="PathSpec.RelURL"></a>
### func \(\*PathSpec\) [RelURL](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/url.go#L116>)

```go
func (p *PathSpec) RelURL(in string, addLanguage bool) string
```



<a name="PathSpec.URLEscape"></a>
### func \(\*PathSpec\) [URLEscape](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/url.go#L41>)

```go
func (p *PathSpec) URLEscape(uri string) string
```

URLEscape escapes unicode letters.

<a name="PathSpec.URLize"></a>
### func \(\*PathSpec\) [URLize](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/url.go#L30>)

```go
func (p *PathSpec) URLize(uri string) string
```

URLize is similar to MakePath, but with Unicode handling Example:

```
uri: Vim (text editor)
urlize: vim-text-editor
```

<a name="PathSpec.URLizeFilename"></a>
### func \(\*PathSpec\) [URLizeFilename](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/url.go#L36>)

```go
func (p *PathSpec) URLizeFilename(filename string) string
```

URLizeFilename creates an URL from a filename by escaping unicode letters and turn any filepath separator into forward slashes.

<a name="ProcessingStats"></a>
## type [ProcessingStats](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/processing_stats.go#L27-L37>)

ProcessingStats represents statistics about a site build.

```go
type ProcessingStats struct {
    Name string

    Pages           uint64
    PaginatorPages  uint64
    Static          uint64
    ProcessedImages uint64
    Files           uint64
    Aliases         uint64
    Cleaned         uint64
}
```

<a name="NewProcessingStats"></a>
### func [NewProcessingStats](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/processing_stats.go#L57>)

```go
func NewProcessingStats(name string) *ProcessingStats
```

NewProcessingStats returns a new ProcessingStats instance.

<a name="ProcessingStats.Add"></a>
### func \(\*ProcessingStats\) [Add](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/processing_stats.go#L67>)

```go
func (s *ProcessingStats) Add(counter *uint64, amount int)
```

Add adds an amount to a given counter.

<a name="ProcessingStats.Incr"></a>
### func \(\*ProcessingStats\) [Incr](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/helpers/processing_stats.go#L62>)

```go
func (s *ProcessingStats) Incr(counter *uint64)
```

Incr increments a given counter.

# htesting

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/htesting"
```

## Index

- [Variables](<#variables>)
- [func BailOut\(after time.Duration\)](<#BailOut>)
- [func CreateTempDir\(fs afero.Fs, prefix string\) \(string, func\(\), error\)](<#CreateTempDir>)
- [func DiffStringSlices\(slice1 \[\]string, slice2 \[\]string\) \[\]string](<#DiffStringSlices>)
- [func DiffStrings\(s1, s2 string\) \[\]string](<#DiffStrings>)
- [func GoMinorVersion\(\) int](<#GoMinorVersion>)
- [func IsCI\(\) bool](<#IsCI>)
- [func IsGitHubAction\(\) bool](<#IsGitHubAction>)
- [func IsRealCI\(\) bool](<#IsRealCI>)
- [func IsWindows\(\) bool](<#IsWindows>)
- [func RandBool\(\) bool](<#RandBool>)

- [func SupportsAll\(\) bool](<#SupportsAll>)
- [type PinnedRunner](<#PinnedRunner>)
  - [func NewPinnedRunner\(t testing.TB, pinnedTestRe string\) \*PinnedRunner](<#NewPinnedRunner>)
  - [func \(r \*PinnedRunner\) Run\(name string, f func\(c \*qt.C\)\) bool](<#PinnedRunner.Run>)


## Variables

<a name="IsTest"></a>IsTest reports whether we're running as a test.

```go
var IsTest bool
```

<a name="Rnd"></a>Rnd is used only for testing.

```go
var Rnd = rand.New(rand.NewSource(time.Now().UnixNano()))
```

<a name="BailOut"></a>
## func [BailOut](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L63>)

```go
func BailOut(after time.Duration)
```

BailOut panics with a stack trace after the given duration. Useful for hanging tests.

<a name="CreateTempDir"></a>
## func [CreateTempDir](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L45>)

```go
func CreateTempDir(fs afero.Fs, prefix string) (string, func(), error)
```

CreateTempDir creates a temp dir in the given filesystem and returns the dirname and a func that removes it when done.

<a name="DiffStringSlices"></a>
## func [DiffStringSlices](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L82>)

```go
func DiffStringSlices(slice1 []string, slice2 []string) []string
```

DiffStringSlices returns the difference between two string slices. Useful in tests. See: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/19374219/how-to-find-the-difference-between-two-slices-of-strings-in-golang

<a name="DiffStrings"></a>
## func [DiffStrings](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L104>)

```go
func DiffStrings(s1, s2 string) []string
```

DiffStrings splits the strings into fields and runs it into DiffStringSlices. Useful for tests.

<a name="GoMinorVersion"></a>
## func [GoMinorVersion](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L139>)

```go
func GoMinorVersion() int
```

GoMinorVersion returns the minor version of the current Go version, e.g. 16 for Go 1.16.

<a name="IsCI"></a>
## func [IsCI](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L117>)

```go
func IsCI() bool
```

IsCI reports whether we're running in a CI server.

<a name="IsGitHubAction"></a>
## func [IsGitHubAction](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L127>)

```go
func IsGitHubAction() bool
```

IsGitHubAction reports whether we're running in a GitHub Action.

<a name="IsRealCI"></a>
## func [IsRealCI](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L122>)

```go
func IsRealCI() bool
```

IsRealCI reports whether we're running in a CI server, but not in a local CI setup.

<a name="IsWindows"></a>
## func [IsWindows](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L144>)

```go
func IsWindows() bool
```

IsWindows reports whether this runs on Windows.

<a name="RandBool"></a>
## func [RandBool](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L74>)

```go
func RandBool() bool
```



<a name="SkipSlowTestUnlessCI"></a>
## func [SkipSlowTestUnlessCI](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L110>)

```go
func SkipSlowTestUnlessCI(t testing.TB)
```

SkipSlowTestUnlessCI skips the test unless we're running in a CI server. Note that you can set CI\_LOCAL=1 to run slow tests locally in a CI\-like setup.

<a name="SupportsAll"></a>
## func [SupportsAll](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L133>)

```go
func SupportsAll() bool
```

SupportsAll reports whether the running system supports all Hugo features, e.g. AsciiDoc, Pandoc etc.

<a name="PinnedRunner"></a>
## type [PinnedRunner](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L180-L183>)



```go
type PinnedRunner struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewPinnedRunner"></a>
### func [NewPinnedRunner](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L168>)

```go
func NewPinnedRunner(t testing.TB, pinnedTestRe string) *PinnedRunner
```

NewPinnedRunner creates a new runner that will only Run tests matching the given regexp. This is added mostly to use in combination with https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=windmilleng.vscode-go-autotest

<a name="PinnedRunner.Run"></a>
### func \(\*PinnedRunner\) [Run](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/htesting/test_helpers.go#L185>)

```go
func (r *PinnedRunner) Run(name string, f func(c *qt.C)) bool
```

```go

```

## Variables

<a name="AppendDirsMerger"></a>AppendDirsMerger merges two directories keeping all regular files with the first slice as the base. Duplicate directories in the second slice will be ignored.

```go
var AppendDirsMerger overlayfs.DirsMerger = func(lofi, bofi []fs.DirEntry) []fs.DirEntry {
    for _, fi1 := range bofi {
        var found bool

        if fi1.IsDir() {
            for _, fi2 := range lofi {
                if fi2.IsDir() && fi2.Name() == fi1.Name() {
                    found = true
                    break
                }
            }
        }
        if !found {
            lofi = append(lofi, fi1)
        }
    }

    return lofi
}
```

<a name="NewHashingFs"></a>
## func [NewHashingFs](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugofs/hashing_fs.go#L46>)

```go
func NewHashingFs(delegate afero.Fs, hashReceiver FileHashReceiver) afero.Fs
```

NewHashingFs creates a new filesystem that will receive MD5 checksums of any written file content on Close. Note that this is probably not a good idea for "full build" situations, but when doing fast render mode, the amount of files published is low, and it would be really nice to know exactly which of these files where actually changed. Note that this will only work for file operations that use the io.Writer to write content to file, but that is fine for the "publish content" use case.

<a name="FileMeta"></a>
## type [FileMeta](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugofs/fileinfo.go#L66-L97>)

FileMeta holds metadata about a file or directory.

```go
type FileMeta struct {
    PathInfo *paths.Path
    Name     string
    Filename string

    BaseDir       string
    SourceRoot    string
    Module        Module
    ModuleOrdinal int
    Component     string

    Weight    int
    IsProject bool
    Watch     bool

    // This file/directory  will be built for these sites.
    SitesMatrix sitesmatrix.VectorStore

    // This file/directory complements these other sites.
    SitesComplements sitesmatrix.VectorStore

    OpenFunc     func() (afero.File, error)
    JoinStatFunc func(name string) (FileMetaInfo, error)

    // Include only files or directories that match.
    InclusionFilter *hglob.FilenameFilter

    // Rename the name part of the file (not the directory).
    // Returns the new name and a boolean indicating if the file
    // should be included.
    Rename func(name string, toFrom bool) (string, bool)
}
```

<a name="Fs"></a>
## type [Fs](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugofs/fs.go#L34-L63>)

Fs holds the core filesystems used by Hugo.

```go
type Fs struct {
    // Source is Hugo's source file system.
    // Note that this will always be a "plain" Afero filesystem:
    // * afero.OsFs when running in production
    // * afero.MemMapFs for many of the tests.
    Source afero.Fs

    // PublishDir is where Hugo publishes its rendered content.
    // It's mounted inside publishDir (default /public).
    PublishDir afero.Fs

    // PublishDirStatic is the file system used for static files.
    PublishDirStatic afero.Fs

    // PublishDirServer is the file system used for serving the public directory with Hugo's development server.
    // This will typically be the same as PublishDir, but not if --renderStaticToDisk is set.
    PublishDirServer afero.Fs

    // Os is an OS file system.
    // NOTE: Field is currently unused.
    Os  afero.Fs

    // WorkingDirReadOnly is a read-only file system
    // restricted to the project working dir.
    WorkingDirReadOnly afero.Fs

    // WorkingDirWritable is a writable file system
    // restricted to the project working dir.
    WorkingDirWritable afero.Fs
}
```

<a name="Module"></a>
## type [Module](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugofs/fileinfo.go#L49-L63>)

A subset of modules.Module used to avoid circular dependencies. Note that it's safe to type assert to the full modules.Module when needed.

```go
type Module interface {
    // Returns the path to this module.
    // This will either be the module path, e.g. "github.com/gohugoio/myshortcodes",
    // or the path below your /theme folder, e.g. "mytheme".
    Path() string

    // Directory holding files for this module.
    Dir() string

    // Returns whether this is a Go Module.
    IsGoMod() bool

    // The module version.
    Version() string
}
```

## type [ReadDirWithContextDir](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugofs/fs.go#L256-L258>)



```go
type ReadDirWithContextDir interface {
    ReadDirWithContext(context context.Context, count int) ([]iofs.DirEntry, context.Context, error)
}
```

```go
type Reseter interface {

}
```

<a name="RootMapping"></a>
## type [RootMapping](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugofs/rootmapping_fs.go#L183-L196>)

RootMapping describes a virtual file or directory mount.

```go
type RootMapping struct {
    From          string    // The virtual mount.
    FromBase      string    // The base directory of the virtual mount.
    To            string    // The source directory or file.
    ToBase        string    // The base of To. May be empty if an absolute path was provided.
    Module        Module    // The module.
    ModuleOrdinal int       // The module ordinal starting with 0 which is the project.
    IsProject     bool      // Whether this is a mount in the main project.
    Meta          *FileMeta // File metadata (lang etc.)
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="RootMappingFs"></a>
## type [RootMappingFs](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugofs/rootmapping_fs.go#L227-L232>)

A RootMappingFs maps several roots into one. Note that the root of this filesystem is directories only, and they will be returned in Readdir and Readdirnames in the order given.

```go
type RootMappingFs struct {
    afero.Fs
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewRootMappingFs"></a>
### func [NewRootMappingFs](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugofs/rootmapping_fs.go#L44>)

```go
func NewRootMappingFs(fs afero.Fs, rms ...*RootMapping) (*RootMappingFs, error)
```

NewRootMappingFs creates a new RootMappingFs on top of the provided with root mappings with some optional metadata about the root. Note that From represents a virtual root that maps to the actual filename in To.

<a name="RootMappingFs.Filter"></a>
### func \(RootMappingFs\) [Filter](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugofs/rootmapping_fs.go#L276>)

```go
func (fs RootMappingFs) Filter(f func(m *RootMapping) bool) *RootMappingFs
```

Filter creates a copy of this filesystem with only mappings matching a filter.


```go
func (fs *RootMappingFs) Mounts(base string) ([]FileMetaInfo, error)
```

```go
func (fs *RootMappingFs) Open(name string) (afero.File, error)
```


<a name="RootMappingFs.Stat"></a>
### func \(\*RootMappingFs\) [Stat](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugofs/rootmapping_fs.go#L310>)

```go
func (fs *RootMappingFs) Stat(name string) (os.FileInfo, error)
```

Stat returns the os.FileInfo structure describing a given file. If there is an error, it will be of type \*os.PathError. If multiple roots are found, the last one will be used.

```go
func NewWalkway(cfg WalkwayConfig) *Walkway
```



<a name="Walkway.Walk"></a>
### func \(\*Walkway\) [Walk](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugofs/walk.go#L94>)




<a name="WalkwayConfig"></a>
## type [WalkwayConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugofs/walk.go#L46-L72>)



```go
type WalkwayConfig struct {
    // The filesystem to walk.
    Fs  afero.Fs

    // The root to start from in Fs.
    Root string

    // The logger to use.
    Logger     loggers.Logger
    PathParser *paths.PathParser

    // One or both of these may be pre-set.
    Info       FileMetaInfo    // The start info.
    DirEntries []FileMetaInfo  // The start info's dir entries.
    Ctx        context.Context // Optional starting context.

    IgnoreFile func(filename string) bool // Optional

    // Will be called in order.
    HookPre  WalkHook // Optional.
    WalkFn   WalkFunc
    HookPost WalkHook // Optional.

    // Some optional flags.
    FailOnNotExist bool // If set, return an error if a directory is not found.
    SortDirEntries bool // If set, sort the dir entries by Name before calling the WalkFn, default is ReaDir order.
}
```

<a name="BuildCfg"></a>
## type [BuildCfg](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/hugo_sites.go#L608-L631>)

BuildCfg holds build options used to, as an example, skip the render step.

```go
type BuildCfg struct {
    // Skip rendering. Useful for testing.
    SkipRender bool

    // Use this to indicate what changed (for rebuilds).
    WhatChanged *WhatChanged

    // This is a partial re-render of some selected pages.
    PartialReRender bool

    // Set in server mode when the last build failed for some reason.
    ErrRecovery bool

    // Recently visited or touched URLs. This is used for partial re-rendering.
    RecentlyTouched *types.EvictingQueue[string]

    // Can be set to build only with a sub set of the content source.
    ContentInclusionFilter *hglob.FilenameFilter

    // Set when the buildlock is already acquired (e.g. the archetype content builder).
    NoBuildLock bool
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="HugoSites"></a>
## type [HugoSites](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/hugo_sites.go#L61-L128>)

HugoSites represents the sites to build. Each site represents a language.

```go
type HugoSites struct {
    // The current sites slice.
    // When rendering, this slice will be shifted out.
    Sites []*Site

    Configs *allconfig.Configs

    *deps.Deps
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewHugoSites"></a>

<a name="IntegrationTestBuilder"></a>
## type [IntegrationTestBuilder](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/integrationtest_builder.go#L217-L240>)

IntegrationTestBuilder is a \(partial\) rewrite of sitesBuilder. The main problem with the "old" one was that it was that the test data was often a little hidden, so it became hard to look at a test and determine what it should do, especially coming back to the test after a year or so.

```go
type IntegrationTestBuilder struct {
    *qt.C

    H   *HugoSites

    Cfg IntegrationTestConfig

    GCCount int
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewIntegrationTestBuilder"></a>
### func [NewIntegrationTestBuilder](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/integrationtest_builder.go#L172>)

```go
func NewIntegrationTestBuilder(conf IntegrationTestConfig) *IntegrationTestBuilder
```

In most cases you should not use this function directly, but the Test or TestRunning function.

<a name="Test"></a>
### func [Test](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/integrationtest_builder.go#L144>)

```go
func Test(t testing.TB, files string, opts ...TestOpt) *IntegrationTestBuilder
```

Test is a convenience method to create a new IntegrationTestBuilder from some files and run a build.

<a name="TestE"></a>
### func [TestE](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/integrationtest_builder.go#L153>)

```go
func TestE(t testing.TB, files string, opts ...TestOpt) (*IntegrationTestBuilder, error)
```

TestE is the same as Test, but returns an error instead of failing the test.

<a name="TestRunning"></a>
### func [TestRunning](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/integrationtest_builder.go#L163>)

```go
func TestRunning(t testing.TB, files string, opts ...TestOpt) *IntegrationTestBuilder
```

TestRunning is a convenience method to create a new IntegrationTestBuilder from some files with Running set to true and run a build. Deprecated: Use Test with TestOptRunning instead.

<a name="IntegrationTestConfig"></a>
## type [IntegrationTestConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/integrationtest_builder.go#L1136-L1191>)



```go
type IntegrationTestConfig struct {
    T   testing.TB

    // The files to use on txtar format, see
    // https://pkg.go.dev/golang.org/x/exp/cmd/txtar
    // There are some contentions used in this test setup.
    // - §§§ can be used to wrap code fences.
    // - §§ can be used to wrap multiline strings.
    // - filenames prefixed with sourcefilename: will be read from the file system relative to the current dir.
    // - filenames with a .png or .jpg extension will be treated as binary and base64 decoded.
    TxtarString string

    // COnfig to use as the base. We will also read the config from the txtar.
    BaseCfg config.Provider

    // Environment variables passed to the config loader.
    Environ []string

    // Whether to simulate server mode.
    Running bool

    // Watch for changes.
    // This is (currently) always set to true when Running is set.
    // Note that the CLI for the server does allow for --watch=false, but that is not used in these test.
    Watching bool

    // Enable verbose logging.
    Verbose bool

    // The log level to use.
    LogLevel logg.Level

    // Whether to panic on warnings.
    PanicOnWarning bool

    // Whether it needs the real file system (e.g. for js.Build tests).
    NeedsOsFS bool

    // Whether to run GC after each build.
    RunGC bool

    // Do not remove the temp dir after the test.
    PrintAndKeepTempDir bool

    // Whether to run npm install before Build.
    NeedsNpmInstall bool

    // Whether to normalize the Unicode filenames to NFD on Darwin.
    NFDFormOnDarwin bool

    // The working dir to use. If not absolute, a temp dir will be created.
    WorkingDir string

    // The config to pass to Build.
    BuildCfg BuildCfg
}
```

<a name="ShortcodeWithPage"></a>
## type [ShortcodeWithPage](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/shortcode.go#L54-L78>)

ShortcodeWithPage is the "." context in a shortcode template.

```go
type ShortcodeWithPage struct {
    Params        any
    Inner         template.HTML
    Page          page.Page
    Parent        *ShortcodeWithPage
    Name          string
    IsNamedParams bool

    // Zero-based ordinal in relation to its parent. If the parent is the page itself,
    // this ordinal will represent the position of this shortcode in the page content.
    Ordinal int
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="ShortcodeWithPage.Get"></a>
### func \(\*ShortcodeWithPage\) [Get](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/shortcode.go#L145>)

```go
func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) Get(key any) any
```

Get is a convenience method to look up shortcode parameters by its key.

<a name="ShortcodeWithPage.InnerDeindent"></a>
### func \(\*ShortcodeWithPage\) [InnerDeindent](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/shortcode.go#L81>)

```go
func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) InnerDeindent() template.HTML
```

InnerDeindent returns the \(potentially de\-indented\) inner content of the shortcode.

<a name="ShortcodeWithPage.Position"></a>
### func \(\*ShortcodeWithPage\) [Position](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/shortcode.go#L103>)

```go
func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) Position() text.Position
```

Position returns this shortcode's detailed position. Note that this information may be expensive to calculate, so only use this in error situations.

<a name="ShortcodeWithPage.Ref"></a>

```go
func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) Ref(args map[string]any) (string, error)
```

Ref is a shortcut to the Ref method on Page. It passes itself as a context to get better error messages.

```go
func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) RelRef(args map[string]any) (string, error)
```

RelRef is a shortcut to the RelRef method on Page. It passes itself as a context to get better error messages.

```go
func (scp *ShortcodeWithPage) Scratch() *hstore.Scratch
```

Scratch returns a scratch\-pad scoped for this shortcode. This can be used as a temporary storage for variables, counters etc. Deprecated: Use Store instead. Note that from the templates this should be considered a "soft deprecation".

<a name="Site.GetInternalRelatedDocsHandler"></a>
### func \(\*Site\) [GetInternalRelatedDocsHandler](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/site.go#L984>)

<a name="Site.GetPage"></a>
### func \(\*Site\) [GetPage](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/hugolib/site.go#L1590>)

```go
func (s *Site) GetPage(ref ...string) (page.Page, error)
```

GetPage looks up a page of a given type for the given ref. In Hugo \<= 0.44 you had to add Page Kind \(section, home\) etc. as the first argument and then either a unix styled path \(with or without a leading slash\)\) or path elements separated. When we now remove the Kind from this API, we need to make the transition as painless as possible for existing sites. Most sites will use \{\{ .Site.GetPage "section" "my/section" \}\}, i.e. 2 arguments, so we test for that.


Home is a shortcut to the home page, equivalent to .Site.GetPage "home".

# identity

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/identity"
```

Package provides ways to identify values in Hugo. Used for dependency tracking etc.

Package provides ways to identify values in Hugo. Used for dependency tracking etc.

## Constants

<a name="Anonymous"></a>

```go
const (
    // Anonymous is an Identity that can be used when identity doesn't matter.
    Anonymous = StringIdentity("__anonymous")

    // GenghisKhan is an Identity everyone relates to.
    GenghisKhan = StringIdentity("__genghiskhan")

    StructuralChangeAdd    = StringIdentity("__structural_change_add")
    StructuralChangeRemove = StringIdentity("__structural_change_remove")
)
```

## Variables

<a name="NopManager"></a>

```go
var NopManager = new(nopManager)
```

<a name="CleanString"></a>
## func [CleanString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L58>)

```go
func CleanString(s string) string
```

CleanString cleans s to be suitable as an identifier.

<a name="PrintIdentityInfo"></a>
## func [PrintIdentityInfo](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L93>)

```go
func PrintIdentityInfo(v any)
```

PrintIdentityInfo is used for debugging/tests only.

<a name="WalkIdentitiesDeep"></a>
## func [WalkIdentitiesDeep](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L113>)

```go
func WalkIdentitiesDeep(v any, cb func(level int, id Identity) bool)
```

WalkIdentitiesDeep walks identities in v and applies cb to every identity found. Return true from cb to terminate. If deep is true, it will also walk nested Identities in any Manager found.

<a name="WalkIdentitiesShallow"></a>
## func [WalkIdentitiesShallow](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L121>)

```go
func WalkIdentitiesShallow(v any, cb func(level int, id Identity) bool)
```

WalkIdentitiesShallow will not walk into a Manager's Identities. See WalkIdentitiesDeep. cb is called for every Identity found and returns whether to terminate the walk.

```go
type DependencyManagerProvider interface {

}
```

Finder finds identities inside another.

```go
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewFinder"></a>
### func [NewFinder](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/finder.go#L25>)

```go
func NewFinder(cfg FinderConfig) *Finder
```

NewFinder creates a new Finder. This is a thread safe implementation with a cache.

<a name="Finder.Contains"></a>
### func \(\*Finder\) [Contains](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/finder.go#L75>)

```go
func (f *Finder) Contains(id, in Identity, maxDepth int) FinderResult
```

Contains returns whether in contains id.

<a name="FinderConfig"></a>
## type [FinderConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/finder.go#L309-L312>)

FinderConfig provides configuration for the Finder. Note that we by default will use a strategy where probable matches are good enough. The primary use case for this is to identity the change set for a given changed identity \(e.g. a template\), and we don't want to have any false negatives there, but some false positives are OK. Also, speed is important.

```go
type FinderConfig struct {
    // Match exact matches only.
    Exact bool
}
```

<a name="FinderResult"></a>
## type [FinderResult](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/finder.go#L65>)



```go
type FinderResult int
```

<a name="FinderNotFound"></a>

```go
const (
    FinderNotFound FinderResult = iota
    FinderFoundOneOfManyRepetition
    FinderFoundOneOfMany
    FinderFound
)
```

<a name="ForEeachIdentityByNameProvider"></a>
## type [ForEeachIdentityByNameProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L166-L170>)

ForEeachIdentityByNameProvider provides a way to look up identities by name.

```go
type ForEeachIdentityByNameProvider interface {
    // ForEeachIdentityByName calls cb for each Identity that relates to name.
    // If cb returns true, the iteration is terminated.
    ForEeachIdentityByName(name string, cb func(id Identity) bool)
}
```

<a name="ForEeachIdentityProvider"></a>
## type [ForEeachIdentityProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L151-L156>)

ForEeachIdentityProvider provides a way iterate over identities.

```go
type ForEeachIdentityProvider interface {
    // ForEeachIdentityProvider calls cb for each Identity.
    // If cb returns false, the iteration is terminated.
    // It returns false if the iteration was terminated early.
    ForEeachIdentity(cb func(id Identity) bool) bool
}
```

<a name="ForEeachIdentityProviderFunc"></a>
## type [ForEeachIdentityProviderFunc](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L159>)

ForEeachIdentityProviderFunc is a function that implements the ForEeachIdentityProvider interface.

```go
type ForEeachIdentityProviderFunc func(func(id Identity) bool) bool
```

<a name="ForEeachIdentityProviderFunc.ForEeachIdentity"></a>
### func \(ForEeachIdentityProviderFunc\) [ForEeachIdentity](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L161>)

```go
func (f ForEeachIdentityProviderFunc) ForEeachIdentity(cb func(id Identity) bool) bool
```

```go
type Identities map[Identity]bool
```

<a name="Identity"></a>
## type [Identity](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L227-L229>)

Identity represents a thing in Hugo \(a Page, a template etc.\) Any implementation must be comparable/hashable.

```go
type Identity interface {
    IdentifierBase() string
}
```

<a name="FirstIdentity"></a>
### func [FirstIdentity](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L83>)

```go
func FirstIdentity(v any) Identity
```

FirstIdentity returns the first Identity in v, Anonymous if none found

```go
func Unwrap(id Identity) Identity
```



<a name="IdentityGroupProvider"></a>
## type [IdentityGroupProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L233-L235>)

IdentityGroupProvider can be implemented by tightly connected types. Current use case is Resource transformation via Hugo Pipes.

```go
type IdentityGroupProvider interface {
    GetIdentityGroup() Identity
}
```

<a name="IdentityProvider"></a>
## type [IdentityProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L239-L241>)

IdentityProvider can be implemented by types that isn't itself and Identity, usually because they're not comparable/hashable.

```go
type IdentityProvider interface {
    GetIdentity() Identity
}
```

## type [IncrementByOne](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L249-L251>)

IncrementByOne implements Incrementer adding 1 every time Incr is called.

```go
type IncrementByOne struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Incrementer"></a>
## type [Incrementer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L259-L261>)

Incrementer increments and returns the value. Typically used for IDs.

```go
type Incrementer interface {
    Incr() int
}
```

<a name="Manager"></a>
## type [Manager](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L274-L281>)

Manager is an Identity that also manages identities, typically dependencies.

```go
type Manager interface {
    Identity
    AddIdentity(ids ...Identity)
    AddIdentityForEach(ids ...ForEeachIdentityProvider)
    GetIdentity() Identity
    Reset()
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

### func [GetDependencyManager](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L70>)

```go
func GetDependencyManager(v any) Manager
```

GetDependencyManager returns the DependencyManager from v or nil if none found.

```go
func NewManager(opts ...ManagerOption) Manager
```

NewIdentityManager creates a new Manager.

```go
}

```

<a name="Question"></a>
## type [Question](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/question.go#L51-L57>)

A Question is defined by its Identity and can be answered once.

```go
type Question[T any] struct {
    Identity
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewQuestion"></a>
### func [NewQuestion](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/question.go#L19>)

```go
func NewQuestion[T any](id Identity) *Question[T]
```

NewQuestion creates a new question with the given identity.

<a name="Question[T].Answer"></a>
### func \(\*Question\[T\]\) [Answer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/question.go#L29>)

```go
func (q *Question[T]) Answer(fn func() T)
```

Answer takes a func that knows the answer. Note that this is a one\-time operation, fn will not be invoked again it the question is already answered. Use Result to check if the question is answered.

<a name="Question[T].Result"></a>
### func \(\*Question\[T\]\) [Result](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/question.go#L43>)

```go
func (q *Question[T]) Result() (any, bool)
```

Result returns the fasit of the question \(if answered\), and a bool indicating if the question has been answered.

<a name="SignalRebuilder"></a>
## type [SignalRebuilder](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/identity/identity.go#L244-L246>)

SignalRebuilder is an optional interface for types that can signal a rebuild.

```go
type SignalRebuilder interface {
    SignalRebuild(ids ...Identity)
}
```

<a name="Collator.CompareStrings"></a>
### func \(\*Collator\) [CompareStrings](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/langs/language.go#L250>)

```go
func (c *Collator) CompareStrings(a, b string) int
```

CompareStrings compares a and b. It returns \-1 if a \< b, 1 if a \> b and 0 if a == b. Note that the Collator is not thread safe, so you may want to acquire a lock on it before calling this method.

<a name="Language"></a>
## type [Language](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/langs/language.go#L36-L62>)



```go
type Language struct {
    // The language code, e.g. "en" or "no".
    // This is the key used in the languages map in the configuration,
    // and currently only settable as the key in the language map in the config.
    Lang string

    // Fields from the language config.
    LanguageConfig
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="LanguageConfig"></a>
## type [LanguageConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/langs/config.go#L31-L60>)

LanguageConfig holds the configuration for a single language. This is what is read from the config file.

```go
type LanguageConfig struct {
    // Deprecated: Use Label instead.
    LanguageName string `json:"-"`

    // Deprecated: Use Locale instead.
    LanguageCode string `json:"-"`

    // The language title. When set, this will
    // override site.Title for this language.
    Title string

    // Deprecated: Use Direction instead.
    LanguageDirection string `json:"-"`

    // The language weight. When set to a non-zero value, this will
    // be the main sort criteria for the language.
    Weight int

    // Set to true to disable this language.
    Disabled bool

    // The language direction, e.g. "ltr" or "rtl".
    Direction string

    // The language name, e.g. "English".
    Label string

    // The locale, e.g. "en-US".
    Locale string
}
```

<a name="LanguageInternal"></a>
## type [LanguageInternal](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/langs/config.go#L62-L72>)



```go
type LanguageInternal struct {
    // Name is the name of the role, extracted from the key in the config.
    Name string

    // Whether this role is the default role.
    // This will be rendered in the root.
    // There is only be one default role.
    Default bool

    LanguageConfig
}
```

<a name="ForceRefresh"></a>

```go
func ForceRefresh()
```

ForceRefresh tells livereload to force a hard refresh.

```go

```

<a name="Initialize"></a>
## func [Initialize](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/livereload/livereload.go#L108>)

```go
func Initialize()
```

Initialize starts the Websocket Hub handling live reloads.

<a name="NavigateToPathForPort"></a>
## func [NavigateToPathForPort](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/livereload/livereload.go#L119>)

```go
func NavigateToPathForPort(path string, port int)
```

NavigateToPathForPort is similar to NavigateToPath but will also set window.location.port to the given port value.

<a name="RefreshPath"></a>
## func [RefreshPath](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/livereload/livereload.go#L126>)

```go
func RefreshPath(s string)
```

RefreshPath tells livereload to refresh only the given path. If that path points to a CSS stylesheet or an image, only the changes will be updated in the browser, not the entire page.

<a name="ServeJS"></a>
## func [ServeJS](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/livereload/livereload.go#L142>)

```go
func ServeJS(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request)
```

ServeJS serves the livereload.js who's reference is injected into the page.

<a name="MediaTypeConfig"></a>
## type [MediaTypeConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/media/config.go#L162-L167>)

Hold the configuration for a given media type.

```go
type MediaTypeConfig struct {
    // The file suffixes used for this media type.
    Suffixes []string
    // Delimiter used before suffix.
    Delimiter string
}
```

<a name="SuffixInfo"></a>
## type [SuffixInfo](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/media/mediaType.go#L61-L67>)

SuffixInfo holds information about a Media Type's suffix.

```go
type SuffixInfo struct {
    // Suffix is the suffix without the delimiter, e.g. "xml".
    Suffix string `json:"suffix"`

    // FullSuffix is the suffix with the delimiter, e.g. ".xml".
    FullSuffix string `json:"fullSuffix"`
}
```

<a name="Type"></a>
## type [Type](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/media/mediaType.go#L36-L58>)

MediaType \(also known as MIME type and content type\) is a two\-part identifier for file formats and format contents transmitted on the Internet. For Hugo's use case, we use the top\-level type name / subtype name \+ suffix. One example would be application/svg\+xml If suffix is not provided, the sub type will be used. \<docsmeta\>\{ "name": "MediaType" \}\</docsmeta\>

```go
type Type struct {
    // The full MIME type string, e.g. "application/rss+xml".
    Type string `json:"-"`

    // The top-level type name, e.g. "application".
    MainType string `json:"mainType"`
    // The subtype name, e.g. "rss".
    SubType string `json:"subType"`
    // The delimiter before the suffix, e.g. ".".
    Delimiter string `json:"delimiter"`

    // FirstSuffix holds the first suffix defined for this MediaType.
    FirstSuffix SuffixInfo `json:"-"`

    // E.g. "jpg,jpeg"
    // Stored as a string to make Type comparable.
    // For internal use only.
    SuffixesCSV string `json:"-"`
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="FromContent"></a>
### func [FromContent](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/media/mediaType.go#L72>)

```go
func FromContent(types Types, extensionHints []string, content []byte) Type
```

FromContent resolve the Type primarily using http.DetectContentType. If http.DetectContentType resolves to application/octet\-stream, a zero Type is returned. If http.DetectContentType resolves to text/plain or application/xml, we try to get more specific using types and ext.

<a name="FromString"></a>
### func [FromString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/media/mediaType.go#L137>)

```go
func FromString(t string) (Type, error)
```

FromString creates a new Type given a type string on the form MainType/SubType and an optional suffix, e.g. "text/html" or "text/html\+html".

```go
func FromStringAndExt(t string, ext ...string) (Type, error)
```

FromStringAndExt creates a Type from a MIME string and a given extensions

<a name="Types.GetByMainSubType"></a>
### func \(Types\) [GetByMainSubType](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/media/mediaType.go#L348>)

```go
func (t Types) GetByMainSubType(mainType, subType string) (tp Type, found bool)
```

GetByMainSubType gets a media type given a main and a sub type e.g. "text" and "plain". It will return false if no format could be found, or if the combination given is ambiguous. The lookup is case insensitive.



GetBySuffix gets a media type given as suffix, e.g. "html". It will return false if no format could be found, or if the suffix given is ambiguous. The lookup is case insensitive.

# metrics

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/metrics"
```

Package metrics provides simple metrics tracking features.

## Index

- [type Provider](<#Provider>)
  - [func NewProvider\(calculateHints bool\) Provider](<#NewProvider>)
- [type Store](<#Store>)
  - [func \(s \*Store\) MeasureSince\(key string, start time.Time\)](<#Store.MeasureSince>)
  - [func \(s \*Store\) Reset\(\)](<#Store.Reset>)
  - [func \(s \*Store\) TrackValue\(key string, value any, cached bool\)](<#Store.TrackValue>)
  - [func \(s \*Store\) WriteMetrics\(w io.Writer\)](<#Store.WriteMetrics>)


<a name="Provider"></a>
## type [Provider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/metrics/metrics.go#L34-L47>)

The Provider interface defines an interface for measuring metrics.

```go
type Provider interface {
    // MeasureSince adds a measurement for key to the metric store.
    // Used with defer and time.Now().
    MeasureSince(key string, start time.Time)

    // WriteMetrics will write a summary of the metrics to w.
    WriteMetrics(w io.Writer)

    // TrackValue tracks the value for diff calculations etc.
    TrackValue(key string, value any, cached bool)

    // Reset clears the metric store.
    Reset()
}
```

<a name="NewProvider"></a>
### func [NewProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/metrics/metrics.go#L81>)

```go
func NewProvider(calculateHints bool) Provider
```

NewProvider returns a new instance of a metric store.

<a name="Store"></a>
## type [Store](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/metrics/metrics.go#L70-L78>)

Store provides storage for a set of metrics.

```go
type Store struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Store.MeasureSince"></a>
### func \(\*Store\) [MeasureSince](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/metrics/metrics.go#L130>)

```go
func (s *Store) MeasureSince(key string, start time.Time)
```

MeasureSince adds a measurement for key to the metric store.

<a name="Store.Reset"></a>
### func \(\*Store\) [Reset](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/metrics/metrics.go#L91>)

```go
func (s *Store) Reset()
```

Reset clears the metrics store.

<a name="Store.TrackValue"></a>

# minifiers

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/minifiers"
```

Package minifiers contains minifiers mapped to MIME types. This package is used in both the resource transformation, i.e. resources.Minify, and in the publishing chain.

## Index

- [type Client](<#Client>)
  - [func New\(mediaTypes media.Types, outputFormats output.Formats, cfg config.AllProvider\) \(Client, error\)](<#New>)
  - [func \(m Client\) Minify\(mediatype media.Type, dst io.Writer, src io.Reader\) error](<#Client.Minify>)
  - [func \(m Client\) Transformer\(mediatype media.Type\) transform.Transformer](<#Client.Transformer>)
- [type MinifyConfig](<#MinifyConfig>)
  - [func DecodeConfig\(v any\) \(conf MinifyConfig, err error\)](<#DecodeConfig>)
- [type TdewolffConfig](<#TdewolffConfig>)


<a name="Client"></a>
## type [Client](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/minifiers/minifiers.go#L32-L37>)

Client wraps a minifier.

```go
type Client struct {
    // Whether output minification is enabled (HTML in /public)
    MinifyOutput bool
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/minifiers/minifiers.go#L77>)

```go
func New(mediaTypes media.Types, outputFormats output.Formats, cfg config.AllProvider) (Client, error)
```

New creates a new Client with the provided MIME types as the mapping foundation. The HTML minifier is also registered for additional HTML types \(AMP etc.\) in the provided list of output formats.

<a name="Client.Minify"></a>
### func \(Client\) [Minify](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/minifiers/minifiers.go#L56>)

```go
func (m Client) Minify(mediatype media.Type, dst io.Writer, src io.Reader) error
```

Minify tries to minify the src into dst given a MIME type.
