```go
func (m Client) Transformer(mediatype media.Type) transform.Transformer
```

Transformer returns a func that can be used in the transformer publishing chain. TODO\(bep\) minify config etc

```go
type MinifyConfig struct {
    // Whether to minify the published output (the HTML written to /public).
```

# modules

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/modules"
```

Package modules provides a client that can be used to manage Hugo Components, what's referred to as Hugo Modules. Hugo Modules is built on top of Go Modules, but also supports vendoring and components stored directly in the themes dir.


```go
func ApplyProjectConfigDefaults(logger logg.Logger, mod Module, cfgs ...config.AllProvider) error
```

<a name="Client"></a>
## type [Client](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/modules/client.go#L108-L131>)

Client contains most of the API provided by this package.

```go
type Client struct {

    // Set when Go modules are initialized in the current repo, that is:
    // a go.mod file exists.
    GoModulesFilename string
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewClient"></a>
### func [NewClient](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/modules/client.go#L77>)

```go
func NewClient(cfg ClientConfig) *Client
```

NewClient creates a new Client that can be used to manage the Hugo Components in a given workingDir. The Client will resolve the dependencies recursively, but needs the top level imports to start out.

```go
func (c *Client) Clean(pattern string) error
```

```go
func (h *Client) Collect() (ModulesConfig, error)
```

```go
func (c *Client) Get(args ...string) error
```

Get runs "go get" with the supplied arguments.

<a name="Client.Graph"></a>
### func \(\*Client\) [Graph](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/modules/client.go#L134>)

```go
func (c *Client) Graph(w io.Writer) error
```

Graph writes a module dependency graph to the given writer.

<a name="Client.Init"></a>
### func \(\*Client\) [Init](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/modules/client.go#L366>)

```go
func (c *Client) Init(path string) error
```

Init initializes this as a Go Module with the given path. If path is empty, Go will try to guess. If this succeeds, this project will be marked as Go Module.

<a name="Client.Tidy"></a>
### func \(\*Client\) [Tidy](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/modules/client.go#L160>)

```go
func (c *Client) Tidy() error
```

Tidy can be used to remove unused dependencies from go.mod and go.sum.

<a name="Client.Vendor"></a>
### func \(\*Client\) [Vendor](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/modules/client.go#L185>)

```go
func (c *Client) Vendor() error
```

Vendor writes all the module dependencies to a \_vendor folder.

Unlike Go, we support it for any level.

We, by default, use the /\_vendor folder first, if found. To disable, run with

```
hugo --ignoreVendorPaths=".*"
```

Given a module tree, Hugo will pick the first module for a given path, meaning that if the top\-level module is vendored, that will be the full set of dependencies.

<a name="Client.Verify"></a>
### func \(\*Client\) [Verify](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/modules/client.go#L382>)

```go
func (c *Client) Verify(clean bool) error
```

Verify checks that the dependencies of the current module, which are stored in a local downloaded source cache, have not been modified since being downloaded.

<a name="ClientConfig"></a>
## type [ClientConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/modules/client.go#L896-L928>)

ClientConfig configures the module Client.

```go
type ClientConfig struct {
    Fs     afero.Fs
    Logger loggers.Logger

    // If set, it will be run before we do any duplicate checks for modules
    // etc.
    HookBeforeFinalize func(m *ModulesConfig) error

    // Ignore any _vendor directory for module paths matching the given pattern.
    // This can be nil.
    IgnoreVendor glob.Glob

    // Ignore any module not found errors.
    IgnoreModuleDoesNotExist bool

    // Absolute path to the project dir.
    WorkingDir string

    // Absolute path to the project's themes dir.
    ThemesDir string

    // The publish dir.
    PublishDir string

    // Eg. "production"
    Environment string

    Exec *hexec.Exec

    CacheDir           string // Module cache
    ModuleQueriesCache ModuleQueriesCache
    ModuleConfig       Config
}
```

<a name="Config"></a>
## type [Config](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/modules/config.go#L267-L322>)

Config holds a module config.

```go
type Config struct {
    // File system mounts.
    Mounts []Mount

    // Module imports.
    Imports []Import

    // Meta info about this module (license information etc.).
    Params map[string]any

    // Will be validated against the running Hugo version.
    HugoVersion HugoVersion

    // Optional Glob pattern matching module paths to skip when vendoring, e.g. “github.com/**”
    NoVendor string

    // When enabled, we will pick the vendored module closest to the module
    // using it.
    // The default behavior is to pick the first.
    // Note that there can still be only one dependency of a given module path,
    // so once it is in use it cannot be redefined.
    VendorClosest bool

    // A comma separated (or a slice) list of module path to directory replacement mapping,
    // e.g. github.com/bep/my-theme -> ../..,github.com/bep/shortcodes -> /some/path.
    // This is mostly useful for temporary locally development of a module, and then it makes sense to set it as an
    // OS environment variable, e.g: env HUGO_MODULE_REPLACEMENTS="github.com/bep/my-theme -> ../..".
    // Any relative path is relate to themesDir, and absolute paths are allowed.
    Replacements []string

    // Defines the proxy server to use to download remote modules. Default is direct, which means “git clone” and similar.
    // Configures GOPROXY when running the Go command for module operations.
    Proxy string

    // Comma separated glob list matching paths that should not use the proxy configured above.
    // Configures GONOPROXY when running the Go command for module operations.
    NoProxy string

    // Comma separated glob list matching paths that should be treated as private.
    // Configures GOPRIVATE when running the Go command for module operations.
    Private string

    // Configures GOAUTH when running the Go command for module operations.
    // This is a semicolon-separated list of authentication commands for go-import and HTTPS module mirror interactions.
    // This is useful for private repositories.
    // See `go help goauth` for more information.
    Auth string

    // Defaults to "off".
    // Set to a work file, e.g. hugo.work, to enable Go "Workspace" mode.
    // Can be relative to the working directory or absolute.
    // Requires Go 1.18+.
    // Note that this can also be set via OS env, e.g. export HUGO_MODULE_WORKSPACE=/my/hugo.work.
    Workspace string
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

```go
}
```


IsValid reports whether this version is valid compared to the running Hugo binary.

```go
func (v HugoVersion) String() string
```



<a name="Import"></a>
## type [Import](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/modules/config.go#L384-L406>)



```go
type Import struct {
    // Module path
    Path string

    // The common case is to leave this empty and let Go Modules resolve the version.
    // Can be set to a version query, e.g. "v1.2.3", ">=v1.2.0", "latest", which will
    // make this a direct dependency.
    Version string

    // Ignore any config in config.toml (will still follow imports).
    IgnoreConfig bool
    // Do not follow any configured imports.
    IgnoreImports bool
    // Do not mount any folder in this import.
    NoMounts bool
    // Never vendor this import (only allowed in main project).
    NoVendor bool
    // Turn off this module.
    Disable bool
    // File mounts.
    Mounts []Mount
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Module"></a>
## type [Module](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/modules/module.go#L28-L85>)



```go
type Module interface {
    // Optional config read from the configFilename above.
    Cfg() config.Provider

    // The decoded module config and mounts.
    Config() Config

    // Optional configuration filenames (e.g. "/themes/mytheme/config.json").
    // This will be added to the special configuration watch list when in
    // server mode.
    ConfigFilenames() []string

    // Directory holding files for this module.
    Dir() string

    // Returns whether this is a Go Module.
    IsGoMod() bool

    // Any directory remappings.
    Mounts() []Mount

    // In the dependency tree, this is the first module that defines this module
    // as a dependency.
    Owner() Module

    // Returns the path to this module.
    // This will either be the module path, e.g. "github.com/gohugoio/myshortcodes",
    // or the path below your /theme folder, e.g. "mytheme".
    Path() string

    // For direct dependencies, this will be Path + "@" + VersionQuery.
    // For managed dependencies, this will be the same as Path.
    PathVersionQuery(escapeQuery bool) string

    // Replaced by this module.
    Replace() Module

    // Returns whether Dir points below the _vendor dir.
    Vendor() bool

    // The module version.
    Version() string

    // The version query requested in the import.
    VersionQuery() string

    // The expected cryptographic hash of the module.
    Sum() string

    // Time version was created.
    Time() time.Time

    // Whether this module's dir is a watch candidate.
    Watch() bool

    // Origin returns the module's origin info if available (VCS, URL, Hash, etc.).
    Origin() ModuleOrigin
}
```

<a name="ModuleOrigin"></a>
## type [ModuleOrigin](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/modules/module.go#L88-L93>)

ModuleOrigin contains origin info for a Go module.

```go
type ModuleOrigin struct {
    VCS  string // version control system, e.g. "git"
    URL  string // repository URL, e.g. "https://github.com/bep/hugo-testing-git-versions"
    Hash string // commit hash
    Ref  string // e.g. "refs/tags/v3.0.1"
}
```

<a name="Mount"></a>
## type [Mount](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/modules/config.go#L408-L436>)



```go
type Mount struct {
    // Relative path in source repo, e.g. "scss".
    Source string

    // Relative target path, e.g. "assets/bootstrap/scss".
    Target string

    // Any file in this mount will be associated with this language.
    // Deprecated, use Sites instead.
    Lang string `json:"-"`

    // Sites defines which sites this mount applies to.
    Sites sitesmatrix.Sites

    // A slice of Glob patterns (string or slice) to exclude or include in this mount.
    // To exclude, prefix with "! ".
    Files []string

    // Include only files matching the given Glob patterns (string or slice).
    // Deprecated, use Files instead.
    IncludeFiles any `json:"-"`

    // Exclude all files matching the given Glob patterns (string or slice).
    // Deprecated, use Files instead.
    ExcludeFiles any `json:"-"`

    // Disable watching in watch mode for this mount.
    DisableWatch bool
}
```

### func \(Menu\) [Reverse](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/navigation/menu.go#L256>)

```go
func (m Menu) Reverse() Menu
```

Reverse reverses the order of the menu entries.

<a name="Menu.Sort"></a>
### func \(Menu\) [Sort](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/navigation/menu.go#L222>)

```go
func (m Menu) Sort() Menu
```

Sort sorts the menu by weight, name and then by identifier.

<a name="MenuConfig"></a>
## type [MenuConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/navigation/menu.go#L150-L162>)

MenuConfig holds the configuration for a menu.

```go
type MenuConfig struct {
    Identifier string
    Parent     string
    Name       string
    Pre        template.HTML
    Post       template.HTML
    URL        string
    PageRef    string
    Weight     int
    Title      string
    // User defined params.
    Params hmaps.Params
}
```

<a name="MenuEntry"></a>
## type [MenuEntry](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/navigation/menu.go#L35-L50>)

MenuEntry represents a menu item defined in either Page front matter or in the project config.

```go
type MenuEntry struct {
    // The menu entry configuration.
    MenuConfig

    // The menu containing this menu entry.
    Menu string

    // The URL value from front matter / config.
    ConfiguredURL string

    // The Page connected to this menu entry.
    Page Page

    // Child entries.
    Children Menu
}
```


HasChildren returns whether this menu item has any children.

```go

```

KeyName returns the key used to identify this menu entry.

## Variables

<a name="AMPFormat"></a>Built\-in output formats.

```go
var (
    AMPFormat = Format{
        Name:          "amp",
        MediaType:     media.Builtin.HTMLType,
        BaseName:      "index",
        Path:          "amp",
        Rel:           "amphtml",
        IsHTML:        true,
        Permalinkable: true,
    }

    CalendarFormat = Format{
        Name:        "calendar",
        MediaType:   media.Builtin.CalendarType,
        IsPlainText: true,
        Protocol:    "webcal://",
        BaseName:    "index",
        Rel:         "alternate",
    }

    CSSFormat = Format{
        Name:           "css",
        MediaType:      media.Builtin.CSSType,
        BaseName:       "styles",
        IsPlainText:    true,
        Rel:            "stylesheet",
        NotAlternative: true,
    }
    CSVFormat = Format{
        Name:        "csv",
        MediaType:   media.Builtin.CSVType,
        BaseName:    "index",
        IsPlainText: true,
        Rel:         "alternate",
    }

    HTMLFormat = Format{
        Name:          "html",
        MediaType:     media.Builtin.HTMLType,
        BaseName:      "index",
        Rel:           "canonical",
        IsHTML:        true,
        Permalinkable: true,

        Weight: 10,
    }

    // Alias is the output format used for alias redirects.
    AliasHTMLFormat = Format{
        Name:          "alias",
        MediaType:     media.Builtin.HTMLType,
        IsHTML:        true,
        Ugly:          true,
        Permalinkable: false,
    }

    MarkdownFormat = Format{
        Name:        "markdown",
        MediaType:   media.Builtin.MarkdownType,
        BaseName:    "index",
        Rel:         "alternate",
        IsPlainText: true,
    }

    JSONFormat = Format{
        Name:        "json",
        MediaType:   media.Builtin.JSONType,
        BaseName:    "index",
        IsPlainText: true,
        Rel:         "alternate",
    }

    WebAppManifestFormat = Format{
        Name:           "webappmanifest",
        MediaType:      media.Builtin.WebAppManifestType,
        BaseName:       "manifest",
        IsPlainText:    true,
        NotAlternative: true,
        Rel:            "manifest",
    }

    RobotsTxtFormat = Format{
        Name:        "robots",
        MediaType:   media.Builtin.TextType,
        BaseName:    "robots",
        IsPlainText: true,
        Root:        true,
        Rel:         "alternate",
    }

    RSSFormat = Format{
        Name:      "rss",
        MediaType: media.Builtin.RSSType,
        BaseName:  "index",
        NoUgly:    true,
        Rel:       "alternate",
    }

    SitemapFormat = Format{
        Name:      "sitemap",
        MediaType: media.Builtin.XMLType,
        BaseName:  "sitemap",
        Ugly:      true,
        Rel:       "sitemap",
    }

    SitemapIndexFormat = Format{
        Name:      "sitemapindex",
        MediaType: media.Builtin.XMLType,
        BaseName:  "sitemap",
        Ugly:      true,
        Root:      true,
        Rel:       "sitemap",
    }

    GotmplFormat = Format{
        Name:           "gotmpl",
        MediaType:      media.Builtin.GotmplType,
        IsPlainText:    true,
        NotAlternative: true,
    }

    // I'm not sure having a 404 format is a good idea,
    // for one, we would want to have multiple formats for this.
    HTTPStatus404HTMLFormat = Format{
        Name:           "404",
        MediaType:      media.Builtin.HTMLType,
        NotAlternative: true,
        Ugly:           true,
        IsHTML:         true,
        Permalinkable:  true,
    }
)
```

<a name="DefaultFormats"></a>DefaultFormats contains the default output formats supported by Hugo.

```go
var DefaultFormats = Formats{
    AMPFormat,
    CalendarFormat,
    CSSFormat,
    CSVFormat,
    HTMLFormat,
    GotmplFormat,
    HTTPStatus404HTMLFormat,
    AliasHTMLFormat,
    JSONFormat,
    MarkdownFormat,
    WebAppManifestFormat,
    RobotsTxtFormat,
    RSSFormat,
    SitemapFormat,
    SitemapIndexFormat,
}
```

<a name="DecodeConfig"></a>
## func [DecodeConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/output/config.go#L40>)

```go
func DecodeConfig(mediaTypes media.Types, in any) (*config.ConfigNamespace[map[string]OutputFormatConfig, Formats], error)
```



<a name="Format"></a>
## type [Format](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/output/outputFormat.go#L28-L83>)

Format represents an output representation, usually to a file on disk. \<docsmeta\>\{ "name": "OutputFormat" \}\</docsmeta\>

```go
type Format struct {
    // The Name is used as an identifier. Internal output formats (i.e. html and rss)
    // can be overridden by providing a new definition for those types.
    // <docsmeta>{ "identifiers": ["html", "rss"] }</docsmeta>
    Name string `json:"-"`

    MediaType media.Type `json:"-"`

    // Must be set to a value when there are two or more conflicting mediatype for the same resource.
    Path string `json:"path"`

    // The base output file name used when not using "ugly URLs", defaults to "index".
    BaseName string `json:"baseName"`

    // The value to use for rel links.
    Rel string `json:"rel"`

    // The protocol to use, i.e. "webcal://". Defaults to the protocol of the baseURL.
    Protocol string `json:"protocol"`

    // IsPlainText decides whether to use text/template or html/template
    // as template parser.
    IsPlainText bool `json:"isPlainText"`

    // IsHTML returns whether this format is int the HTML family. This includes
    // HTML, AMP etc. This is used to decide when to create alias redirects etc.
    IsHTML bool `json:"isHTML"`

    // Enable to ignore the global uglyURLs setting.
    NoUgly bool `json:"noUgly"`

    // Enable to override the global uglyURLs setting.
    Ugly bool `json:"ugly"`

    // Enable if it doesn't make sense to include this format in an alternative
    // format listing, CSS being one good example.
    // Note that we use the term "alternative" and not "alternate" here, as it
    // does not necessarily replace the other format, it is an alternative representation.
    NotAlternative bool `json:"notAlternative"`

    // Eneable if this is a resource which path always starts at the root,
    // e.g. /robots.txt.
    Root bool `json:"root"`

    // Setting this will make this output format control the value of
    // .Permalink and .RelPermalink for a rendered Page.
    // If not set, these values will point to the main (first) output format
    // configured. That is probably the behavior you want in most situations,
    // as you probably don't want to link back to the RSS version of a page, as an
    // example. AMP would, however, be a good example of an output format where this
    // behavior is wanted.
    Permalinkable bool `json:"permalinkable"`

    // Setting this to a non-zero value will be used as the first sort criteria.
    Weight int `json:"weight"`
}
```

<a name="Format.BaseFilename"></a>
### func \(Format\) [BaseFilename](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/output/outputFormat.go#L314>)

<a name="Formats.GetBySuffix"></a>
### func \(Formats\) [GetBySuffix](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/output/outputFormat.go#L269>)

```go
func (formats Formats) GetBySuffix(suffix string) (f Format, found bool)
```

GetBySuffix gets a output format given as suffix, e.g. "html". It will return false if no format could be found, or if the suffix given is ambiguous. The lookup is case insensitive.

<a name="Descriptor"></a>
## type [Descriptor](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/publisher/publisher.go#L42-L69>)

Descriptor describes the needed publishing chain for an item.

```go
type Descriptor struct {
    // The content to publish.
    Src io.Reader

    // The OutputFormat of the this content.
    OutputFormat output.Format

    // Where to publish this content. This is a filesystem-relative path.
    TargetPath string

    // Counter for the end build summary.
    StatCounter *uint64

    // Configuration that trigger pre-processing.
    // LiveReload script will be injected if this is != nil
    LiveReloadBaseURL *url.URL

    // Enable to inject the Hugo generated tag in the header. Is currently only
    // injected on the home page for HTML type of output formats.
    AddHugoGeneratorTag bool

    // If set, will replace all relative URLs with this one.
    AbsURLPath string

    // Enable to minify the output using the OutputFormat defined above to
    // pick the correct minifier configuration.
    Minify bool
}
```

<a name="DestinationPublisher"></a>
## type [DestinationPublisher](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/publisher/publisher.go#L73-L77>)

DestinationPublisher is the default and currently only publisher in Hugo. This publisher prepares and publishes an item to the defined destination, e.g. /public.

```go
type DestinationPublisher struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

## Constants

<a name="TypeBasic"></a>

```go
const (
    TypeBasic     = "basic"
    TypeFragments = "fragments"
)
```

## Variables

<a name="DefaultConfig"></a>

```go
var (

    // DefaultConfig is the default related config.
    DefaultConfig = Config{
        Threshold: 80,
        Indices: IndicesConfig{
            IndexConfig{Name: "keywords", Weight: 100, Type: TypeBasic},
            IndexConfig{Name: "date", Weight: 10, Type: TypeBasic},
        },
    }
)
```

<a name="Config"></a>
## type [Config](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L64-L76>)

Config is the top level configuration element used to configure how to retrieve related content in Hugo.

```go
type Config struct {
    // Only include matches >= threshold, a normalized rank between 0 and 100.
    Threshold int

    // To get stable "See also" sections we, by default, exclude newer related pages.
    IncludeNewer bool

    // Will lower case all string values and queries to the indices.
    // May get better results, but at a slight performance cost.
    ToLower bool

    Indices IndicesConfig
}
```

<a name="DecodeConfig"></a>
### func [DecodeConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L559>)

```go
func DecodeConfig(m hmaps.Params) (Config, error)
```

DecodeConfig decodes a slice of map into Config.

<a name="Config.Add"></a>
### func \(\*Config\) [Add](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L79>)

```go
func (c *Config) Add(index IndexConfig)
```

Add adds a given index.

<a name="Config.HasType"></a>
### func \(\*Config\) [HasType](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L86>)

```go
func (c *Config) HasType(s string) bool
```



<a name="Document"></a>
## type [Document](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L131-L141>)

Document is the interface an indexable document in Hugo must fulfill.

```go
type Document interface {
    // RelatedKeywords returns a list of keywords for the given index config.
    RelatedKeywords(cfg IndexConfig) ([]Keyword, error)

    // When this document was or will be published.
    PublishDate() time.Time

    // Name is used as an tiebreaker if both Weight and PublishDate are
    // the same.
    Name() string
}
```

<a name="FragmentKeyword"></a>
## type [FragmentKeyword](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L610>)

FragmentKeyword represents a document fragment.

```go
type FragmentKeyword string
```

<a name="FragmentKeyword.String"></a>
### func \(FragmentKeyword\) [String](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L612>)

```go
func (f FragmentKeyword) String() string
```



<a name="FragmentProvider"></a>
## type [FragmentProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L144-L149>)

FragmentProvider is an optional interface that can be implemented by a Document.

```go
type FragmentProvider interface {
    Fragments(context.Context) *tableofcontents.Fragments

    // For internal use.
    ApplyFilterToHeadings(context.Context, func(*tableofcontents.Heading) bool) Document
}
```

<a name="IndexConfig"></a>
## type [IndexConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L99-L128>)

IndexConfig configures an index.

```go
type IndexConfig struct {
    // The index name. This directly maps to a field or Param name.
    Name string

    // The index type.
    Type string

    // Enable to apply a type specific filter to the results.
    // This is currently only used for the "fragments" type.
    ApplyFilter bool

    // Contextual pattern used to convert the Param value into a string.
    // Currently only used for dates. Can be used to, say, bump posts in the same
    // time frame when searching for related documents.
    // For dates it follows Go's time.Format patterns, i.e.
    // "2006" for YYYY and "200601" for YYYYMM.
    Pattern string

    // This field's weight when doing multi-index searches. Higher is "better".
    Weight int

    // A percentage (0-100) used to remove common keywords from the index.
    // As an example, setting this to 50 will remove all keywords that are
    // used in more than 50% of the documents in the index.
    CardinalityThreshold int

    // Will lower case all string values in and queries tothis index.
    // May get better accurate results, but at a slight performance cost.
    ToLower bool
}
```

```go
}
```

<a name="InvertedIndex.Add"></a>


Add documents to the inverted index. The value must support == and \!=.

<a name="InvertedIndex.Finalize"></a>
### func \(\*InvertedIndex\) [Finalize](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L239>)

```go
func (idx *InvertedIndex) Finalize(ctx context.Context) error
```



<a name="InvertedIndex.Search"></a>
### func \(\*InvertedIndex\) [Search](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L346>)

```go
func (idx *InvertedIndex) Search(ctx context.Context, opts SearchOpts) ([]Document, error)
```

Search finds the documents matching any of the keywords in the given indices against query options in opts. The resulting document set will be sorted according to number of matches and the index weights, and any matches with a rank below the configured threshold \(normalize to 0..100\) will be removed. If an index name is provided, only that index will be queried.

<a name="Keyword"></a>
## type [Keyword](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L617-L619>)

Keyword is the interface a keyword in the search index must implement.

```go
type Keyword interface {
    String() string
}
```

<a name="SearchOpts"></a>
## type [SearchOpts](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L324-L338>)

SearchOpts holds the options for a related search.

```go
type SearchOpts struct {
    // The Document to search for related content for.
    Document Document

    // The keywords to search for.
    NamedSlices []types.KeyValues

    // The indices to search in.
    Indices []string

    // Fragments holds a a list of special keywords that is used
    // for indices configured as type "fragments".
    // This will match the fragment identifiers of the documents.
    Fragments []string
}
```

## type [StringKeyword](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/related/inverted_index.go#L603>)

StringKeyword is a string search keyword.

```go
type StringKeyword string
```

## Index

<a name="ReleaseHandler"></a>
## type [ReleaseHandler](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/releaser/releaser.go#L76-L89>)

ReleaseHandler provides functionality to release a new version of Hugo. Test this locally without doing an actual release: go run \-tags release main.go release \-\-skip\-publish \-\-try \-r 0.90.0 Or a variation of the above \-\- the skip\-publish flag makes sure that any changes are performed to the local Git only.

```go
type ReleaseHandler struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

```go

```

<a name="ResourceSourceDescriptor"></a>
## type [ResourceSourceDescriptor](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource.go#L62-L108>)



```go
type ResourceSourceDescriptor struct {
    // The source content.
    OpenReadSeekCloser hugio.OpenReadSeekCloser

    // The canonical source path.
    Path *paths.Path

    // The normalized name of the resource.
    NameNormalized string

    // The name of the resource as it was read from the source.
    NameOriginal string

    // The title of the resource.
    Title string

    // Any base paths prepended to the target path. This will also typically be the
    // language code, but setting it here means that it should not have any effect on
    // the permalink.
    // This may be several values. In multihost mode we may publish the same resources to
    // multiple targets.
    TargetBasePaths []string

    TargetPath           string
    BasePathRelPermalink string
    BasePathTargetPath   string
    SourceFilenameOrPath string // Used for error logging.

    // The Data to associate with this resource.
    Data map[string]any

    // The Params to associate with this resource.
    Params hmaps.Params

    // Delay publishing until either Permalink or RelPermalink is called. Maybe never.
    LazyPublish bool

    // Set when its known up front, else it's resolved from the target filename.
    MediaType media.Type

    // Used to track dependencies (e.g. imports). May be nil if that's of no concern.
    DependencyManager identity.Manager

    // A shared identity for this resource and all its clones.
    // If this is not set, an Identity is created.
    GroupIdentity identity.Identity
}
```

<a name="ResourceTransformation"></a>
## type [ResourceTransformation](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/transform.go#L97-L100>)

ResourceTransformation is the interface that a resource transformation step needs to implement.

```go
type ResourceTransformation interface {
    Key() internal.ResourceTransformationKey
    Transform(ctx *ResourceTransformationCtx) error
}
```

<a name="NewFeatureNotAvailableTransformer"></a>
### func [NewFeatureNotAvailableTransformer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource.go#L205>)

```go
func NewFeatureNotAvailableTransformer(key string, elements ...any) ResourceTransformation
```



<a name="ResourceTransformationCtx"></a>
## type [ResourceTransformationCtx](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/transform.go#L102-L139>)



```go
type ResourceTransformationCtx struct {
    // The context that started the transformation.
    Ctx context.Context

    // The dependency manager to use for dependency tracking.
    DependencyManager identity.Manager

    // The content to transform.
    From io.Reader

    // The target of content transformation.
    // The current implementation requires that r is written to w
    // even if no transformation is performed.
    To  io.Writer

    // This is the relative path to the original source. Unix styled slashes.
    SourcePath string

    // This is the relative target path to the resource. Unix styled slashes.
    InPath string

    // The relative target path to the transformed resource. Unix styled slashes.
    OutPath string

    // The input media type
    InMediaType media.Type

    // The media type of the transformed resource.
    OutMediaType media.Type

    // Data data can be set on the transformed Resource. Not that this need
    // to be simple types, as it needs to be serialized to JSON and back.
    Data map[string]any

    // This is used to publish additional artifacts, e.g. source hhmaps.
    // We may improve this.
    OpenResourcePublisher func(relTargetPath string) (io.WriteCloser, error)
}
```

<a name="ResourceTransformationCtx.AddOutPathIdentifier"></a>
### func \(\*ResourceTransformationCtx\) [AddOutPathIdentifier](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/transform.go#L143>)

```go
func (ctx *ResourceTransformationCtx) AddOutPathIdentifier(identifier string)
```

AddOutPathIdentifier transforming InPath to OutPath adding an identifier, eg '.min' before any extension.

<a name="ResourceTransformationCtx.PublishSourceMap"></a>
### func \(\*ResourceTransformationCtx\) [PublishSourceMap](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/transform.go#L149>)

```go
func (ctx *ResourceTransformationCtx) PublishSourceMap(content []byte) error
```

PublishSourceMap writes the content to the target folder of the main resource with the ".map" extension added.

The parts of Spec that's common for all sites.

<a name="StaleValue"></a>
## type [StaleValue](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource.go#L315-L322>)



```go
type StaleValue[V any] struct {
    // The value.
    Value V

    // StaleVersionFunc reports the current version of the value.
    // This always starts out at 0 and get incremented on staleness.
    StaleVersionFunc func() uint32
}
```

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/tpl"
```

Package tpl contains template functions and related types.

## Index

## Constants

<a name="HugoDeferredTemplatePrefix"></a>

```go
const (
    // HugoDeferredTemplatePrefix is the prefix for placeholders for deferred templates.
    HugoDeferredTemplatePrefix = "__hdeferred/"
    // HugoDeferredTemplateSuffix is the suffix for placeholders for deferred templates.
    HugoDeferredTemplateSuffix = "__d="
)
```

## Variables

<a name="Context"></a>Context manages values passed in the context to templates.

```go
var Context = struct {
    DependencyManagerScopedProvider    contexthelpers.ContextDispatcher[identity.DependencyManagerScopedProvider]
    GetDependencyManagerInCurrentScope func(context.Context) identity.Manager
    DependencyScope                    contexthelpers.ContextDispatcher[int]
    Page                               contexthelpers.ContextDispatcher[page]
    IsInGoldmark                       contexthelpers.ContextDispatcher[bool]
    CurrentTemplate                    contexthelpers.ContextDispatcher[*CurrentTemplateInfo]
    PartialDecoratorIDStack            contexthelpers.ContextDispatcher[*collections.Stack[*StringBool]]
}{
    DependencyManagerScopedProvider: contexthelpers.NewContextDispatcher[identity.DependencyManagerScopedProvider](contextKeyDependencyManagerScopedProvider),
    DependencyScope:                 contexthelpers.NewContextDispatcher[int](contextKeyDependencyScope),
    Page:                            contexthelpers.NewContextDispatcher[page](contextKeyPage),
    IsInGoldmark:                    contexthelpers.NewContextDispatcher[bool](contextKeyIsInGoldmark),
    CurrentTemplate:                 contexthelpers.NewContextDispatcher[*CurrentTemplateInfo](cntextKeyCurrentTemplateInfo),
    PartialDecoratorIDStack:         contexthelpers.NewContextDispatcher[*collections.Stack[*StringBool]](contextKeyPartialDecoratorIDStack),
}
```

# transform

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/transform"
```

## Index

- [type BytesReader](<#BytesReader>)
- [type Chain](<#Chain>)
  - [func New\(trs ...Transformer\) Chain](<#New>)
  - [func NewEmpty\(\) Chain](<#NewEmpty>)
  - [func \(c \*Chain\) Apply\(to io.Writer, from io.Reader\) error](<#Chain.Apply>)
- [type FromTo](<#FromTo>)

<a name="BytesReader"></a>
## type [BytesReader](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/transform/chain.go#L31-L38>)

BytesReader wraps the Bytes method, usually implemented by bytes.Buffer, and an io.Reader.

```go
type BytesReader interface {
    // The slice given by Bytes is valid for use only until the next buffer modification.
    // That is, if you want to use this value outside of the current transformer step,
    // you need to take a copy.
    Bytes() []byte

    io.Reader
}
```

<a name="Chain"></a>
## type [Chain](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/transform/chain.go#L48>)

Chain is an ordered processing chain. The next transform operation will receive the output from the previous.

```go
type Chain []Transformer
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/transform/chain.go#L51>)

```go
func New(trs ...Transformer) Chain
```

New creates a content transformer chain given the provided transform funcs.

<a name="NewEmpty"></a>
### func [NewEmpty](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/transform/chain.go#L56>)

```go
func NewEmpty() Chain
```

NewEmpty creates a new slice of transformers with a capacity of 20.

<a name="Chain.Apply"></a>
### func \(\*Chain\) [Apply](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/transform/chain.go#L77>)

```go
func (c *Chain) Apply(to io.Writer, from io.Reader) error
```

Apply passes the given from io.Reader through the transformation chain. The result is written to to.

<a name="FromTo"></a>
## type [FromTo](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/transform/chain.go#L41-L44>)

FromTo is sent to each transformation step in the chain.

```go
type FromTo interface {
    From() BytesReader
    To() io.Writer
}
```

<a name="Transformer"></a>
## type [Transformer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/transform/chain.go#L27>)

Transformer is the func that needs to be implemented by a transformation step.

```go
type Transformer func(ft FromTo) error
```

<a name="Batcher"></a>
## type [Batcher](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/watcher/batcher.go#L24-L30>)

Batcher batches file watch events in a given interval.

```go
type Batcher struct {
    filenotify.FileWatcher

    Events chan []fsnotify.Event // Events are returned on this channel
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/watcher/batcher.go#L35>)

```go
func New(intervalBatcher, intervalPoll time.Duration, poll bool) (*Batcher, error)
```

New creates and starts a Batcher with the given time interval. It will fall back to a poll based watcher if native isn't supported. To always use polling, set poll to true.

```go
func (b *Batcher) Close()
```

Close stops the watching of the files.

<a name="CleanKey"></a>
## func [CleanKey](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/dynacache/dynacache.go#L645>)

```go
func CleanKey(s string) string
```

CleanKey turns s into a format suitable for a cache key for this package. The key will be a Unix\-styled path with a leading slash but no trailing slash.

## type [Cache](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/dynacache/dynacache.go#L126-L140>)

A dynamic partitioned cache.

```go
type Cache struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/dynacache/dynacache.go#L47>)

```go
func New(opts Options) *Cache
```

New creates a new cache.

<a name="Cache.ClearMatching"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [ClearMatching](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/dynacache/dynacache.go#L153>)

```go
func (c *Cache) ClearMatching(predicatePartition func(k string, p PartitionManager) bool, predicateValue func(k, v any) bool)
```

ClearMatching clears all partition for which the predicate returns true.

<a name="Cache.ClearOnRebuild"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [ClearOnRebuild](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/dynacache/dynacache.go#L180>)

```go
func (c *Cache) ClearOnRebuild(predicate func(k, v any) bool, changeset ...identity.Identity)
```

ClearOnRebuild prepares the cache for a new rebuild taking the given changeset into account. predicate is optional and will clear any entry for which it returns true.

<a name="Cache.DrainEvictedIdentities"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [DrainEvictedIdentities](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/dynacache/dynacache.go#L143>)

```go
func (c *Cache) DrainEvictedIdentities() []KeyIdentity
```

DrainEvictedIdentities drains the evicted identities from the cache.

<a name="Cache.DrainEvictedIdentitiesMatching"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [DrainEvictedIdentitiesMatching](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/dynacache/dynacache.go#L148>)

```go
func (c *Cache) DrainEvictedIdentitiesMatching(predicate func(KeyIdentity) bool) []KeyIdentity
```

DrainEvictedIdentitiesMatching drains the evicted identities from the cache that match the given predicate.

# filecache

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/cache/filecache"
```

Package filecache provides a file based cache for Hugo.

## Variables

<a name="ErrFatal"></a>ErrFatal can be used to signal an unrecoverable error.

```go
var ErrFatal = errors.New("fatal filecache error")
```

<a name="Cache"></a>
## type [Cache](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L46-L56>)

Cache caches a set of files in a directory. This is usually a file on disk, but since this is backed by an Afero file system, it can be anything.

```go
type Cache struct {
    Fs afero.Fs
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewCache"></a>
### func [NewCache](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L88>)

```go
func NewCache(fs afero.Fs, cfg FileCacheConfig) *Cache
```

NewCache creates a new file cache with the given filesystem and max age.

<a name="Cache.AbsFilenameFromID"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [AbsFilenameFromID](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L242>)

```go
func (c *Cache) AbsFilenameFromID(id string) string
```

AbsFilenameFromID returns the filename for the given id in the cache. This will be an absolute path.

<a name="Cache.AsHTTPCache"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [AsHTTPCache](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L550>)

```go
func (c *Cache) AsHTTPCache() httpcache.Cache
```

AsHTTPCache returns an httpcache.Cache implementation for this file cache. Note that none of the methods are protected by named locks, so you need to make sure to do that in your own code.

<a name="Cache.Get"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [Get](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L375>)

```go
func (c *Cache) Get(id string) (ItemInfo, io.ReadCloser, error)
```

Get gets the file with the given id from the cache, nil if none found.

<a name="Cache.GetBytes"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [GetBytes](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L348>)

```go
func (c *Cache) GetBytes(id string) ([]byte, error)
```

GetBytes gets the file content with the given id from the cache, nil if none found.

<a name="Cache.GetItemBytes"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [GetItemBytes](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L354>)

```go
func (c *Cache) GetItemBytes(id string) (ItemInfo, []byte, error)
```

GetItemBytes gets the ItemInfo and file content with the given id from the cache, nil if none found.

<a name="Cache.GetOrCreate"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [GetOrCreate](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L204>)

```go
func (c *Cache) GetOrCreate(id string, create func() (io.ReadCloser, error)) (ItemInfo, io.ReadCloser, error)
```

GetOrCreate tries to get the file with the given id from cache. If not found or expired, create will be invoked and the result cached. This method is protected by a named lock using the given id as identifier.

<a name="Cache.GetOrCreateBytes"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [GetOrCreateBytes](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L291>)

```go
func (c *Cache) GetOrCreateBytes(id string, create func() ([]byte, error)) (ItemInfo, []byte, error)
```

GetOrCreateBytes is the same as GetOrCreate, but produces a byte slice.

<a name="Cache.GetOrCreateInfo"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [GetOrCreateInfo](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L249>)

```go
func (c *Cache) GetOrCreateInfo(id string, create func(id string) error) (ItemInfo, error)
```

GetOrCreateInfo tries to get the item info with the given id from cache. If not found or expired, create will be invoked with the id. The create function is expected to create the cache item with the given id. The returned ItemInfo will have the id as Name. This method is protected by a named lock using the given id as identifier.

<a name="Cache.GetString"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [GetString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L486>)

```go
func (c *Cache) GetString(id string) string
```

For testing

<a name="Cache.NamedLock"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [NamedLock](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L193>)

```go
func (c *Cache) NamedLock(id string) func()
```

NamedLock locks the given id. The lock is released when the returned function is called.

<a name="Cache.Prune"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [Prune](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache_pruner.go#L52>)

```go
func (c *Cache) Prune(force bool) (int, error)
```

Prune removes expired and unused items from this cache. If force is set, everything will be removed not considering expiry time.

<a name="Cache.ReadOrCreate"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [ReadOrCreate](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L155-L158>)

```go
func (c *Cache) ReadOrCreate(id string, read func(info ItemInfo, r io.ReadSeeker) error, create func(info ItemInfo, w io.WriteCloser) error) (info ItemInfo, err error)
```

ReadOrCreate tries to lookup the file in cache. If found, it is passed to read and then closed. If not found a new file is created and passed to create, which should close it when done.

<a name="Cache.SetBytes"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [SetBytes](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L330>)

```go
func (c *Cache) SetBytes(id string, data []byte) error
```

SetBytes sets the file content with the given id in the cache.

<a name="Cache.WriteCloser"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\) [WriteCloser](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L129>)

```go
func (c *Cache) WriteCloser(id string) (ItemInfo, io.WriteCloser, error)
```

WriteCloser returns a transactional writer into the cache. It's important that it's closed when done.


### func \(Caches\) [MiscCache](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache_config.go#L199>)

```go
func (f Caches) MiscCache() *Cache
```

MiscCache gets the file cache for miscellaneous stuff.

<a name="Caches.ModuleGitInfoCache"></a>
### func \(Caches\) [ModuleGitInfoCache](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache_config.go#L185>)

```go
func (f Caches) ModuleGitInfoCache() *Cache
```

ModuleGitInfoCache gets the file cache for Hugo Module git info.

<a name="Caches.ModuleQueriesCache"></a>
### func \(Caches\) [ModuleQueriesCache](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache_config.go#L176>)

```go
func (f Caches) ModuleQueriesCache() *Cache
```

ModuleQueriesCache gets the file cache for Hugo Module version queries. Returns nil if not found.

<a name="Caches.ModulesCache"></a>
### func \(Caches\) [ModulesCache](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache_config.go#L170>)

```go
func (f Caches) ModulesCache() *Cache
```

ModulesCache gets the file cache for Hugo Modules.

<a name="Caches.Prune"></a>
### func \(Caches\) [Prune](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache_pruner.go#L31>)

```go
func (c Caches) Prune() (int, error)
```

Prune removes expired and unused items from this cache. The last one requires a full build so the cache usage can be tracked. Note that we operate directly on the filesystem here, so this is not thread safe.

```go
func (f Caches) SetResourceFs(fs afero.Fs)
```



<a name="FileCacheConfig"></a>
## type [FileCacheConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache_config.go#L107-L121>)



```go
type FileCacheConfig struct {
    // Max age of cache entries in this cache. Any items older than this will
    // be removed and not returned from the cache.
    // A negative value means forever, 0 means cache is disabled.
    // Hugo is lenient with what types it accepts here, but we recommend using
    // a duration string, a sequence of  decimal numbers, each with optional fraction and a unit suffix,
    // such as "300ms", "1.5h" or "2h45m".
    // Valid time units are "ns", "us" (or "µs"), "ms", "s", "m", "h".
    MaxAge time.Duration

    // The directory where files are stored.
    Dir string
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="FileCacheConfig.MarshalJSON"></a>
### func \(FileCacheConfig\) [MarshalJSON](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache_config.go#L148>)

```go
func (c FileCacheConfig) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error)
```

MarshalJSON marshals FileCacheConfig to JSON with MaxAge as a human\-readable string.

<a name="ItemInfo"></a>
## type [ItemInfo](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/filecache/filecache.go#L82-L85>)

ItemInfo contains info about a cached file.

```go
type ItemInfo struct {
    // This is the file's name relative to the cache's filesystem.
    Name string
}
```

## Variables

<a name="DefaultConfig"></a>DefaultConfig holds the default configuration for the HTTP cache.

```go
var DefaultConfig = Config{
    RespectCacheControlNoStoreInRequest:  true,
    RespectCacheControlNoStoreInResponse: false,
    Cache: Cache{
        For: GlobMatcher{
            Excludes: []string{"**"},
        },
    },
    Polls: []PollConfig{
        {
            For: GlobMatcher{
                Includes: []string{"**"},
            },
            Disable: true,
        },
    },
}
```

<a name="Cache"></a>
## type [Cache](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/httpcache/httpcache.go#L62-L65>)



```go
type Cache struct {
    // Enable HTTP cache behavior (RFC 9111) for these resources.
    For GlobMatcher
}
```

<a name="Config"></a>
## type [Config](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/httpcache/httpcache.go#L46-L60>)

Config holds the configuration for the HTTP cache.

```go
type Config struct {
    // When enabled and there's a Cache-Control: no-store directive in the request, response will never be stored in disk cache.
    RespectCacheControlNoStoreInRequest bool

    // When enabled and there's a Cache-Control: no-store directive in the response, response will never be stored in disk cache.
    RespectCacheControlNoStoreInResponse bool

    // Enables HTTP cache behavior (RFC 9111) for these resources.
    // When this is not enabled for a resource, Hugo will go straight to the file cache.
    Cache Cache

    // Polls holds a list of configurations for polling remote resources to detect changes in watch mode.
    // This can be disabled for some resources, typically if they are known to not change.
    Polls []PollConfig
}
```

```go
type GlobMatcher struct {

    Excludes []string
```

<a name="PollConfig"></a>
## type [PollConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/cache/httpcache/httpcache.go#L96-L111>)

PollConfig holds the configuration for polling remote resources to detect changes in watch mode.

```go
type PollConfig struct {
    // What remote resources to apply this configuration to.
    For GlobMatcher

    // Disable polling for this configuration.
    Disable bool

    // Low is the lower bound for the polling interval.
    // This is the starting point when the resource has recently changed,
    // if that resource stops changing, the polling interval will gradually increase towards High.
    Low time.Duration

    // High is the upper bound for the polling interval.
    // This is the interval used when the resource is stable.
    High time.Duration
}
```


```go
    Config PollConfig
}
```

<a name="Append"></a>
## func [Append](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/collections/append.go#L26>)

```go
func Append(to any, from ...any) (any, error)
```

Append appends from to a slice to and returns the resulting slice. If length of from is one and the only element is a slice of same type as to, it will be appended.

<a name="Slice"></a>
## func [Slice](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/collections/slice.go#L29>)

```go
func Slice(args ...any) any
```

Slice returns a slice of all passed arguments.

<a name="StringSliceToInterfaceSlice"></a>
## func [StringSliceToInterfaceSlice](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/collections/slice.go#L70>)

```go
func StringSliceToInterfaceSlice(ss []string) []any
```

StringSliceToInterfaceSlice converts ss to \[\]interface\{\}.

<a name="Grouper"></a>
## type [Grouper](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/collections/collections.go#L19-L21>)

Grouper defines a very generic way to group items by a given key.

```go
type Grouper interface {
    Group(key any, items any) (any, error)
}
```

<a name="Order"></a>
## type [Order](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/collections/order.go#L16-L20>)



```go
type Order interface {
    // Ordinal is a zero-based ordinal that represents the order of an object
    // in a collection.
    Ordinal() int
}
```

<a name="Slicer"></a>
## type [Slicer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/collections/slice.go#L24-L26>)

Slicer defines a very generic way to create a typed slice. This is used in collections.Slice template func to get types such as Pages, PageGroups etc. instead of the less useful \[\]interface\{\}.

```go
type Slicer interface {
    Slice(items any) (any, error)
}
```



Contains returns true if s is in ss.


Count returns the number of times s is in ss.

# constants

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/constants"
```

## Index

- [Constants](<#constants>)
- [func IsFieldRelOrPermalink\(name string\) bool](<#IsFieldRelOrPermalink>)
- [func IsResourceTransformationPermalinkHash\(name string\) bool](<#IsResourceTransformationPermalinkHash>)


## Constants

<a name="ErrRemoteGetJSON"></a>Error/Warning IDs. Do not change these values.

```go
const (
    // IDs for remote errors in tpl/data.
    ErrRemoteGetJSON = "error-remote-getjson"
    ErrRemoteGetCSV  = "error-remote-getcsv"

    WarnFrontMatterParamsOverrides = "warning-frontmatter-params-overrides"
    WarnRenderShortcodesInHTML     = "warning-rendershortcodes-in-html"
    WarnGoldmarkRawHTML            = "warning-goldmark-raw-html"
    WarnPartialSuperfluousPrefix   = "warning-partial-superfluous-prefix"
    WarnHomePageIsLeafBundle       = "warning-home-page-is-leaf-bundle"
)
```

<a name="FieldRelPermalink"></a>Field/method names with special meaning.

```go
const (
    FieldRelPermalink = "RelPermalink"
    FieldPermalink    = "Permalink"
)
```

<a name="ResourceTransformationFingerprint"></a>Resource transformations.

```go
const (
    ResourceTransformationFingerprint = "fingerprint"
)
```

<a name="IsFieldRelOrPermalink"></a>
## func [IsFieldRelOrPermalink](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/constants/constants.go#L37>)

```go
func IsFieldRelOrPermalink(name string) bool
```

IsFieldRelOrPermalink returns whether the given name is a RelPermalink or Permalink.

<a name="IsResourceTransformationPermalinkHash"></a>
## func [IsResourceTransformationPermalinkHash](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/constants/constants.go#L47>)

```go
func IsResourceTransformationPermalinkHash(name string) bool
```

IsResourceTransformationPermalinkHash returns whether the given name is a resource transformation that changes the permalink based on the content.

<a name="Hash"></a>
## func [Hash](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hashing/hashing.go#L165>)

```go
func Hash(vs ...any) (uint64, error)
```

Hash returns a hash from vs.

<a name="HashString"></a>
## func [HashString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hashing/hashing.go#L112>)

```go
func HashString(vs ...any) string
```

HashString returns a hash from the given elements. It will panic if the hash cannot be calculated. Note that this hash should be used primarily for identity, not for change detection as it in the more complex values \(e.g. Page\) will not hash the full content.

<a name="HashStringHex"></a>
## func [HashStringHex](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hashing/hashing.go#L119>)

```go
func HashStringHex(vs ...any) string
```

HashStringHex returns a hash from the given elements as a hex encoded string. See HashString for more information.

<a name="HashUint64"></a>
## func [HashUint64](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hashing/hashing.go#L145>)

```go
func HashUint64(vs ...any) uint64
```

HashUint64 returns a hash from the given elements. It will panic if the hash cannot be calculated. Note that this hash should be used primarily for identity, not for change detection as it in the more complex values \(e.g. Page\) will not hash the full content.

<a name="MD5FromStringHexEncoded"></a>

```go

```

MD5FromStringHexEncoded returns the MD5 hash of the given string.

## Variables

<a name="ErrFeatureNotAvailable"></a>ErrFeatureNotAvailable denotes that a feature is unavailable.

We will, at least to begin with, make some Hugo features \(SCSS with libsass\) optional, and this error is used to signal those situations.

```go
var ErrFeatureNotAvailable = &FeatureNotAvailableError{Cause: errors.New("this feature is not available in your current Hugo version, see https://goo.gl/YMrWcn for more information")}
```

<a name="NopLineMatcher"></a>NopLineMatcher is a matcher that always returns 1. This will effectively give line 1, column 1.

```go
var NopLineMatcher = func(m LineMatcher) int {
    return 1
}
```

<a name="OffsetMatcher"></a>OffsetMatcher is a line matcher that matches by offset.

```go
var OffsetMatcher = func(m LineMatcher) int {
    if m.Offset+len(m.Line) >= m.Position.Offset {

        return 0
    }
    return -1
}
```

<a name="SimpleLineMatcher"></a>SimpleLineMatcher simply matches by line number.

```go
var SimpleLineMatcher = func(m LineMatcher) int {
    if m.Position.LineNumber == m.LineNumber {

        return 0
    }
    return -1
}
```

<a name="Cause"></a>
## func [Cause](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/herrors/file_error.go#L268>)

```go
func Cause(err error) error
```

Cause returns the underlying error, that is, it unwraps errors until it finds one that does not implement the Unwrap method. For a shallow variant, see Unwrap.

<a name="ErrorContext"></a>
## type [ErrorContext](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/herrors/error_locator.go#L76-L91>)

ErrorContext contains contextual information about an error. This will typically be the lines surrounding some problem in a file.

```go
type ErrorContext struct {
    // If a match will contain the matched line and up to 2 lines before and after.
    // Will be empty if no match.
    Lines []string

    // The position of the error in the Lines above. 0 based.
    LinesPos int

    // The position of the content in the file. Note that this may be different from the error's position set
    // in FileError.
    Position text.Position

    // The lexer to use for syntax highlighting.
    // https://gohugo.io/content-management/syntax-highlighting/#list-of-chroma-highlighting-languages
    ChromaLexer string
}
```

## type [ErrorSender](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/herrors/errors.go#L28-L30>)

ErrorSender is a, typically, non\-blocking error handler.

```go
type ErrorSender interface {
    SendError(err error)
}
```

<a name="FileError"></a>
## type [FileError](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/herrors/file_error.go#L34-L50>)

FileError represents an error when handling a file: Parsing a config file, execute a template etc.

```go
type FileError interface {

    // ErrorContext holds some context information about the error.
    ErrorContext() *ErrorContext

    text.Positioner

    // UpdatePosition updates the position of the error.
    UpdatePosition(pos text.Position) FileError

    // UpdateContent updates the error with a new ErrorContext from the content of the file.
    UpdateContent(r io.Reader, linematcher LineMatcherFn) FileError

    // SetFilename sets the filename of the error.
    SetFilename(filename string) FileError
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

```go
func NewFileError(err error) FileError
```

NewFileError creates a new FileError that wraps err. It will try to extract the filename and line number from err.

```go
type LineMatcher struct {

}
```

<a name="LineMatcherFn"></a>
## type [LineMatcherFn](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/herrors/error_locator.go#L37>)

LineMatcherFn is used to match a line with an error. It returns the column number or 0 if the line was found, but column could not be determined. Returns \-1 if no line match.

```go
type LineMatcherFn func(m LineMatcher) int
```

## Variables

<a name="WithContext"></a>

```go
var WithContext = func(ctx context.Context) func(c *commandeer) {
    return func(c *commandeer) {
        c.ctx = ctx
    }
}
```

<a name="WithDir"></a>

```go
var WithDir = func(dir string) func(c *commandeer) {
    return func(c *commandeer) {
        c.dir = dir
    }
}
```

<a name="WithEnviron"></a>

```go
var WithEnviron = func(env []string) func(c *commandeer) {
    return func(c *commandeer) {
        setOrAppend := func(s string) {
            k1, _ := config.SplitEnvVar(s)
            var found bool
            for i, v := range c.env {
                k2, _ := config.SplitEnvVar(v)
                if k1 == k2 {
                    found = true
                    c.env[i] = s
                }
            }

            if !found {
                c.env = append(c.env, s)
            }
        }

        for _, s := range env {
            setOrAppend(s)
        }
    }
}
```

<a name="WithStderr"></a>

```go
var WithStderr = func(w io.Writer) func(c *commandeer) {
    return func(c *commandeer) {
        c.stderr = w
    }
}
```

<a name="WithStdin"></a>

```go
var WithStdin = func(r io.Reader) func(c *commandeer) {
    return func(c *commandeer) {
        c.stdin = r
    }
}
```

<a name="WithStdout"></a>

```go
var WithStdout = func(w io.Writer) func(c *commandeer) {
    return func(c *commandeer) {
        c.stdout = w
    }
}
```

<a name="Exec"></a>

Exec enforces a security policy for commands run via os/exec.

```go
type Exec struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hexec/exec.go#L91>)

```go
func New(cfg security.Config, workingDir string, log loggers.Logger) *Exec
```

New creates a new Exec using the provided security config.

<a name="Exec.New"></a>
### func \(\*Exec\) [New](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hexec/exec.go#L129>)

```go
func (e *Exec) New(name string, arg ...any) (Runner, error)
```



<a name="Exec.Npx"></a>
### func \(\*Exec\) [Npx](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hexec/exec.go#L177>)

```go
func (e *Exec) Npx(name string, arg ...any) (Runner, error)
```

Npx will in order: 1. Try fo find the binary in the WORKINGDIR/node\_modules/.bin directory. 2. If not found, and npx is available, run npx \-\-no\-install \<name\> \<args\>. 3. Fall back to the PATH. If name is "tailwindcss", we will try the PATH as the second option.

### func \(\*Exec\) [Sec](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hexec/exec.go#L253>)

```go
func (e *Exec) Sec() security.Config
```

Sec returns the security policies this Exec is configured with.

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/himage"
```

Package himage provides some high level image types and interfaces.

<a name="FrameDurationsToGifDelays"></a>
## func [FrameDurationsToGifDelays](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/himage/image.go#L48>)

```go
func FrameDurationsToGifDelays(frameDurations []int) []int
```

FrameDurationsToGifDelays converts frame durations in milliseconds to GIF delays in 100ths of a second.

<a name="GifDelaysToFrameDurations"></a>
## func [GifDelaysToFrameDurations](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/himage/image.go#L61>)

```go
func GifDelaysToFrameDurations(delays []int) []int
```

GifDelaysToFrameDurations converts GIF delays in 100ths of a second to frame durations in milliseconds.

<a name="AnimatedImage"></a>
## type [AnimatedImage](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/himage/image.go#L21-L26>)

AnimatedImage represents an animated image. This is currently supported for GIF and WebP images.

```go
type AnimatedImage interface {
    image.Image        // The first frame.
    GetRaw() any       // *gif.GIF or *WEBP.
    GetLoopCount() int // Number of times to loop the animation. 0 means infinite.
    ImageFrames
}
```

<a name="ImageConfigProvider"></a>
## type [ImageConfigProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/himage/image.go#L42-L44>)

ImageConfigProvider provides access to the image.Config of an image.

```go
type ImageConfigProvider interface {
    GetImageConfig() image.Config
}
```

<a name="ImageFrames"></a>
## type [ImageFrames](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/himage/image.go#L29-L39>)

ImageFrames provides access to the frames of an animated image.

```go
type ImageFrames interface {
    GetFrames() []image.Image

    // Frame durations in milliseconds.
    // Note that Gif frame durations are in 100ths of a second,
    // so they need to be multiplied by 10 to get milliseconds and vice versa.
    GetFrameDurations() []int

    SetFrames(frames []image.Image)
    SetWidthHeight(width, height int)
}
```

<a name="Lock"></a>
## func [Lock](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hiter/iter.go#L43>)

```go
func Lock[V any](seq iter.Seq[V], lock, unlock func()) iter.Seq[V]
```

Lock returns an iterator that locks before iterating and unlocks after.

<a name="Lock2"></a>

```go
func Lock2[K, V any](seq iter.Seq2[K, V], lock, unlock func()) iter.Seq2[K, V]
```

Lock2 returns an iterator that locks before iterating and unlocks after.

```go
func CleanConfigStringMap(m map[string]any) map[string]any
```

CleanConfigStringMap is the same as CleanConfigStringMapString but for map\[string\]any.

<a name="CleanConfigStringMapString"></a>

```go
func CleanConfigStringMapString(m map[string]string) map[string]string
```

CleanConfigStringMapString removes any processing instructions from m, m will never be modified.

<a name="ConvertFloat64WithNoDecimalsToInt"></a>
## func [ConvertFloat64WithNoDecimalsToInt](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/maps.go#L216>)

```go
func ConvertFloat64WithNoDecimalsToInt(m map[string]any)
```

ConvertFloat64WithNoDecimalsToInt converts float64 values with no decimals to int recursively.

<a name="GetNestedParam"></a>
## func [GetNestedParam](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L244>)

```go
func GetNestedParam(keyStr, separator string, candidates ...Params) (any, error)
```

GetNestedParam gets the first match of the keyStr in the candidates given. It will first try the exact match and then try to find it as a nested map value, using the given separator, e.g. "mymap.name". It assumes that all the maps given have lower cased keys.

<a name="GetNestedParamFn"></a>
## func [GetNestedParamFn](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L264>)

```go
func GetNestedParamFn(keyStr, separator string, lookupFn func(key string) any) (any, string, map[string]any, error)
```



<a name="LookupEqualFold"></a>
## func [LookupEqualFold](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/maps.go#L117>)

```go
func LookupEqualFold[T any | string](m map[string]T, key string) (T, string, bool)
```

LookupEqualFold finds key in m with case insensitive equality checks.

<a name="MergeParams"></a>
## func [MergeParams](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L96>)

```go
func MergeParams(dst, src Params)
```

MergeParams transfers values from src to dst for new keys using the merge encoded in dst. This is done recursively.

<a name="MergeParamsWithStrategy"></a>
## func [MergeParamsWithStrategy](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L90>)

```go
func MergeParamsWithStrategy(strategy string, dst, src Params)
```

MergeParamsWithStrategy transfers values from src to dst for new keys using the merge strategy given. This is done recursively.

<a name="MergeShallow"></a>
## func [MergeShallow](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/maps.go#L132>)

```go
func MergeShallow(dst, src map[string]any)
```

MergeShallow merges src into dst, but only if the key does not already exist in dst. The keys are compared case insensitively.

<a name="PrepareParams"></a>
## func [PrepareParams](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L376>)

```go
func PrepareParams(m Params)
```

PrepareParams \* makes all the keys in the given map lower cased and will do so recursively. \* This will modify the map given. \* Any nested map\[interface\{\}\]interface\{\}, map\[string\]interface\{\},map\[string\]string will be converted to Params. \* Any \_merge value will be converted to proper type and value.

<a name="SetParams"></a>
## func [SetParams](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L43>)

```go
func SetParams(dst, src Params)
```

SetParams overwrites values in dst with values in src for common or new keys. This is done recursively.

```go
func ToSliceStringMap(in any) ([]map[string]any, error)
```

ToSliceStringMap converts in to \[\]map\[string\]interface\{\}.
