<a name="Cache"></a>
## type [Cache](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L21-L26>)

Cache is a simple thread safe cache backed by a map.

```go
type Cache[K comparable, T any] struct {
    sync.RWMutex
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewCache"></a>
### func [NewCache](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L37>)

```go
func NewCache[K comparable, T any]() *Cache[K, T]
```

NewCache creates a new Cache with default options.

<a name="NewCacheWithOptions"></a>
### func [NewCacheWithOptions](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L42>)

```go
func NewCacheWithOptions[K comparable, T any](opts CacheOptions) *Cache[K, T]
```

NewCacheWithOptions creates a new Cache with the given options.

<a name="Cache[K, T].Contains"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\[K, T\]\) [Contains](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L88>)

```go
func (c *Cache[K, T]) Contains(key K) bool
```

Contains returns whether the given key exists in the cache.

<a name="Cache[K, T].Drain"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\[K, T\]\) [Drain](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L169>)

```go
func (c *Cache[K, T]) Drain() map[K]T
```



<a name="Cache[K, T].ForEeach"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\[K, T\]\) [ForEeach](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L159>)

```go
func (c *Cache[K, T]) ForEeach(f func(K, T) bool)
```

ForEeach calls the given function for each key/value pair in the cache. If the function returns false, the iteration stops.

<a name="Cache[K, T].Get"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\[K, T\]\) [Get](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L48>)

```go
func (c *Cache[K, T]) Get(key K) (T, bool)
```

Delete deletes the given key from the cache. If c is nil, this method is a no\-op.

<a name="Cache[K, T].GetOrCreate"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\[K, T\]\) [GetOrCreate](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L65>)

```go
func (c *Cache[K, T]) GetOrCreate(key K, create func() (T, error)) (T, error)
```

GetOrCreate gets the value for the given key if it exists, or creates it if not.

<a name="Cache[K, T].InitAndGet"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\[K, T\]\) [InitAndGet](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L97>)

```go
func (c *Cache[K, T]) InitAndGet(key K, init func(get func(key K) (T, bool), set func(key K, value T)) error) (T, error)
```

InitAndGet initializes the cache if not already done and returns the value for the given key. The init state will be reset on Reset or Drain.

<a name="Cache[K, T].Len"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\[K, T\]\) [Len](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L178>)

```go
func (c *Cache[K, T]) Len() int
```



<a name="Cache[K, T].Reset"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\[K, T\]\) [Reset](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L184>)

```go
func (c *Cache[K, T]) Reset()
```



<a name="Cache[K, T].Set"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\[K, T\]\) [Set](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L128>)

```go
func (c *Cache[K, T]) Set(key K, value T)
```

Set sets the given key to the given value.

<a name="Cache[K, T].SetIfAbsent"></a>
### func \(\*Cache\[K, T\]\) [SetIfAbsent](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L135>)

```go
func (c *Cache[K, T]) SetIfAbsent(key K, value T)
```

SetIfAbsent sets the given key to the given value if the key does not already exist in the cache.

<a name="CacheOptions"></a>
## type [CacheOptions](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L29-L32>)

CacheOptions are the options for the Cache.

```go
type CacheOptions struct {
    // If set, the cache will not grow beyond this size.
    Size uint64
}
```

<a name="KeyParams"></a>
## type [KeyParams](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L28-L31>)

KeyParams is an utility struct for the WalkParams method.

```go
type KeyParams struct {
    Key    string
    Params Params
}
```

<a name="KeyRenamer"></a>
## type [KeyRenamer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/maps.go#L153-L155>)

KeyRenamer supports renaming of keys in a map.

```go
type KeyRenamer struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewKeyRenamer"></a>
### func [NewKeyRenamer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/maps.go#L159>)

```go
func NewKeyRenamer(patternKeys ...string) (KeyRenamer, error)
```

NewKeyRenamer creates a new KeyRenamer given a list of pattern and new key value pairs.

<a name="KeyRenamer.Rename"></a>
### func \(KeyRenamer\) [Rename](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/maps.go#L184>)

```go
func (r KeyRenamer) Rename(m map[string]any)
```

Rename renames the keys in the given map according to the patterns in the current KeyRenamer.

<a name="Map"></a>
## type [Map](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/map.go#L28-L31>)

Map is a thread safe map backed by a Go map.

```go
type Map[K comparable, T any] struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewMap"></a>
### func [NewMap](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/map.go#L21>)

```go
func NewMap[K comparable, T any]() *Map[K, T]
```



<a name="Map[K, T].All"></a>
### func \(\*Map\[K, T\]\) [All](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/map.go#L119>)

```go
func (m *Map[K, T]) All() iter.Seq2[K, T]
```

All returns an iterator over all key/value pairs in the map. A read lock is held during the iteration.

<a name="Map[K, T].Delete"></a>
### func \(\*Map\[K, T\]\) [Delete](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/map.go#L78>)

```go
func (m *Map[K, T]) Delete(key K) bool
```

Delete deletes the given key from the map. It returns true if the key was found and deleted, false otherwise.

<a name="Map[K, T].Get"></a>
### func \(\*Map\[K, T\]\) [Get](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/map.go#L35>)

```go
func (m *Map[K, T]) Get(key K) T
```

Get gets the value for the given key. It returns the zero value of T if the key is not found.

<a name="Map[K, T].GetOrCreate"></a>
### func \(\*Map\[K, T\]\) [GetOrCreate](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/map.go#L50>)

```go
func (m *Map[K, T]) GetOrCreate(key K, create func() (T, error)) (T, error)
```

GetOrCreate gets the value for the given key if it exists, or creates it if not.

<a name="Map[K, T].Lookup"></a>
### func \(\*Map\[K, T\]\) [Lookup](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/map.go#L42>)

```go
func (m *Map[K, T]) Lookup(key K) (T, bool)
```

Lookup looks up the given key in the map. It returns the value and a boolean indicating whether the key was found.

<a name="Map[K, T].Set"></a>
### func \(\*Map\[K, T\]\) [Set](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/map.go#L70>)

```go
func (m *Map[K, T]) Set(key K, value T)
```

Set sets the given key to the given value.

<a name="Map[K, T].SetIfAbsent"></a>
### func \(\*Map\[K, T\]\) [SetIfAbsent](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/map.go#L97>)

```go
func (m *Map[K, T]) SetIfAbsent(key K, value T) bool
```

SetIfAbsent sets the given key to the given value if the key does not already exist in the map. It returns true if the value was set, false otherwise.

<a name="Map[K, T].WithWriteLock"></a>
### func \(\*Map\[K, T\]\) [WithWriteLock](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/map.go#L89>)

```go
func (m *Map[K, T]) WithWriteLock(f func(m map[K]T) error) error
```

WithWriteLock executes the given function with a write lock on the map.

<a name="Ordered"></a>
## type [Ordered](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/ordered.go#L26-L31>)

Ordered is a map that can be iterated in the order of insertion. Note that insertion order is not affected if a key is re\-inserted into the map. In a nil map, all operations are no\-ops. This is not thread safe.

```go
type Ordered[K comparable, T any] struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewOrdered"></a>
### func [NewOrdered](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/ordered.go#L34>)

```go
func NewOrdered[K comparable, T any]() *Ordered[K, T]
```

NewOrdered creates a new Ordered map.

<a name="Ordered[K, T].Clone"></a>
### func \(\*Ordered\[K, T\]\) [Clone](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/ordered.go#L85>)

```go
func (m *Ordered[K, T]) Clone() *Ordered[K, T]
```

Clone creates a shallow copy of the map.

<a name="Ordered[K, T].Delete"></a>
### func \(\*Ordered\[K, T\]\) [Delete](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/ordered.go#L71>)

```go
func (m *Ordered[K, T]) Delete(key K)
```

Delete deletes the value for the given key.

<a name="Ordered[K, T].Get"></a>
### func \(\*Ordered\[K, T\]\) [Get](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/ordered.go#L52>)

```go
func (m *Ordered[K, T]) Get(key K) (T, bool)
```

Get gets the value for the given key.

<a name="Ordered[K, T].Has"></a>
### func \(\*Ordered\[K, T\]\) [Has](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/ordered.go#L62>)

```go
func (m *Ordered[K, T]) Has(key K) bool
```

Has returns whether the given key exists in the map.

<a name="Ordered[K, T].Hash"></a>
### func \(\*Ordered\[K, T\]\) [Hash](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/ordered.go#L139>)

```go
func (m *Ordered[K, T]) Hash() (uint64, error)
```

Hash calculates a hash from the values.

<a name="Ordered[K, T].Keys"></a>
### func \(\*Ordered\[K, T\]\) [Keys](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/ordered.go#L97>)

```go
func (m *Ordered[K, T]) Keys() []K
```

Keys returns the keys in the order they were added.

<a name="Ordered[K, T].Len"></a>
### func \(\*Ordered\[K, T\]\) [Len](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/ordered.go#L117>)

```go
func (m *Ordered[K, T]) Len() int
```

Len returns the number of items in the map.

<a name="Ordered[K, T].Range"></a>
### func \(\*Ordered\[K, T\]\) [Range](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/ordered.go#L127>)

```go
func (m *Ordered[K, T]) Range(f func(key K, value T) bool)
```

Range calls f sequentially for each key and value present in the map. If f returns false, range stops the iteration. TODO\(bep\) replace with iter.Seq2 when we bump go Go 1.24.

<a name="Ordered[K, T].Set"></a>
### func \(\*Ordered\[K, T\]\) [Set](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/ordered.go#L40>)

```go
func (m *Ordered[K, T]) Set(key K, value T)
```

Set sets the value for the given key. Note that insertion order is not affected if a key is re\-inserted into the map.

<a name="Ordered[K, T].Values"></a>
### func \(\*Ordered\[K, T\]\) [Values](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/ordered.go#L105>)

```go
func (m *Ordered[K, T]) Values() []T
```

Values returns the values in the order they were added.

<a name="OrderedIntSet"></a>
## type [OrderedIntSet](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L23-L26>)



```go
type OrderedIntSet struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewOrderedIntSet"></a>
### func [NewOrderedIntSet](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L30>)

```go
func NewOrderedIntSet(vals ...int) *OrderedIntSet
```

NewOrderedIntSet creates a new OrderedIntSet. Note that this is backed by https://github.com/bits-and-blooms/bitset

<a name="OrderedIntSet.Clone"></a>
### func \(\*OrderedIntSet\) [Clone](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L65>)

```go
func (m *OrderedIntSet) Clone() *OrderedIntSet
```



<a name="OrderedIntSet.ForEachKey"></a>
### func \(\*OrderedIntSet\) [ForEachKey](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L89>)

```go
func (m *OrderedIntSet) ForEachKey(yield func(int) bool) bool
```

The reason we don't use iter.Seq is https://github.com/golang/go/issues/69015 This is 70% faster than using iter.Seq2\[int, int\] for the keys. It returns false if the iteration was stopped early.

<a name="OrderedIntSet.Has"></a>
### func \(\*OrderedIntSet\) [Has](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L101>)

```go
func (m *OrderedIntSet) Has(key int) bool
```



<a name="OrderedIntSet.IsSuperSet"></a>
### func \(\*OrderedIntSet\) [IsSuperSet](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L139>)

```go
func (m *OrderedIntSet) IsSuperSet(other *OrderedIntSet) bool
```



<a name="OrderedIntSet.KeysSorted"></a>
### func \(\*OrderedIntSet\) [KeysSorted](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L116>)

```go
func (m *OrderedIntSet) KeysSorted() []int
```

KeysSorted returns the keys in sorted order.

<a name="OrderedIntSet.Len"></a>
### func \(\*OrderedIntSet\) [Len](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L108>)

```go
func (m *OrderedIntSet) Len() int
```



<a name="OrderedIntSet.Next"></a>
### func \(\*OrderedIntSet\) [Next](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L78>)

```go
func (m *OrderedIntSet) Next(i int) int
```

Next returns the next key in the set possibly including the given key. It returns \-1 if the key is not found or if there are no keys greater than the given key.

<a name="OrderedIntSet.Set"></a>
### func \(\*OrderedIntSet\) [Set](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L43>)

```go
func (m *OrderedIntSet) Set(key int)
```

Set sets the value for the given key. Note that insertion order is not affected if a key is re\-inserted into the set.

<a name="OrderedIntSet.SetFrom"></a>
### func \(\*OrderedIntSet\) [SetFrom](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L56>)

```go
func (m *OrderedIntSet) SetFrom(other *OrderedIntSet)
```

SetFrom sets the values from another OrderedIntSet.

<a name="OrderedIntSet.String"></a>
### func \(\*OrderedIntSet\) [String](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L125>)

```go
func (m *OrderedIntSet) String() string
```



<a name="OrderedIntSet.Values"></a>
### func \(\*OrderedIntSet\) [Values](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L132>)

```go
func (m *OrderedIntSet) Values() *bitset.BitSet
```



<a name="OrderedIntSet.Words"></a>
### func \(\*OrderedIntSet\) [Words](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/orderedintset.go#L149>)

```go
func (m *OrderedIntSet) Words() []uint64
```

Words returns the bitset as array of 64\-bit words, giving direct access to the internal representation. It is not a copy, so changes to the returned slice will affect the bitset. It is meant for advanced users.

<a name="Params"></a>
## type [Params](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L25>)

Params is a map where all keys are lower case.

```go
type Params map[string]any
```

<a name="CloneParamsDeep"></a>
### func [CloneParamsDeep](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L415>)

```go
func CloneParamsDeep(m Params) Params
```

CloneParamsDeep does a deep clone of the given Params, meaning that any nested Params will be cloned as well.

<a name="CreateNestedParamsFromSegements"></a>
### func [CreateNestedParamsFromSegements](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L191>)

```go
func CreateNestedParamsFromSegements(target Params, keySegments ...string) Params
```

CreateNestedParamsFromSegements creates empty nested maps for the given keySegments in the target map.

<a name="CreateNestedParamsSepString"></a>
### func [CreateNestedParamsSepString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L216>)

```go
func CreateNestedParamsSepString(keyStr, separator string, target Params) Params
```

CreateNestedParamsSepString creates empty nested maps for the given keyStr in the target map It returns the last map created.

<a name="MustToParamsAndPrepare"></a>
### func [MustToParamsAndPrepare](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/maps.go#L59>)

```go
func MustToParamsAndPrepare(in any) Params
```

MustToParamsAndPrepare calls ToParamsAndPrepare and panics if it fails.

<a name="PrepareParamsClone"></a>
### func [PrepareParamsClone](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L437>)

```go
func PrepareParamsClone(m Params) Params
```

PrepareParamsClone is like PrepareParams, but it does not modify the input.

<a name="SetNestedParamIfNotSet"></a>
### func [SetNestedParamIfNotSet](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L223>)

```go
func SetNestedParamIfNotSet(keyStr, separator string, value any, target Params) Params
```

SetNestedParamIfNotSet sets the value for the given keyStr in the target map if it does not exist. It assumes that all but the last key in keyStr is a Params map or should be one.

<a name="ToParamsAndPrepare"></a>
### func [ToParamsAndPrepare](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/maps.go#L46>)

```go
func ToParamsAndPrepare(in any) (Params, error)
```

ToParamsAndPrepare converts in to Params and prepares it for use. If in is nil, an empty map is returned. See PrepareParams.

<a name="Params.DeleteMergeStrategy"></a>
### func \(Params\) [DeleteMergeStrategy](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L143>)

```go
func (p Params) DeleteMergeStrategy() bool
```

For internal use.

<a name="Params.GetMergeStrategy"></a>
### func \(Params\) [GetMergeStrategy](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L133>)

```go
func (p Params) GetMergeStrategy() (ParamsMergeStrategy, bool)
```

For internal use.

<a name="Params.GetNested"></a>
### func \(Params\) [GetNested](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L36>)

```go
func (p Params) GetNested(indices ...string) any
```

GetNested does a lower case and nested search in this map. It will return nil if none found. Make all of these methods internal somehow.

<a name="Params.IsZero"></a>
### func \(Params\) [IsZero](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L72>)

```go
func (p Params) IsZero() bool
```

IsZero returns true if p is considered empty.

<a name="Params.SetMergeStrategy"></a>
### func \(Params\) [SetMergeStrategy](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L152>)

```go
func (p Params) SetMergeStrategy(s ParamsMergeStrategy)
```

For internal use.

<a name="ParamsMergeStrategy"></a>
## type [ParamsMergeStrategy](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/params.go#L292>)

ParamsMergeStrategy tells what strategy to use in Params.Merge.

```go
type ParamsMergeStrategy string
```

<a name="ParamsMergeStrategyNone"></a>

```go
const (
    // Do not merge.
    ParamsMergeStrategyNone ParamsMergeStrategy = "none"
    // Only add new keys.
    ParamsMergeStrategyShallow ParamsMergeStrategy = "shallow"
    // Add new keys, merge existing.
    ParamsMergeStrategyDeep ParamsMergeStrategy = "deep"

    MergeStrategyKey = "_merge"
)
```

<a name="SliceCache"></a>
## type [SliceCache](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L192-L195>)

SliceCache is a simple thread safe cache backed by a map.

```go
type SliceCache[T any] struct {
    sync.RWMutex
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewSliceCache"></a>
### func [NewSliceCache](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L197>)

```go
func NewSliceCache[T any]() *SliceCache[T]
```



<a name="SliceCache[T].Append"></a>
### func \(\*SliceCache\[T\]\) [Append](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L208>)

```go
func (c *SliceCache[T]) Append(key string, values ...T)
```



<a name="SliceCache[T].Get"></a>
### func \(\*SliceCache\[T\]\) [Get](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L201>)

```go
func (c *SliceCache[T]) Get(key string) ([]T, bool)
```



<a name="SliceCache[T].Reset"></a>
### func \(\*SliceCache\[T\]\) [Reset](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hmaps/cache.go#L214>)

```go
func (c *SliceCache[T]) Reset()
```



# hreflect

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/hreflect"
```

Package hreflect contains reflect helpers.

## Index

- [func AsTime\(v reflect.Value, loc \*time.Location\) \(time.Time, bool\)](<#AsTime>)
- [func CallMethodByName\(cxt context.Context, name string, v reflect.Value\) \[\]reflect.Value](<#CallMethodByName>)
- [func ConvertIfPossible\(val reflect.Value, typ reflect.Type\) \(reflect.Value, bool\)](<#ConvertIfPossible>)
- [func GetMethodByName\(v reflect.Value, name string\) reflect.Value](<#GetMethodByName>)
- [func GetMethodByNameForType\(tp reflect.Type, name string\) reflect.Method](<#GetMethodByNameForType>)
- [func GetMethodIndexByName\(tp reflect.Type, name string\) int](<#GetMethodIndexByName>)
- [func Indirect\(v reflect.Value\) \(vv reflect.Value, isNil bool\)](<#Indirect>)
- [func IndirectElem\(v reflect.Value\) \(vv reflect.Value, isNil bool\)](<#IndirectElem>)
- [func IsContextType\(tp reflect.Type\) bool](<#IsContextType>)
- [func IsFloat\(kind reflect.Kind\) bool](<#IsFloat>)
- [func IsInt\(kind reflect.Kind\) bool](<#IsInt>)
- [func IsInterfaceOrPointer\(kind reflect.Kind\) bool](<#IsInterfaceOrPointer>)
- [func IsMap\(v any\) bool](<#IsMap>)
- [func IsNil\(v reflect.Value\) bool](<#IsNil>)
- [func IsNumber\(kind reflect.Kind\) bool](<#IsNumber>)
- [func IsSlice\(v any\) bool](<#IsSlice>)
- [func IsString\(kind reflect.Kind\) bool](<#IsString>)
- [func IsTime\(tp reflect.Type\) bool](<#IsTime>)
- [func IsTruthful\(in any\) bool](<#IsTruthful>)
- [func IsTruthfulValue\(val reflect.Value\) \(truth bool\)](<#IsTruthfulValue>)
- [func IsUint\(kind reflect.Kind\) bool](<#IsUint>)
- [func IsValid\(v reflect.Value\) bool](<#IsValid>)
- [func ToFloat64\(v reflect.Value\) float64](<#ToFloat64>)
- [func ToFloat64E\(v reflect.Value\) \(float64, error\)](<#ToFloat64E>)
- [func ToInt64\(v reflect.Value\) int64](<#ToInt64>)
- [func ToInt64E\(v reflect.Value\) \(int64, error\)](<#ToInt64E>)
- [func ToSliceAny\(v any\) \(\[\]any, bool\)](<#ToSliceAny>)
- [func ToString\(v reflect.Value\) string](<#ToString>)
- [func ToStringE\(v reflect.Value\) \(string, error\)](<#ToStringE>)
- [func ToStringValueE\(v reflect.Value\) \(reflect.Value, error\)](<#ToStringValueE>)


<a name="AsTime"></a>
## func [AsTime](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L254>)

```go
func AsTime(v reflect.Value, loc *time.Location) (time.Time, bool)
```

AsTime returns v as a time.Time if possible. The given location is only used if the value implements AsTimeProvider \(e.g. go\-toml local\). A zero Time and false is returned if this isn't possible. Note that this function does not accept string dates.

<a name="CallMethodByName"></a>
## func [CallMethodByName](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L292>)

```go
func CallMethodByName(cxt context.Context, name string, v reflect.Value) []reflect.Value
```

CallMethodByName calls the method with the given name on v.

<a name="ConvertIfPossible"></a>
## func [ConvertIfPossible](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/convert.go#L97>)

```go
func ConvertIfPossible(val reflect.Value, typ reflect.Type) (reflect.Value, bool)
```

ConvertIfPossible tries to convert val to typ if possible. This is currently only implemented for int kinds, added to handle the move to a new YAML library which produces uint64 for unsigned integers. We can expand on this later if needed. This conversion is lossless. See Issue 14079.

<a name="GetMethodByName"></a>
## func [GetMethodByName](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L186>)

```go
func GetMethodByName(v reflect.Value, name string) reflect.Value
```

GetMethodByName is the same as reflect.Value.MethodByName, but it caches the lookup.

<a name="GetMethodByNameForType"></a>
## func [GetMethodByNameForType](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L170>)

```go
func GetMethodByNameForType(tp reflect.Type, name string) reflect.Method
```

GetMethodByNameForType returns the method with the given name for the given type, or a zero Method if no such method exists. It panics if tp is an interface type. It caches the lookup.

<a name="GetMethodIndexByName"></a>
## func [GetMethodIndexByName](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L198>)

```go
func GetMethodIndexByName(tp reflect.Type, name string) int
```

GetMethodIndexByName returns the index of the method with the given name, or \-1 if no such method exists.

<a name="Indirect"></a>
## func [Indirect](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L312>)

```go
func Indirect(v reflect.Value) (vv reflect.Value, isNil bool)
```

Indirect unwraps interfaces and pointers until it finds a non\-interface/pointer value. If a nil is encountered, the second return value is true. If a pointer to a struct is encountered, it is not unwrapped.

<a name="IndirectElem"></a>
## func [IndirectElem](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L332>)

```go
func IndirectElem(v reflect.Value) (vv reflect.Value, isNil bool)
```

IndirectElem is like Indirect, but if the final value is a pointer, it unwraps it.

<a name="IsContextType"></a>
## func [IsContextType](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L365>)

```go
func IsContextType(tp reflect.Type) bool
```

IsContextType returns whether tp is a context.Context type.

<a name="IsFloat"></a>
## func [IsFloat](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L60>)

```go
func IsFloat(kind reflect.Kind) bool
```

IsFloat returns whether the given kind is a float.

<a name="IsInt"></a>
## func [IsInt](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L40>)

```go
func IsInt(kind reflect.Kind) bool
```

IsInt returns whether the given kind is an int.

<a name="IsInterfaceOrPointer"></a>
## func [IsInterfaceOrPointer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L29>)

```go
func IsInterfaceOrPointer(kind reflect.Kind) bool
```

IsInterfaceOrPointer returns whether the given kind is an interface or a pointer.

<a name="IsMap"></a>
## func [IsMap](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L86>)

```go
func IsMap(v any) bool
```

IsMap reports whether v is a map.

<a name="IsNil"></a>
## func [IsNil](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L345>)

```go
func IsNil(v reflect.Value) bool
```

IsNil reports whether v is nil. Based on reflect.Value.IsNil, but also considers invalid values as nil.

<a name="IsNumber"></a>
## func [IsNumber](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L35>)

```go
func IsNumber(kind reflect.Kind) bool
```

TODO\(bep\) replace the private versions in /tpl with these. IsNumber returns whether the given kind is a number.

<a name="IsSlice"></a>
## func [IsSlice](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L91>)

```go
func IsSlice(v any) bool
```

IsSlice reports whether v is a slice.

<a name="IsString"></a>
## func [IsString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L70>)

```go
func IsString(kind reflect.Kind) bool
```

IsString returns whether the given kind is a string.

<a name="IsTime"></a>
## func [IsTime](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L225>)

```go
func IsTime(tp reflect.Type) bool
```

IsTime returns whether tp is a time.Time type or if it can be converted into one in ToTime.

<a name="IsTruthful"></a>
## func [IsTruthful](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L76>)

```go
func IsTruthful(in any) bool
```

IsTruthful returns whether in represents a truthful value. See IsTruthfulValue

<a name="IsTruthfulValue"></a>
## func [IsTruthfulValue](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L116>)

```go
func IsTruthfulValue(val reflect.Value) (truth bool)
```

IsTruthfulValue returns whether the given value has a meaningful truth value. This is based on template.IsTrue in Go's stdlib, but also considers IsZero and any interface value will be unwrapped before it's considered for truthfulness.

Based on: https://github.com/golang/go/blob/178a2c42254166cffed1b25fb1d3c7a5727cada6/src/text/template/exec.go#L306

<a name="IsUint"></a>
## func [IsUint](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L50>)

```go
func IsUint(kind reflect.Kind) bool
```

IsUint returns whether the given kind is an uint.

<a name="IsValid"></a>
## func [IsValid](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/helpers.go#L237>)

```go
func IsValid(v reflect.Value) bool
```

IsValid returns whether v is not nil and a valid value.

<a name="ToFloat64"></a>
## func [ToFloat64](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/convert.go#L54>)

```go
func ToFloat64(v reflect.Value) float64
```

ToFloat64 converts v to float64 if possible, panicking if not.

<a name="ToFloat64E"></a>
## func [ToFloat64E](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/convert.go#L46>)

```go
func ToFloat64E(v reflect.Value) (float64, error)
```

ToFloat64E converts v to float64 if possible, returning an error if not.

<a name="ToInt64"></a>
## func [ToInt64](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hreflect/convert.go#L37>)

```go
func ToInt64(v reflect.Value) int64
```

ToInt64 converts v to int64 if possible. It panics if the conversion is not possible.


ToInt64 converts v to int64 if possible, returning an error if not.


ToSliceAny converts the given value to a slice of any if possible.

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/hstore"
```

## Index

  - [func \(c \*Scratch\) SetInMap\(key string, mapKey string, value any\) string](<#Scratch.SetInMap>)
  - [func \(c \*Scratch\) Values\(\) map\[string\]any](<#Scratch.Values>)
- [type StoreProvider](<#StoreProvider>)


<a name="Scratch"></a>
## type [Scratch](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstore/scratch.go#L32-L35>)

Scratch is a writable context used for stateful build operations

```go
type Scratch struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewScratch"></a>
### func [NewScratch](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstore/scratch.go#L158>)

```go
func NewScratch() *Scratch
```

NewScratch returns a new instance of Scratch.

<a name="Scratch.Add"></a>
### func \(\*Scratch\) [Add](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstore/scratch.go#L41>)

```go
func (c *Scratch) Add(key string, newAddend any) (string, error)
```

Add will, for single values, add \(using the \+ operator\) the addend to the existing addend \(if found\). Supports numeric values and strings.

If the first add for a key is an array or slice, then the next value\(s\) will be appended.

<a name="Scratch.Delete"></a>
### func \(\*Scratch\) [Delete](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstore/scratch.go#L81>)

```go
func (c *Scratch) Delete(key string) string
```

Delete deletes the given key.

<a name="Scratch.DeleteInMap"></a>
### func \(\*Scratch\) [DeleteInMap](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstore/scratch.go#L121>)

```go
func (c *Scratch) DeleteInMap(key string, mapKey string) string
```

DeleteInMap deletes a value to a map with the given key in the Node context.

<a name="Scratch.Get"></a>
### func \(\*Scratch\) [Get](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstore/scratch.go#L89>)

```go
func (c *Scratch) Get(key string) any
```

Get returns a value previously set by Add or Set.

<a name="Scratch.GetSortedMapValues"></a>
### func \(\*Scratch\) [GetSortedMapValues](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstore/scratch.go#L132>)

```go
func (c *Scratch) GetSortedMapValues(key string) any
```

GetSortedMapValues returns a sorted map previously filled with SetInMap.

<a name="Scratch.Set"></a>
### func \(\*Scratch\) [Set](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstore/scratch.go#L73>)

```go
func (c *Scratch) Set(key string, value any) string
```

Set stores a value with the given key in the Node context. This value can later be retrieved with Get.

<a name="Scratch.SetInMap"></a>
### func \(\*Scratch\) [SetInMap](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstore/scratch.go#L108>)

```go
func (c *Scratch) SetInMap(key string, mapKey string, value any) string
```

SetInMap stores a value to a map with the given key in the Node context. This map can later be retrieved with GetSortedMapValues.

<a name="Scratch.Values"></a>
### func \(\*Scratch\) [Values](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstore/scratch.go#L100>)

```go
func (c *Scratch) Values() map[string]any
```

Values returns the raw backing map. Note that you should just use this method on the locally scoped Scratch instances you obtain via newScratch, not .Page.Scratch etc., as that will lead to concurrency issues.

<a name="StoreProvider"></a>
## type [StoreProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstore/scratch.go#L25-L29>)



```go
type StoreProvider interface {
    // Store returns a Scratch that can be used to store temporary state.
    // Store is not reset on server rebuilds.
    Store() *Scratch
}
```

<a name="EqualAny"></a>

```go
func EqualAny(a string, b ...string) bool
```

EqualAny returns whether a string is equal to any of the given strings.

<a name="GetOrCompileRegexp"></a>
## func [GetOrCompileRegexp](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstrings/strings.go#L96>)

```go
func GetOrCompileRegexp(pattern string) (re *regexp.Regexp, err error)
```

GetOrCompileRegexp retrieves a regexp object from the cache based upon the pattern. If the pattern is not found in the cache, the pattern is compiled and added to the cache.

<a name="HasAnyPrefix"></a>
## func [HasAnyPrefix](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstrings/strings.go#L101>)

```go
func HasAnyPrefix(s string, prefixes ...string) bool
```

HasAnyPrefix checks if the string s has any of the prefixes given.

<a name="InSlicEqualFold"></a>
## func [InSlicEqualFold](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstrings/strings.go#L119>)

```go
func InSlicEqualFold(arr []string, el string) bool
```

InSlicEqualFold checks if a string is an element of a slice of strings and returns a boolean value. It uses strings.EqualFold to compare.

<a name="InSlice"></a>
## func [InSlice](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstrings/strings.go#L112>)

```go
func InSlice(arr []string, el string) bool
```

InSlice checks if a string is an element of a slice of strings and returns a boolean value.

<a name="ToString"></a>
## func [ToString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstrings/strings.go#L132>)

```go
func ToString(v any) (string, bool)
```

ToString converts the given value to a string. Note that this is a more strict version compared to cast.ToString, as it will not try to convert numeric values to strings, but only accept strings or fmt.Stringer.

<a name="UniqueStrings"></a>
## func [UniqueStrings](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstrings/strings.go#L143>)

```go

```

UniqueStrings returns a new slice with any duplicates removed.

```go

```

UniqueStringsReuse returns a slice with any duplicates removed. It will modify the input slice.

## func [UniqueStringsSorted](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstrings/strings.go#L183>)

```go
func UniqueStringsSorted(s []string) []string
```

UniqueStringsSorted returns a sorted slice with any duplicates removed. It will modify the input slice.

<a name="StringEqualFold"></a>
## type [StringEqualFold](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hstrings/strings.go#L32>)

StringEqualFold is a string that implements the compare.Eqer interface and considers two strings equal if they are equal when folded to lower case. The compare.Eqer interface is used in Hugo to compare values in templates \(e.g. using the eq template function\).

```go
type StringEqualFold string
```


```go

```

```go

```

<a name="OnceMore"></a>
## type [OnceMore](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hsync/oncemore.go#L27-L31>)

OnceMore is similar to sync.Once.

Additional features are: \* it can be reset, so the action can be repeated if needed \* it has methods to check if it's done or in progress

```go
type OnceMore struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="OnceMore.Do"></a>
### func \(\*OnceMore\) [Do](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hsync/oncemore.go#L33>)

```go

```

```go
func (t *OnceMore) Done() bool
```



<a name="OnceMore.Reset"></a>
### func \(\*OnceMore\) [Reset](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hsync/oncemore.go#L54>)

```go
func (t *OnceMore) Reset()
```



<a name="ValueResetter"></a>
## type [ValueResetter](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hsync/oncemore.go#L60-L63>)



```go
type ValueResetter[T any] struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="OnceMoreValue"></a>
### func [OnceMoreValue](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hsync/oncemore.go#L77>)

```go
func OnceMoreValue[T any](f func(context.Context) T) ValueResetter[T]
```

OnceMoreValue returns a function that invokes f only once and returns the value returned by f. The returned function may be called concurrently.

If f panics, the returned function will panic with the same value on every call.


```go
func (h *HasBytesWriter) Write(p []byte) (n int, err error)
```

<a name="OpenReadSeekCloser"></a>
## type [OpenReadSeekCloser](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hugio/readers.go#L135>)

OpenReadSeekCloser allows setting some other way \(than reading from a filesystem\) to open or create a ReadSeekCloser.

```go
type OpenReadSeekCloser func() (ReadSeekCloser, error)
```

<a name="NewOpenReadSeekCloser"></a>
### func [NewOpenReadSeekCloser](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hugio/readers.go#L126>)

```go
func NewOpenReadSeekCloser(r ReadSeekCloser) OpenReadSeekCloser
```

NewOpenReadSeekCloser creates a new ReadSeekCloser from the given ReadSeeker. The ReadSeeker will be seeked to the beginning before returned.

<a name="PipeReadWriteCloser"></a>
## type [PipeReadWriteCloser](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hugio/writers.go#L92-L95>)

PipeReadWriteCloser is a convenience type to create a pipe with a ReadCloser and a WriteCloser.

```go
type PipeReadWriteCloser struct {
    *io.PipeReader
    *io.PipeWriter
}
```


<a name="ReadSeekCloser"></a>
## type [ReadSeekCloser](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hugio/readers.go#L24-L27>)

ReadSeekCloser is implemented by afero.File. We use this as the common type for content in Resource objects, even for strings.

```go
type ReadSeekCloser interface {
    io.ReadSeeker
    io.Closer
}
```


```go
func NewReadSeekerNoOpCloserFromString(content string) ReadSeekCloser
```

ReadSeekCloserProvider provides a ReadSeekCloser.

```go
type ReadSeekCloserProvider interface {

}
```

```go
type ReadWriteCloser interface {
    io.Reader
    io.Writer

}
```

## type [SizeReader](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hugio/readers.go#L35-L38>)



```go
type SizeReader interface {
    io.Reader
    Sizer
}
```

<a name="ToSizeReader"></a>
### func [ToSizeReader](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hugio/readers.go#L42>)

```go
func ToSizeReader(r io.Reader) (SizeReader, error)
```

ToSizeReader converts the given io.Reader to a SizeReader. Note that if r is not a SizeReader, the entire content will be read into memory

<a name="Sizer"></a>
## type [Sizer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hugio/readers.go#L31-L33>)

Sizer provides the size of, typically, a io.Reader. As implemented by e.g. os.File and io.SectionReader.

```go
type Sizer interface {
    Size() int64
}
```

```go
}
```

<a name="EnvironmentDevelopment"></a>

```go
const (
    EnvironmentDevelopment = "development"
    EnvironmentProduction  = "production"
)
```

## Variables

<a name="CurrentVersion"></a>CurrentVersion represents the current build version. This should be the only one.

```go
var CurrentVersion = version.Version{
    Major:      0,
    Minor:      158,
    PatchLevel: 0,
    Suffix:     "-DEV",
}
```

<a name="DartSassBinaryName"></a>DartSassBinaryName is the name of the Dart Sass binary to use. TODO\(bep\) find a better place for this.

```go
var DartSassBinaryName string
```

<a name="IsExtended"></a>

```go
var IsExtended = false
```

<a name="IsWithdeploy"></a>

```go
var IsWithdeploy = false
```

<a name="BuildVersionString"></a>
## func [BuildVersionString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hugo/hugo.go#L368>)

```go
func BuildVersionString() string
```

BuildVersionString creates a version string. This is what you see when running "hugo version".

<a name="Deprecate"></a>
## func [Deprecate](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hugo/hugo.go#L313>)

```go
func Deprecate(item, alternative string, version string)
```

Deprecate informs about a deprecation starting at the given version.

A deprecation typically needs a simple change in the template, but doing so will make the template incompatible with older versions. Theme maintainers generally want 1. No warnings or errors in the console when building a Hugo site. 2. Their theme to work for at least the last few Hugo versions.

<a name="DeprecateLevelMin"></a>
## func [DeprecateLevelMin](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hugo/hugo.go#L325>)

```go
func DeprecateLevelMin(item, alternative string, version string, minLevel logg.Level)
```

DeprecateLevelMin informs about a deprecation starting at the given version, but with a minimum log level.

<a name="DeprecateWithLogger"></a>
## func [DeprecateWithLogger](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hugo/hugo.go#L319>)

```go
func DeprecateWithLogger(item, alternative string, version string, log logg.Logger)
```

See Deprecate for details.

<a name="GetDependencyList"></a>
## func [GetDependencyList](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hugo/hugo.go#L188>)

```go
func GetDependencyList() []string
```

GetDependencyList returns a sorted dependency list on the format package="version". It includes both Go dependencies and \(a manually maintained\) list of C\(\+\+\) dependencies.

<a name="Dependency"></a>
## type [Dependency](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/hugo/hugo.go#L243-L264>)

Dependency is a single dependency, which can be either a Hugo Module or a local theme.

```go
type Dependency struct {
    // Returns the path to this module.
    // This will either be the module path, e.g. "github.com/gohugoio/myshortcodes",
    // or the path below your /theme folder, e.g. "mytheme".
    Path string

    // The module version.
    Version string

    // Whether this dependency is vendored.
    Vendor bool

    // Time version was created.
    Time time.Time

    // In the dependency tree, this is the first module that defines this module
    // as a dependency.
    Owner *Dependency

    // Replaced by this dependency.
    Replace *Dependency
}
```

## Variables

<a name="FieldNameCmd"></a>

```go
var (

    // FieldNameCmd is the name of the field that holds the command name.
    FieldNameCmd = reservedFieldNamePrefix + "_cmd"
    // Used to suppress statements.
    FieldNameStatementID = reservedFieldNamePrefix + "__h_field_statement_id"
)
```

<a name="PanicOnWarningHook"></a>PanicOnWarningHook panics on warnings.

```go
var PanicOnWarningHook = func(e *logg.Entry) error {
    if e.Level != logg.LevelWarn {
        return nil
    }
    panic(e.Message)
}
```

<a name="Logger"></a>
## type [Logger](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/loggers/logger.go#L179-L209>)



```go
type Logger interface {
    Debug() logg.LevelLogger
    DebugCommand(command string) logg.LevelLogger
    Debugf(format string, v ...any)
    Debugln(v ...any)
    Error() logg.LevelLogger
    Errorf(format string, v ...any)
    Erroridf(id, format string, v ...any)
    Errorln(v ...any)
    Errors() string
    Info() logg.LevelLogger
    InfoCommand(command string) logg.LevelLogger
    Infof(format string, v ...any)
    Infoln(v ...any)
    Level() logg.Level
    LoggCount(logg.Level) int
    Logger() logg.Logger
    StdOut() io.Writer
    StdErr() io.Writer
    Printf(format string, v ...any)
    Println(v ...any)
    PrintTimerIfDelayed(start time.Time, name string)
    Reset()
    Warn() logg.LevelLogger
    WarnCommand(command string) logg.LevelLogger
    Warnf(format string, v ...any)
    Warnidf(id, format string, v ...any)
    Warnln(v ...any)
    Deprecatef(fail bool, format string, v ...any)
    Trace(s logg.StringFunc)
}
```

```go
type Options struct {

}
```

# para

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/common/para"
```

Package para implements parallel execution helpers.

## Index

- [type Runner](<#Runner>)
- [type Workers](<#Workers>)
  - [func New\(numWorkers int\) \*Workers](<#New>)
  - [func \(w \*Workers\) Start\(ctx context.Context\) \(Runner, context.Context\)](<#Workers.Start>)


<a name="Runner"></a>
## type [Runner](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/para/para.go#L33-L36>)

Runner wraps the lifecycle methods of a new task set.

Run will block until a worker is available or the context is cancelled, and then run the given func in a new goroutine. Wait will wait for all the running goroutines to finish.

```go
type Runner interface {
    Run(func() error)
    Wait() error
}
```

<a name="Workers"></a>
## type [Workers](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/para/para.go#L24-L26>)

Workers configures a task executor with the most number of tasks to be executed in parallel.

```go
type Workers struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/para/para.go#L59>)

```go
func New(numWorkers int) *Workers
```

New creates a new Workers with the given number of workers.

```go
func (w *Workers) Start(ctx context.Context) (Runner, context.Context)
```

Start starts a new Runner.

## Constants

<a name="FilePathSeparator"></a>FilePathSeparator as defined by os.Separator.

```go
const (
    FilePathSeparator = string(filepath.Separator)
)
```

<a name="AbsPathify"></a>
## func [AbsPathify](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L72>)

```go
func AbsPathify(workingDir, inPath string) string
```

AbsPathify creates an absolute path if given a working dir and a relative path. If already absolute, the path is just cleaned.

<a name="AddContextRoot"></a>
## func [AddContextRoot](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/url.go#L90>)

```go
func AddContextRoot(baseURL, relativePath string) string
```

AddContextRoot adds the context root to an URL if it's not already set. For relative URL entries on sites with a base url with a context root set \(i.e. http://example.com/mysite\), relative URLs must not include the context root if canonifyURLs is enabled. But if it's disabled, it must be set.

<a name="AddLeadingAndTrailingSlash"></a>
## func [AddLeadingAndTrailingSlash](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L99>)

```go
func AddLeadingAndTrailingSlash(path string) string
```

AddTrailingAndLeadingSlash adds a leading and trailing Unix styled slash \(/\) if not already there.

<a name="AddLeadingSlash"></a>
## func [AddLeadingSlash](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L90>)

```go
func AddLeadingSlash(path string) string
```

AddLeadingSlash adds a leading Unix styled slash \(/\) if not already there.

<a name="AddTrailingSlash"></a>
## func [AddTrailingSlash](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L81>)

```go
func AddTrailingSlash(path string) string
```

AddTrailingSlash adds a trailing Unix styled slash \(/\) if not already there.

<a name="CommonDirPath"></a>
## func [CommonDirPath](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L241>)

```go
func CommonDirPath(path1, path2 string) string
```

CommonDirPath returns the common directory of the given paths.

<a name="Dir"></a>
## func [Dir](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L358>)

```go
func Dir(s string) string
```

Dir behaves like path.Dir without the path.Clean step.

```
The returned path ends in a slash only if it is the root "/".
```

<a name="Ext"></a>
## func [Ext](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L131>)

```go
func Ext(in string) string
```

Ext takes a path and returns the extension, including the delimiter, i.e. ".md".

<a name="ExtNoDelimiter"></a>
## func [ExtNoDelimiter](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L126>)

```go
func ExtNoDelimiter(in string) string
```

ExtNoDelimiter takes a path and returns the extension, excluding the delimiter, i.e. "md".

<a name="FieldsSlash"></a>
## func [FieldsSlash](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L367>)

```go
func FieldsSlash(s string) []string
```

FieldsSlash cuts s into fields separated with '/'.

<a name="FileAndExt"></a>
## func [FileAndExt](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L143>)

```go
func FileAndExt(in string) (string, string)
```

FileAndExt takes a path and returns the file and extension separated, the extension including the delimiter, i.e. ".md".

<a name="FileAndExtNoDelimiter"></a>
## func [FileAndExtNoDelimiter](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L149>)

```go
func FileAndExtNoDelimiter(in string) (string, string)
```

FileAndExtNoDelimiter takes a path and returns the file and extension separated, the extension excluding the delimiter, e.g "md".

<a name="Filename"></a>
## func [Filename](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L156>)

```go
func Filename(in string) (name string)
```

Filename takes a file path, strips out the extension, and returns the name of the file.

<a name="GetRelativePath"></a>
## func [GetRelativePath](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L205>)

```go
func GetRelativePath(path, base string) (final string, err error)
```

GetRelativePath returns the relative path of a given path.

<a name="HasExt"></a>
## func [HasExt](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L856>)

```go
func HasExt(p string) bool
```

HasExt returns true if the Unix styled path has an extension.

<a name="IsSameFilePath"></a>
## func [IsSameFilePath](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L428>)

```go
func IsSameFilePath(s1, s2 string) bool
```

IsSameFilePath checks if s1 and s2 are the same file path.

<a name="MakePermalink"></a>
## func [MakePermalink](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/url.go#L59>)

```go
func MakePermalink(host, plink string) *url.URL
```

MakePermalink combines base URL with content path to create full URL paths. Example

```
base:   http://spf13.com/
path:   post/how-i-blog
result: http://spf13.com/post/how-i-blog
```

<a name="MakeTitle"></a>
## func [MakeTitle](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L105>)

```go
func MakeTitle(inpath string) string
```

MakeTitle converts the path given to a suitable title, trimming whitespace and replacing hyphens with whitespace.

<a name="ModifyPathBundleTypeResource"></a>
## func [ModifyPathBundleTypeResource](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L386>)

```go
func ModifyPathBundleTypeResource(p *Path)
```



<a name="NormalizePathStringBasic"></a>
## func [NormalizePathStringBasic](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L75>)

```go
func NormalizePathStringBasic(s string) string
```

NormalizePathString returns a normalized path string using the very basic Hugo rules.

<a name="PathAndExt"></a>
## func [PathAndExt](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L137>)

```go
func PathAndExt(in string) (string, string)
```

PathAndExt is the same as FileAndExt, but it uses the path package.

<a name="PathEscape"></a>
## func [PathEscape](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L388>)

```go
func PathEscape(pth string) string
```

PathEscape escapes unicode letters in pth. Use URLEscape to escape full URLs including scheme, query etc. This is slightly faster for the common case. Note, there is a url.PathEscape function, but that also escapes /.

<a name="PrettifyURL"></a>
## func [PrettifyURL](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/url.go#L108>)

```go
func PrettifyURL(in string) string
```

PrettifyURL takes a URL string and returns a semantic, clean URL.

<a name="PrettifyURLPath"></a>
## func [PrettifyURLPath](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/url.go#L128>)

```go
func PrettifyURLPath(in string) string
```

PrettifyURLPath takes a URL path to a content and converts it to enable pretty URLs.

```
/section/name.html       becomes /section/name/index.html
/section/name/           becomes /section/name/index.html
/section/name/index.html becomes /section/name/index.html
```

<a name="ReplaceExtension"></a>
## func [ReplaceExtension](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L111>)

```go
func ReplaceExtension(path string, newExt string) string
```

ReplaceExtension takes a path and an extension, strips the old extension and returns the path with the new extension.

<a name="Sanitize"></a>
## func [Sanitize](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L282>)

```go
func Sanitize(s string) string
```

Sanitize sanitizes string to be used in Hugo's file paths and URLs, allowing only a predefined set of special Unicode characters.

Spaces will be replaced with a single hyphen.

This function is the core function used to normalize paths in Hugo.

Note that this is the first common step for URL/path sanitation, the final URL/path may end up looking differently if the user has stricter rules defined \(e.g. removePathAccents=true\).

<a name="ToSlashPreserveLeading"></a>
## func [ToSlashPreserveLeading](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L423>)

```go
func ToSlashPreserveLeading(s string) string
```

ToSlashPreserveLeading converts the path given to a forward slash separated path and preserves the leading slash if present trimming any trailing slash.

<a name="ToSlashTrim"></a>
## func [ToSlashTrim](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L417>)

```go
func ToSlashTrim(s string) string
```

ToSlashTrim trims any leading and trailing slashes from the given string and converts it to a forward slash separated path.

<a name="ToSlashTrimLeading"></a>
## func [ToSlashTrimLeading](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L397>)

```go
func ToSlashTrimLeading(s string) string
```

ToSlashTrimLeading is just a filepath.ToSlash with an added / prefix trimmer.

<a name="ToSlashTrimTrailing"></a>
## func [ToSlashTrimTrailing](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L407>)

```go
func ToSlashTrimTrailing(s string) string
```

ToSlashTrimTrailing is just a filepath.ToSlash with an added / suffix trimmer.

<a name="TrimExt"></a>
## func [TrimExt](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/url.go#L174>)

```go
func TrimExt(in string) string
```

TrimExt trims the extension from a path..

<a name="TrimLeading"></a>
## func [TrimLeading](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L402>)

```go
func TrimLeading(s string) string
```

TrimLeading trims the leading slash from the given string.

<a name="TrimTrailing"></a>
## func [TrimTrailing](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L412>)

```go
func TrimTrailing(s string) string
```

TrimTrailing trims the trailing slash from the given string.

<a name="URLEscape"></a>
## func [URLEscape](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/url.go#L164>)

```go
func URLEscape(uri string) string
```

URLEscape escapes unicode letters.

<a name="Uglify"></a>
## func [Uglify](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/url.go#L137>)

```go
func Uglify(in string) string
```

Uglify does the opposite of PrettifyURLPath\(\).

```
/section/name/index.html becomes /section/name.html
/section/name/           becomes /section/name.html
/section/name.html       becomes /section/name.html
```

<a name="UrlFromFilename"></a>
## func [UrlFromFilename](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/url.go#L179>)

```go
func UrlFromFilename(filename string) (*url.URL, error)
```

From https://github.com/golang/go/blob/e0c76d95abfc1621259864adb3d101cf6f1f90fc/src/cmd/go/internal/web/url.go#L45

<a name="UrlStringToFilename"></a>
## func [UrlStringToFilename](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/url.go#L233>)

```go
func UrlStringToFilename(s string) (string, bool)
```

UrlStringToFilename converts the URL s to a filename. If ParseRequestURI fails, the input is just converted to OS specific slashes and returned.

<a name="ValidateIdentifier"></a>
## func [ValidateIdentifier](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L870>)

```go
func ValidateIdentifier(s string) error
```

ValidateIdentifier returns true if the given string is a valid identifier according to Hugo's basic path normalization rules.

<a name="DirFile"></a>
## type [DirFile](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L373-L376>)

DirFile holds the result from path.Split.

```go
type DirFile struct {
    Dir  string
    File string
}
```

<a name="DirFile.String"></a>
### func \(DirFile\) [String](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/path.go#L379>)

```go
func (df DirFile) String() string
```

Used in test.

<a name="Path"></a>
## type [Path](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L428-L453>)



```go
type Path struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Path.Base"></a>
### func \(\*Path\) [Base](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L704>)

```go
func (p *Path) Base() string
```

For content files, Base returns the path without any identifiers \(extension, language code etc.\). Any 'index' as the last path element is ignored.

For other files \(Resources\), any extension is kept.

<a name="Path.BaseNameNoIdentifier"></a>
### func \(\*Path\) [BaseNameNoIdentifier](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L589>)

```go
func (p *Path) BaseNameNoIdentifier() string
```

BaseNameNoIdentifier returns the logical base name for a resource without any identifier \(e.g. no extension\). For bundles this will be the containing directory's name, e.g. "blog".

<a name="Path.BaseNoLeadingSlash"></a>
### func \(\*Path\) [BaseNoLeadingSlash](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L729>)

```go
func (p *Path) BaseNoLeadingSlash() string
```

BaseNoLeadingSlash returns the base path without the leading slash.

<a name="Path.BaseReTyped"></a>
### func \(\*Path\) [BaseReTyped](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L715>)

```go
func (p *Path) BaseReTyped(typ string) (d string)
```

Used in template lookups. For pages with Type set, we treat that as the section.

<a name="Path.BaseRel"></a>
### func \(\*Path\) [BaseRel](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L692>)

```go
func (p *Path) BaseRel(owner *Path) string
```

BaseRel returns the base path relative to the given owner.

<a name="Path.Component"></a>
### func \(\*Path\) [Component](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L513>)

```go
func (p *Path) Component() string
```

Component returns the component for this path \(e.g. "content"\).

<a name="Path.Container"></a>
### func \(\*Path\) [Container](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L518>)

```go
func (p *Path) Container() string
```

Container returns the base name of the container directory for this path.

<a name="Path.ContainerDir"></a>
### func \(\*Path\) [ContainerDir](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L534>)

```go
func (p *Path) ContainerDir() string
```

ContainerDir returns the container directory for this path. For content bundles this will be the parent directory.

<a name="Path.Custom"></a>
### func \(\*Path\) [Custom](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L785>)

```go
func (p *Path) Custom() string
```

```go
func (p *Path) Dir() (d string)
```

Dir returns all but the last element of path, typically the path's directory.


```go

```

```go
func (p *Path) NameNoExt() string
```


NameNoIdentifier returns the last element of path without any identifier \(e.g. no extension\).



```go
func (p *Path) Path() (d string)
```

Path returns the full path.

<a name="Path.PathBeforeLangAndOutputFormatAndExt"></a>
### func \(\*Path\) [PathBeforeLangAndOutputFormatAndExt](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L661>)

```go
func (p *Path) PathBeforeLangAndOutputFormatAndExt() string
```

PathBeforeLangAndOutputFormatAndExt returns the path up to the first identifier that is not a language or output format.

<a name="Path.PathNoIdentifier"></a>
### func \(\*Path\) [PathNoIdentifier](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L656>)

```go
func (p *Path) PathNoIdentifier() string
```

PathNoIdentifier returns the Path but with any identifier \(ext, lang\) removed.

<a name="Path.PathNoLang"></a>


PathNoLang returns the Path but with any language identifier removed.

```go
func (p *Path) PathNoLeadingSlash() string
```

<a name="PathParser"></a>
## type [PathParser](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L46-L66>)

PathParser parses and manages paths.

```go
type PathParser struct {
    // Maps the language code to its index in the languages/sites slice.
    LanguageIndex map[string]int

    // Reports whether the given language is disabled.
    IsLangDisabled func(string) bool

    // IsOutputFormat reports whether the given name is a valid output format.
    // The second argument is optional.
    IsOutputFormat func(name, ext string) bool

    // Reports whether the given ext is a content file.
    IsContentExt func(string) bool

    // The configured sites matrix.
    ConfiguredDimensions *sitesmatrix.ConfiguredDimensions
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="PathParser.Parse"></a>
### func \(\*PathParser\) [Parse](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L137>)

```go
func (pp *PathParser) Parse(c, s string) *Path
```

Parse parses component c with path s into Path using Hugo's content path rules.

<a name="PathParser.ParseBaseAndBaseNameNoIdentifier"></a>
### func \(\*PathParser\) [ParseBaseAndBaseNameNoIdentifier](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L119>)

```go
func (pp *PathParser) ParseBaseAndBaseNameNoIdentifier(c, s string) (string, string)
```

ParseBaseAndBaseNameNoIdentifier parses component c with path s into a base and a base name without any identifier.

<a name="PathParser.ParseIdentity"></a>
### func \(\*PathParser\) [ParseIdentity](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L112>)

```go
func (pp *PathParser) ParseIdentity(c, s string) identity.StringIdentity
```

ParseIdentity parses component c with path s into a StringIdentity.

<a name="PathParser.SitesMatrixFromPath"></a>
### func \(\*PathParser\) [SitesMatrixFromPath](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L85>)

```go
func (pp *PathParser) SitesMatrixFromPath(p *Path) sitesmatrix.VectorStore
```



<a name="Type"></a>
## type [Type](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/paths/pathparser.go#L396>)



```go
type Type int
```

<a name="TypeFile"></a>

```go
const (

    // A generic file, e.g. a JSON file.
    TypeFile Type = iota

    // All below are content files.
    // A resource of a content type with front matter.
    TypeContentResource

    // E.g. /blog/my-post.md
    TypeContentSingle

    // Leaf bundles, e.g. /blog/my-post/index.md
    TypeLeaf

    // Branch bundles, e.g. /blog/_index.md
    TypeBranch

    // Content data file, _content.gotmpl.
    TypeContentData

    // Layout types.
    TypeMarkup
    TypeShortcode
    TypePartial
    TypeBaseof
)
```

```go
func (i Type) String() string
```

```go
var (

```

<a name="BoolMatch"></a>
## type [BoolMatch](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L36>)

BoolMatch is a simple Match implementation based on a boolean value.

```go
type BoolMatch bool
```

### func \(BoolMatch\) [OK](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L38>)

```go
func (b BoolMatch) OK() bool
```



<a name="IndexMatcher"></a>
## type [IndexMatcher](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L299-L301>)



```go
type IndexMatcher interface {
    IndexMatch(match P[IndexString]) (iter.Seq[int], error)
}
```

<a name="IndexString"></a>
## type [IndexString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L294-L297>)



```go
type IndexString struct {
    Index  int
    String string
}
```

<a name="Match"></a>
## type [Match](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L25-L27>)

Match represents the result of a predicate evaluation.

```go
type Match interface {
    OK() bool
}
```

<a name="P"></a>
## type [P](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L52>)

P is a predicate function that tests whether a value of type T satisfies some condition.

```go
type P[T any] func(T) bool
```

<a name="NewIndexStringPredicateFromGlobsAndRanges"></a>
### func [NewIndexStringPredicateFromGlobsAndRanges](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L222>)

```go
func NewIndexStringPredicateFromGlobsAndRanges(patterns []string, getIndex func(s string) int, getGlob func(pattern string) (glob.Glob, error)) (P[IndexString], error)
```

NewIndexStringPredicateFromGlobsAndRanges creates an IndexString predicate from the given glob patterns and range patterns. A glob pattern starting with "\!" is a negation pattern which will be ANDed with the rest. A range pattern is one of "\> value", "\>= value", "\< value" or "\<= value".

<a name="NewStringPredicateFromGlobs"></a>
### func [NewStringPredicateFromGlobs](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L187>)

```go
func NewStringPredicateFromGlobs(patterns []string, getGlob func(pattern string) (glob.Glob, error)) (P[string], error)
```

NewStringPredicateFromGlobs creates a string predicate from the given glob patterns. A glob pattern starting with "\!" is a negation pattern which will be ANDed with the rest.

<a name="P[T].Or"></a>
### func \(P\[T\]\) [Or](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L56>)

```go
func (p P[T]) Or(ps ...P[T]) P[T]
```

Or returns a predicate that is a short\-circuiting logical OR of this and the given predicates. Note that P\[T\] only supports Or. For chained AND/OR logic, use PR\[T\].

<a name="PR"></a>
## type [PR](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L71>)

PR is a predicate function that tests whether a value of type T satisfies some condition and returns a Match result.

```go
type PR[T any] func(T) Match
```

<a name="PR[T].And"></a>
### func \(PR\[T\]\) [And](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L84>)

```go
func (p PR[T]) And(ps ...PR[T]) PR[T]
```

And returns a predicate that is a short\-circuiting logical AND of this and the given predicates.

<a name="PR[T].BoolFunc"></a>
### func \(PR\[T\]\) [BoolFunc](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L74>)

```go
func (p PR[T]) BoolFunc() P[T]
```

BoolFunc returns a P\[T\] version of this predicate.

<a name="PR[T].Filter"></a>
### func \(PR\[T\]\) [Filter](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L134>)

```go
func (p PR[T]) Filter(s []T) []T
```

Filter returns a new slice holding only the elements of s that satisfy p. Filter modifies the contents of the slice s and returns the modified slice, which may have a smaller length.

<a name="PR[T].FilterCopy"></a>
### func \(PR\[T\]\) [FilterCopy](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L146>)

```go
func (p PR[T]) FilterCopy(s []T) []T
```

FilterCopy returns a new slice holding only the elements of s that satisfy p.

<a name="PR[T].Or"></a>
### func \(PR\[T\]\) [Or](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/predicate/predicate.go#L103>)

```go
func (p PR[T]) Or(ps ...PR[T]) PR[T]
```

Or returns a predicate that is a short\-circuiting logical OR of this and the given predicates.

<a name="Group"></a>
## type [Group](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/rungroup/rungroup.go#L24-L27>)

```go
type Group[T any] interface {

    Wait() error
}
```

<a name="Func"></a>

```go
type Func struct {
    // The shortest interval between each run.
    IntervalLow time.Duration

    // The longest interval between each run.
    IntervalHigh time.Duration

    // The function to run.
    F   func(interval time.Duration) (time.Duration, error)

}
```

<a name="RunEvery"></a>
## type [RunEvery](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/tasks/tasks.go#L23-L37>)

RunEvery runs a function at intervals defined by the function itself. Functions can be added and removed while running.

```go
type RunEvery struct {
    // Any error returned from the function will be passed to this function.
    HandleError func(string, error)

    // If set, the function will be run immediately.
    RunImmediately bool
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```


<a name="Position"></a>
## type [Position](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/text/position.go#L33-L38>)

Position holds a source position in a text file or stream.

```go
type Position struct {
    Filename     string // filename, if any
    Offset       int    // byte offset, starting at 0. It's set to -1 if not provided.
    LineNumber   int    // line number, starting at 1
    ColumnNumber int    // column number, starting at 1 (character count per line)
}
```


```go

```



<a name="Positioner"></a>
## type [Positioner](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/text/position.go#L26-L30>)

Positioner represents a thing that knows its position in a text file or stream, typically an error.

```go
type Positioner interface {
    // Position returns the current position.
    // Useful in error logging, e.g. {{ errorf "error in code block: %s" .Position }}.
    Position() Position
}
```

<a name="EvictingQueue"></a>
## type [EvictingQueue](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/types/evictingqueue.go#L25-L31>)

EvictingQueue is a queue which automatically evicts elements from the head of the queue when attempting to add new elements onto the queue and it is full. This queue orders elements LIFO \(last\-in\-first\-out\). It throws away duplicates.

```go
type EvictingQueue[T comparable] struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewEvictingQueue"></a>
### func [NewEvictingQueue](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/types/evictingqueue.go#L34>)

```go
func NewEvictingQueue[T comparable](size int) *EvictingQueue[T]
```

NewEvictingQueue creates a new queue with the given size.

<a name="EvictingQueue[T].Add"></a>
### func \(\*EvictingQueue\[T\]\) [Add](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/types/evictingqueue.go#L39>)

```go
func (q *EvictingQueue[T]) Add(v T) *EvictingQueue[T]
```

Add adds a new string to the tail of the queue if it's not already there.

<a name="EvictingQueue[T].Contains"></a>
### func \(\*EvictingQueue\[T\]\) [Contains](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/types/evictingqueue.go#L68>)

```go
func (q *EvictingQueue[T]) Contains(v T) bool
```

Contains returns whether the queue contains v.

<a name="EvictingQueue[T].Len"></a>
### func \(\*EvictingQueue\[T\]\) [Len](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/types/evictingqueue.go#L58>)

```go
func (q *EvictingQueue[T]) Len() int
```



<a name="EvictingQueue[T].Peek"></a>
### func \(\*EvictingQueue\[T\]\) [Peek](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/types/evictingqueue.go#L78>)

```go
func (q *EvictingQueue[T]) Peek() T
```

Peek looks at the last element added to the queue.

<a name="EvictingQueue[T].PeekAll"></a>
### func \(\*EvictingQueue\[T\]\) [PeekAll](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/types/evictingqueue.go#L91>)

```go
func (q *EvictingQueue[T]) PeekAll() []T
```

PeekAll looks at all the elements in the queue, with the newest first.

<a name="EvictingQueue[T].PeekAllSet"></a>
### func \(\*EvictingQueue\[T\]\) [PeekAllSet](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/types/evictingqueue.go#L106>)

```go
func (q *EvictingQueue[T]) PeekAllSet() map[T]bool
```

PeekAllSet returns PeekAll as a set.

Unwrapper is implemented by types that can unwrap themselves.

```go
type Unwrapper interface {
    // Unwrapv is for internal use only.
    // It got its slightly odd name to prevent collisions with user types.
    Unwrapv() any
}
```

<a name="Weight0Provider"></a>
## type [Weight0Provider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/types/types.go#L149-L151>)

Weight0Provider provides a weight that's considered before the WeightProvider in sorting. This allows the weight set on a given term to win.

```go
type Weight0Provider interface {
    Weight0() int
}
```

<a name="WeightProvider"></a>
## type [WeightProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/types/types.go#L143-L145>)

WeightProvider provides a weight.

```go
type WeightProvider interface {
    Weight() int
}
```

<a name="Zeroer"></a>
## type [Zeroer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/types/types.go#L80-L82>)

Zeroer, as implemented by time.Time, will be used by the truth template funcs in Hugo \(if, with, not, and, or\).

```go
type Zeroer interface {
    IsZero() bool
}
```

A BaseURL in Hugo is normally on the form scheme://path, but the form scheme: is also valid \(mailto:hugo@rules.com\).

```go
    WithoutPath             string
    BasePath                string
    BasePathNoTrailingSlash string

}
```

<a name="RefLinker"></a>
## type [RefLinker](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/urls/ref.go#L19-L22>)

RefLinker is implemented by those who support reference linking. args must contain a path, but can also point to the target language or output format.

```go
type RefLinker interface {
    Ref(args map[string]any) (string, error)
    RelRef(args map[string]any) (string, error)
}
```

<a name="Version"></a>
## type [Version](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/common/version/version.go#L29-L43>)

Version represents the Hugo build version.

```go
type Version struct {
    Major int

    Minor int

    // Increment this for bug releases
    PatchLevel int

    // HugoVersionSuffix is the suffix used in the Hugo version string.
    // It will be blank for release versions.
    Suffix string
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

# allconfig

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/config/allconfig"
```

Package allconfig contains the full configuration for Hugo. \<docsmeta\>\{ "name": "Configuration", "description": "This section holds all configuration options in Hugo." \}\</docsmeta\>
