## Variables

<a name="Default"></a>Default is a Decoder in its default configuration.

```go
var Default = Decoder{
    Delimiter:  ',',
    TargetType: "slice",
}
```

<a name="MarshalYAML"></a>MarshalYAML marshals the given value to YAML.

```go
var MarshalYAML = func(v any) ([]byte, error) {
    return yaml.MarshalWithOptions(v, yamlEncodeOptions...)
}
```

<a name="UnmarshalYaml"></a>
## func [UnmarshalYaml](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/metadecoders/decoder.go#L167>)

```go
func UnmarshalYaml(data []byte, v any) error
```

UnmarshalYaml unmarshals data in YAML format into v.

<a name="Decoder"></a>
## type [Decoder](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/metadecoders/decoder.go#L39-L60>)

Decoder provides some configuration options for the decoders.

```go
type Decoder struct {
    // Format specifies a specific format to decode from. If empty or
    // unspecified, it's inferred from the contents or the filename.
    Format string

    // Delimiter is the field delimiter. Used in the CSV decoder. Default is
    // ','.
    Delimiter rune

    // Comment, if not 0, is the comment character. Lines beginning with the
    // Comment character without preceding whitespace are ignored. Used in the
    // CSV decoder.
    Comment rune

    // If true, a quote may appear in an unquoted field and a non-doubled quote
    // may appear in a quoted field. Used in the CSV decoder. Default is false.
    LazyQuotes bool

    // The target data type, either slice or map. Used in the CSV decoder.
    // Default is slice.
    TargetType string
}
```

<a name="Decoder.FormatFromContentString"></a>
### func \(Decoder\) [FormatFromContentString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/metadecoders/format.go#L73>)

```go
func (d Decoder) FormatFromContentString(data string) Format
```

FormatFromContentString tries to detect the format \(JSON, YAML, TOML or XML\) in the given string. It return an empty string if no format could be detected.

<a name="Decoder.OptionsKey"></a>
### func \(Decoder\) [OptionsKey](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/metadecoders/decoder.go#L63>)

```go
func (d Decoder) OptionsKey() string
```

OptionsKey is used in cache keys.

<a name="Decoder.Unmarshal"></a>
### func \(Decoder\) [Unmarshal](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/metadecoders/decoder.go#L144>)

```go
func (d Decoder) Unmarshal(data []byte, f Format) (any, error)
```

Unmarshal will unmarshall data in format f into an interface\{\}. This is what's needed for Hugo's /data handling.

<a name="Decoder.UnmarshalFileToMap"></a>
### func \(Decoder\) [UnmarshalFileToMap](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/metadecoders/decoder.go#L103>)

```go
func (d Decoder) UnmarshalFileToMap(fs afero.Fs, filename string) (map[string]any, error)
```

UnmarshalFileToMap is the same as UnmarshalToMap, but reads the data from the given filename.

<a name="Decoder.UnmarshalStringTo"></a>
### func \(Decoder\) [UnmarshalStringTo](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/metadecoders/decoder.go#L117>)

```go
func (d Decoder) UnmarshalStringTo(data string, typ any) (any, error)
```

UnmarshalStringTo tries to unmarshal data to a new instance of type typ.

<a name="Decoder.UnmarshalTo"></a>
### func \(Decoder\) [UnmarshalTo](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/metadecoders/decoder.go#L278>)

```go
func (d Decoder) UnmarshalTo(data []byte, f Format, v any) error
```

UnmarshalTo unmarshals data in format f into v.

<a name="Decoder.UnmarshalToMap"></a>
### func \(Decoder\) [UnmarshalToMap](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/metadecoders/decoder.go#L81>)

```go
func (d Decoder) UnmarshalToMap(data []byte, f Format) (map[string]any, error)
```

UnmarshalToMap will unmarshall data in format f into a new map. This is what's needed for Hugo's front matter decoding.

<a name="Format"></a>
## type [Format](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/metadecoders/format.go#L21>)



```go
type Format string
```

<a name="ORG"></a>

```go
const (
    // These are the supported metadata  formats in Hugo. Most of these are also
    // supported as /data formats.
    ORG  Format = "org"
    JSON Format = "json"
    TOML Format = "toml"
    YAML Format = "yaml"
    CSV  Format = "csv"
    XML  Format = "xml"
)
```

<a name="FormatFromString"></a>
### func [FormatFromString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/metadecoders/format.go#L46>)

```go
func FormatFromString(formatStr string) Format
```

FormatFromString turns formatStr, typically a file extension without any ".", into a Format. It returns an empty string for unknown formats.

<a name="FormatFromStrings"></a>
### func [FormatFromStrings](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/metadecoders/format.go#L35>)

```go
func FormatFromStrings(ss ...string) Format
```

FormatFromStrings returns the first non\-empty Format from the given strings.

# pageparser

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/parser/pageparser"
```

Package pageparser provides a parser for Hugo content files \(Markdown, HTML etc.\) in Hugo. This implementation is highly inspired by the great talk given by Rob Pike called "Lexical Scanning in Go" It's on YouTube, Google it\!. See slides here: https://go.dev/talks/2011/lex.slide#1

## Constants

<a name="TypeLeadSummaryDivider"></a>

```go
const (

    // page items
    TypeLeadSummaryDivider // <!--more-->,  # more
    TypeFrontMatterYAML
    TypeFrontMatterTOML
    TypeFrontMatterJSON
    TypeFrontMatterORG
    TypeIgnore // // The BOM Unicode byte order marker and possibly others

)
```

<a name="FormatFromFrontMatterType"></a>
## func [FormatFromFrontMatterType](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L94>)

```go
func FormatFromFrontMatterType(typ ItemType) metadecoders.Format
```



<a name="HasShortcode"></a>
## func [HasShortcode](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L255>)

```go
func HasShortcode(s string) bool
```

HasShortcode returns true if the given string contains a shortcode.

<a name="IsProbablySourceOfItems"></a>
## func [IsProbablySourceOfItems](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L215>)

```go
func IsProbablySourceOfItems(source []byte, items Items) bool
```

IsProbablySourceOfItems returns true if the given source looks like original source of the items. There may be some false positives, but that is highly unlikely and good enough for the planned purpose. It will also return false if the last item is not EOF \(error situations\) and true if both source and items are empty.

<a name="Iterator"></a>
## type [Iterator](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L135-L138>)

An Iterator has methods to iterate a parsed page with support going back if needed.

```go
type Iterator struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewIterator"></a>
### func [NewIterator](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L129>)

```go
func NewIterator(items Items) *Iterator
```

NewIterator creates a new Iterator.

<a name="Iterator.Backup"></a>
### func \(\*Iterator\) [Backup](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L157>)

```go
func (t *Iterator) Backup()
```

backs up one token.

<a name="Iterator.Consume"></a>
### func \(\*Iterator\) [Consume](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L194>)

```go
func (t *Iterator) Consume(cnt int)
```

Consume is a convenience method to consume the next n tokens, but back off Errors and EOF.

<a name="Iterator.Current"></a>
### func \(\*Iterator\) [Current](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L149>)

```go
func (t *Iterator) Current() Item
```

Current will repeatably return the current item.

<a name="Iterator.IsValueNext"></a>
### func \(\*Iterator\) [IsValueNext](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L170>)

```go
func (t *Iterator) IsValueNext() bool
```

check for non\-error and non\-EOF types coming next

<a name="Iterator.LineNumber"></a>
### func \(\*Iterator\) [LineNumber](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L205>)

```go
func (t *Iterator) LineNumber(source []byte) int
```

LineNumber returns the current line number. Used for logging.

<a name="Iterator.Next"></a>
### func \(\*Iterator\) [Next](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L141>)

```go
func (t *Iterator) Next() Item
```

consumes and returns the next item

<a name="Iterator.Peek"></a>
### func \(\*Iterator\) [Peek](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L177>)

```go
func (t *Iterator) Peek() Item
```

look at, but do not consume, the next item repeated, sequential calls will return the same item

<a name="Iterator.PeekWalk"></a>
### func \(\*Iterator\) [PeekWalk](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L183>)

```go
func (t *Iterator) PeekWalk(walkFn func(item Item) bool)
```

PeekWalk will feed the next items in the iterator to walkFn until it returns false.

<a name="Iterator.Pos"></a>
### func \(\*Iterator\) [Pos](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L165>)

```go
func (t *Iterator) Pos() int
```

Pos returns the current position in the input.

<a name="Result"></a>
## type [Result](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L28-L33>)

Result holds the parse result.

```go
type Result interface {
    // Iterator returns a new Iterator positioned at the beginning of the parse tree.
    Iterator() *Iterator
    // Input returns the input to Parse.
    Input() []byte
}
```

<a name="ParseMain"></a>
### func [ParseMain](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/parser/pageparser/pageparser.go#L110>)

```go
func ParseMain(r io.Reader, cfg Config) (Result, error)
```

ParseMain parses starting with the main section. Used in tests.

## Constants

<a name="ActionResize"></a>

```go
const (
    ActionResize = "resize"
    ActionCrop   = "crop"
    ActionFit    = "fit"
    ActionFill   = "fill"
)
```

<a name="SmartCropAnchor"></a>

```go
const (
    SmartCropAnchor = 1000
)
```

## Variables

<a name="Actions"></a>

```go
var Actions = map[string]bool{
    ActionResize: true,
    ActionCrop:   true,
    ActionFit:    true,
    ActionFill:   true,
}
```

<a name="AddColorToPalette"></a>
## func [AddColorToPalette](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/color.go#L93>)

```go
func AddColorToPalette(c color.Color, p color.Palette) color.Palette
```

AddColorToPalette adds c as the first color in p if not already there. Note that it does no additional checks, so callers must make sure that the palette is valid for the relevant format.

<a name="ColorGoToHexString"></a>
## func [ColorGoToHexString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/color.go#L113>)

```go
func ColorGoToHexString(c color.Color) string
```

ColorGoToHexString converts a color.Color to a hex string.

<a name="DecodeConfig"></a>
## func [DecodeConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/config.go#L197>)

```go
func DecodeConfig(in map[string]any) (*config.ConfigNamespace[ImagingConfig, ImagingConfigInternal], error)
```



<a name="ImageFormatFromMediaSubType"></a>
## func [ImageFormatFromMediaSubType](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/config.go#L152>)

```go
func ImageFormatFromMediaSubType(sub string) (Format, ImageResourceType)
```

ImageFormatFromMediaSubType returns the image format for the given media subtype, and how much image processing operations are supported for this format.

<a name="IsOpaque"></a>
## func [IsOpaque](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/image.go#L493>)

```go
func IsOpaque(img image.Image) bool
```

IsOpaque returns false if the image has alpha channel and there is at least 1 pixel that is not \(fully\) opaque.

<a name="ReplaceColorInPalette"></a>
## func [ReplaceColorInPalette](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/color.go#L108>)

```go
func ReplaceColorInPalette(c color.Color, p color.Palette)
```

ReplaceColorInPalette will replace the color in palette p closest to c in Euclidean R,G,B,A space with c.

<a name="ToFilters"></a>
## func [ToFilters](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/image.go#L474>)

```go
func ToFilters(in any) []gift.Filter
```

ToFilters converts the given input to a slice of gift.Filter.

<a name="UnwrapFilter"></a>
## func [UnwrapFilter](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/image.go#L466>)

```go
func UnwrapFilter(in gift.Filter) gift.Filter
```

UnwrapFilter unwraps the given filter if it is a filter wrapper.

<a name="Decoder"></a>
## type [Decoder](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/codec.go#L40-L43>)

Decoder defines the decoding of an image format. These matches the globalmpackage image functions.

```go
type Decoder interface {
    Decode(r io.Reader) (image.Image, error)
    DecodeConfig(r io.Reader) (image.Config, error)
}
```

<a name="EncodeDecoder"></a>
## type [EncodeDecoder](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/codec.go#L55-L58>)

EncodeDecoder defines both decoding and encoding of an image format as defined by the standard library.

```go
type EncodeDecoder interface {
    Decoder
    Encoder
}
```

<a name="Encoder"></a>
## type [Encoder](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/codec.go#L50-L52>)

EncoderWithOptions defines the encoding of an image format with the given options.

```go
type Encoder interface {
    Encode(w io.Writer, src image.Image, options map[string]any) error
}
```

<a name="ExifConfig"></a>
## type [ExifConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/config.go#L576-L596>)



```go
type ExifConfig struct {
    // Regexp matching the Exif fields you want from the (massive) set of Exif info
    // available. As we cache this info to disk, this is for performance and
    // disk space reasons more than anything.
    // If you want it all, put ".*" in this config setting.
    // Note that if neither this or ExcludeFields is set, Hugo will return a small
    // default set.
    IncludeFields string

    // Regexp matching the Exif fields you want to exclude. This may be easier to use
    // than IncludeFields above, depending on what you want.
    ExcludeFields string

    // Hugo extracts the "photo taken" date/time into .Date by default.
    // Set this to true to turn it off.
    DisableDate bool

    // Hugo extracts the "photo taken where" (GPS latitude and longitude) into
    // .Long and .Lat. Set this to true to turn it off.
    DisableLatLong bool
}
```

<a name="Filters"></a>
## type [Filters](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L35>)



```go
type Filters struct{}
```

<a name="Filters.AutoOrient"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [AutoOrient](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L258>)

```go
func (*Filters) AutoOrient() gift.Filter
```

AutoOrient creates a filter that rotates and flips an image as needed per its EXIF orientation tag.

<a name="Filters.Brightness"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Brightness](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L266>)

```go
func (*Filters) Brightness(percentage any) gift.Filter
```

Brightness creates a filter that changes the brightness of an image. The percentage parameter must be in range \(\-100, 100\).

<a name="Filters.ColorBalance"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [ColorBalance](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L275>)

```go
func (*Filters) ColorBalance(percentageRed, percentageGreen, percentageBlue any) gift.Filter
```

ColorBalance creates a filter that changes the color balance of an image. The percentage parameters for each color channel \(red, green, blue\) must be in range \(\-100, 500\).

<a name="Filters.Colorize"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Colorize](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L286>)

```go
func (*Filters) Colorize(hue, saturation, percentage any) gift.Filter
```

Colorize creates a filter that produces a colorized version of an image. The hue parameter is the angle on the color wheel, typically in range \(0, 360\). The saturation parameter must be in range \(0, 100\). The percentage parameter specifies the strength of the effect, it must be in range \(0, 100\).

<a name="Filters.Contrast"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Contrast](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L295>)

```go
func (*Filters) Contrast(percentage any) gift.Filter
```

Contrast creates a filter that changes the contrast of an image. The percentage parameter must be in range \(\-100, 100\).

<a name="Filters.Dither"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Dither](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L204>)

```go
func (*Filters) Dither(options ...any) gift.Filter
```

Dither creates a filter that dithers an image.

<a name="Filters.Gamma"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Gamma](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L305>)

```go
func (*Filters) Gamma(gamma any) gift.Filter
```

Gamma creates a filter that performs a gamma correction on an image. The gamma parameter must be positive. Gamma = 1 gives the original image. Gamma less than 1 darkens the image and gamma greater than 1 lightens it.

<a name="Filters.GaussianBlur"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [GaussianBlur](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L313>)

```go
func (*Filters) GaussianBlur(sigma any) gift.Filter
```

GaussianBlur creates a filter that applies a gaussian blur to an image.

<a name="Filters.Grayscale"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Grayscale](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L321>)

```go
func (*Filters) Grayscale() gift.Filter
```

Grayscale creates a filter that produces a grayscale version of an image.

<a name="Filters.Hue"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Hue](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L329>)

```go
func (*Filters) Hue(shift any) gift.Filter
```

Hue creates a filter that rotates the hue of an image. The hue angle shift is typically in range \-180 to 180.

<a name="Filters.Invert"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Invert](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L337>)

```go
func (*Filters) Invert() gift.Filter
```

Invert creates a filter that negates the colors of an image.

<a name="Filters.Mask"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Mask](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L57>)

```go
func (*Filters) Mask(mask ImageSource) gift.Filter
```

Mask creates a filter that applies a mask image to the source image.

<a name="Filters.Opacity"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Opacity](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L66>)

```go
func (*Filters) Opacity(opacity any) gift.Filter
```

Opacity creates a filter that changes the opacity of an image. The opacity parameter must be in range \(0, 1\).

<a name="Filters.Overlay"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Overlay](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L49>)

```go
func (*Filters) Overlay(src ImageSource, x, y any) gift.Filter
```

Overlay creates a filter that overlays src at position x y.

<a name="Filters.Padding"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Padding](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L148>)

```go
func (*Filters) Padding(args ...any) gift.Filter
```

Padding creates a filter that resizes the image canvas without resizing the image. The last argument is the canvas color, expressed as an RGB or RGBA hexadecimal color. The default value is \`ffffffff\` \(opaque white\). The preceding arguments are the padding values, in pixels, using the CSS shorthand property syntax. Negative padding values will crop the image. The signature is images.Padding V1 \[V2\] \[V3\] \[V4\] \[COLOR\].

<a name="Filters.Pixelate"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Pixelate](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L344>)

```go
func (*Filters) Pixelate(size any) gift.Filter
```

Pixelate creates a filter that applies a pixelation effect to an image.

<a name="Filters.Process"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Process](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L38>)

```go
func (*Filters) Process(spec any) gift.Filter
```

Process creates a filter that processes an image using the given specification.

<a name="Filters.Saturation"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Saturation](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L352>)

```go
func (*Filters) Saturation(percentage any) gift.Filter
```

Saturation creates a filter that changes the saturation of an image.

<a name="Filters.Sepia"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Sepia](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L360>)

```go
func (*Filters) Sepia(percentage any) gift.Filter
```

Sepia creates a filter that produces a sepia\-toned version of an image.

<a name="Filters.Sigmoid"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Sigmoid](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L369>)

```go
func (*Filters) Sigmoid(midpoint, factor any) gift.Filter
```

Sigmoid creates a filter that changes the contrast of an image using a sigmoidal function and returns the adjusted image. It's a non\-linear contrast change useful for photo adjustments as it preserves highlight and shadow detail.

<a name="Filters.Text"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [Text](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L74>)

```go
func (*Filters) Text(text string, options ...any) gift.Filter
```

Text creates a filter that draws text with the given options.

<a name="Filters.UnsharpMask"></a>
### func \(\*Filters\) [UnsharpMask](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/filters.go#L381>)

```go
func (*Filters) UnsharpMask(sigma, amount, threshold any) gift.Filter
```

UnsharpMask creates a filter that sharpens an image. The sigma parameter is used in a gaussian function and affects the radius of effect. Sigma must be positive. Sharpen radius roughly equals 3 \* sigma. The amount parameter controls how much darker and how much lighter the edge borders become. Typically between 0.5 and 1.5. The threshold parameter controls the minimum brightness change that will be sharpened. Typically between 0 and 0.05.

<a name="Format"></a>
## type [Format](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/image.go#L336>)

Format is an image file format.

```go
type Format int
```

<a name="JPEG"></a>

```go
const (
    JPEG Format = iota + 1
    PNG
    GIF
    TIFF
    BMP
    WEBP

    // Below: We have no encoder/decoder for these, but we can provide metadata support for them (including width/height).
    AVIF
    HEIF
    HEIC
)
```


DefaultExtension returns the default file extension of this format, starting with a dot. For example: .jpg for JPEG

```go
func (f Format) String() string
```



<a name="Format.SupportsAnimation"></a>


<a name="Format.SupportsTransparency"></a>

```go
func (f Format) SupportsTransparency() bool
```

SupportsTransparency reports whether it supports transparency in any form.

<a name="Format.ToImageMetaImageFormatFormat"></a>
### func \(Format\) [ToImageMetaImageFormatFormat](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/image.go#L357>)

```go

```

```go
func (f Format) UseImageMetaConfigDecoder() bool
```

Whether to use imagemeta to decode image config \(width/height \).

<a name="ImageConfig"></a>
## type [ImageConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/config.go#L390-L432>)

ImageConfig holds configuration to create a new image from an existing one, resize etc.

```go
type ImageConfig struct {
    // This defines the output format of the output image. It defaults to the source format.
    TargetFormat Format

    Action string

    // If set, this will be used as the key in filenames etc.
    Key string

    // Quality ranges from 1 to 100 inclusive, higher is better.
    // This is only relevant for JPEG and WEBP images.
    // For WEBP it's only relevant for lossy encoding.
    // Default is 75.
    Quality int

    // Rotate rotates an image by the given angle counter-clockwise.
    // The rotation will be performed first.
    Rotate int

    // Used to fill any transparency.
    // When set in project config, it's used when converting to a format that does
    // not support transparency.
    // When set per image operation, it's used even for formats that does support
    // transparency.
    BgColor color.Color

    // Hint about what type of picture this is. Used to optimize encoding
    // when target is set to webp.
    Hint string

    Compression string

    // WebP-specific options.
    UseSharpYuv bool
    Method      int

    Width  int
    Height int

    Filter gift.Resampling

    Anchor gift.Anchor
}
```

<a name="ImageResource"></a>
## type [ImageResource](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/image_resource.go#L24-L27>)

ImageResource represents an image resource.

```go
type ImageResource interface {
    resource.Resource
    ImageResourceOps
}
```

<a name="ImageResourceOps"></a>
## type [ImageResourceOps](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/image_resource.go#L29-L74>)



```go
type ImageResourceOps interface {
    // Height returns the height of the Image.
    Height() int

    // Width returns the width of the Image.
    Width() int

    // Process applies the given image processing options to the image.
    Process(spec string) (ImageResource, error)

    // Crop an image to match the given dimensions without resizing.
    // You must provide both width and height.
    // Use the anchor option to change the crop box anchor point.
    //    {{ $image := $image.Crop "600x400" }}
    Crop(spec string) (ImageResource, error)

    // Fill scales the image to the smallest possible size that will cover the specified dimensions in spec,
    // crops the resized image to the specified dimensions using the given anchor point.
    // The spec is space delimited, e.g. `200x300 TopLeft`.
    Fill(spec string) (ImageResource, error)

    // Fit scales down the image using the given spec.
    Fit(spec string) (ImageResource, error)

    // Resize resizes the image to the given spec. If one of width or height is 0, the image aspect
    // ratio is preserved.
    Resize(spec string) (ImageResource, error)

    // Filter applies one or more filters to an Image.
    //    {{ $image := $image.Filter (images.GaussianBlur 6) (images.Pixelate 8) }}
    Filter(filters ...any) (ImageResource, error)

    // Exif returns an ExifInfo object containing Image metadata.
    // Deprecated: Use Meta() instead.
    Exif() *meta.ExifInfo

    // Meta returns a MetaInfo object containing Image metadata from all sources (EXIF, IPTC, XMP).
    Meta() *meta.MetaInfo

    // Colors returns a slice of the most dominant colors in an image
    // using a simple histogram method.
    Colors() ([]Color, error)

    // For internal use.
    DecodeImage() (image.Image, error)
}
```

<a name="ImageResourceType"></a>
## type [ImageResourceType](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/config.go#L138>)



```go
type ImageResourceType int
```

<a name="ImageResourceTypeNone"></a>

```go
const (
    // ImageResourceTypeNone means that the resource is not an image, and thus does not support any image operations.
    ImageResourceTypeNone ImageResourceType = iota
    // This is an image, but with no support for any image operations.
    ImageResourceTypeBasic
    // ImageResourceTypeMetaOnly means that only metadata operations (e.g. getting width/height and other metadata) are supported for this format.
    ImageResourceTypeMetaOnly
    // ImageResourceTypeProcessable means that all image operations (resizing, cropping, etc.) are supported for this format.
    ImageResourceTypeProcessable
)
```

ImageSource identifies and decodes an image.

```go
type ImageSource interface {

    Key() string
}
```

<a name="ImagingConfig"></a>
## type [ImagingConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/config.go#L474-L502>)

ImagingConfig contains default image processing configuration. This will be fetched from site \(or language\) config.

```go
type ImagingConfig struct {
    // Default image quality setting (1-100). Only used for JPEG and WebP images.
    Quality int

    // Compression method to use.
    // One of "lossy" or "lossless".
    // Note that lossless is currently only supported for WebP.
    Compression string

    // Resample filter to use in resize operations.
    ResampleFilter string

    // Hint about what type of image this is.
    // Currently only used when encoding to Webp.
    // Default is "photo".
    // Valid values are "picture", "photo", "drawing", "icon", or "text".
    // Moved to WebpConfig in v0.155.0, but kept here for backwards compatibility.
    Hint string `json:"-"`

    // The anchor to use in Fill. Default is "smart", i.e. Smart Crop.
    Anchor string

    // Default color used in fill operations (e.g. "fff" for white).
    BgColor string

    Exif ExifConfig
    Meta MetaConfig
    Webp WebpConfig
}
```

<a name="ImagingConfigInternal"></a>
## type [ImagingConfigInternal](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/config.go#L440-L446>)



```go
type ImagingConfigInternal struct {
    BgColor        color.Color
    ResampleFilter gift.Resampling
    Anchor         gift.Anchor

    Imaging ImagingConfig
}
```

<a name="ImagingConfigInternal.Compile"></a>
### func \(\*ImagingConfigInternal\) [Compile](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/config.go#L448>)

```go
func (i *ImagingConfigInternal) Compile(externalCfg *ImagingConfig) error
```



<a name="MetaConfig"></a>
## type [MetaConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/config.go#L598-L610>)



```go
type MetaConfig struct {
    // Glob patterns for which metadata fields to include.
    // Use "! " prefix to exclude patterns (e.g., "! *GPS*" excludes GPS fields).
    // Patterns are OR'd together for inclusion, AND'd for exclusion.
    // If empty, a default set excluding technical metadata is used.
    // Use ["**"] to include all fields.
    Fields []string

    // Which metadata sources to include.
    // Valid values are "exif", "iptc", "xmp".
    // Default is ["exif", "iptc"] (XMP is excluded for performance reasons).
    Sources []string
}
```

<a name="Spec"></a>
## type [Spec](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/image.go#L330-L333>)



```go
type Spec interface {
    // Loads the image source.
    ReadSeekCloser() (hugio.ReadSeekCloser, error)
}
```

<a name="ToEncoder"></a>
## type [ToEncoder](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/codec.go#L45-L47>)



```go
type ToEncoder interface {
    EncodeTo(conf ImageConfig, w io.Writer, src image.Image) error
}
```

<a name="WebpConfig"></a>
## type [WebpConfig](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/images/config.go#L613-L626>)

WebpConfig holds WebP\-specific encoding configuration.

```go
type WebpConfig struct {
    // Hint about what type of image this is.
    // Valid values are "picture", "photo", "drawing", "icon", or "text".
    // Default is "photo".
    Hint string

    // Use sharp (and slow) RGB->YUV conversion.
    // Default is true.
    UseSharpYuv bool

    // Quality/speed trade-off (0=fast, 6=slower-better).
    // Default is 2.
    Method int
}
```

<a name="ResourcePaths"></a>
## type [ResourcePaths](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/internal/resourcepaths.go#L27-L42>)

ResourcePaths holds path information for a resource. All directories in here have Unix\-style slashes, with leading slash, but no trailing slash. Empty directories are represented with an empty string.

```go
type ResourcePaths struct {
    // This is the directory component for the target file or link.
    Dir string

    // Any base directory for the target file. Will be prepended to Dir.
    BaseDirTarget string

    // This is the directory component for the link will be prepended to Dir.
    BaseDirLink string

    // Set when publishing in a multihost setup.
    TargetBasePaths []string

    // This is the File component, e.g. "data.json".
    File string
}
```

<a name="ResourceTransformationKey"></a>
## type [ResourceTransformationKey](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/internal/key.go#L22-L25>)

ResourceTransformationKey are provided by the different transformation implementations. It identifies the transformation \(name\) and its configuration \(elements\). We combine this in a chain with the rest of the transformations with the target filename and a content hash of the origin to use as cache key.

```go
type ResourceTransformationKey struct {
    Name string
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewResourceTransformationKey"></a>
### func [NewResourceTransformationKey](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/internal/key.go#L30>)

```go
func NewResourceTransformationKey(name string, elements ...any) ResourceTransformationKey
```

NewResourceTransformationKey creates a new ResourceTransformationKey from the transformation name and elements. We will create a 64 bit FNV hash from the elements, which when combined with the other key elements should be unique for all practical applications.

<a name="ResourceTransformationKey.Value"></a>
### func \(ResourceTransformationKey\) [Value](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/internal/key.go#L36>)

```go
func (k ResourceTransformationKey) Value() string
```

Value returns the Key as a string. Do not change this without good reasons.

## Constants

<a name="KindPage"></a>

```go
const (
    KindPage = "page"

    KindHome    = "home"
    KindSection = "section"

    // Note that before Hugo 0.73 these were confusingly named
    // taxonomy (now: term)
    // taxonomyTerm (now: taxonomy)
    KindTaxonomy = "taxonomy"
    KindTerm     = "term"

    // The following are (currently) temporary nodes,
    // i.e. nodes we create just to render in isolation.
    KindTemporary    = "temporary"
    KindRSS          = "rss"
    KindSitemap      = "sitemap"
    KindSitemapIndex = "sitemapindex"
    KindRobotsTXT    = "robotstxt"
    KindStatus404    = "404"
)
```

## Variables

<a name="AllKindsInPages"></a>

```go
var (
    // This is all the kinds we can expect to find in .Site.Pages.
    AllKindsInPages []string
    // This is all the kinds, including the temporary ones.
    AllKinds []string
)
```

GetKindMain gets the page kind given a string, empty if not found. Note that this will not return any temporary kinds \(e.g. robotstxt\).

<a name="SummaryTypeAuto"></a>

```go
const (
    SummaryTypeAuto        = "auto"
    SummaryTypeManual      = "manual"
    SummaryTypeFrontMatter = "frontmatter"
)
```

## Variables

<a name="NopPage"></a>

```go
var (
    NopPage                 Page            = new(nopPage)
    NopContentRenderer      ContentRenderer = new(nopContentRenderer)
    NopMarkup               Markup          = new(nopMarkup)
    NopContent              Content         = new(nopContent)
    NopCPageContentRenderer                 = struct {
        OutputFormatPageContentProvider
        ContentRenderer
    }{
        NopPage,
        NopContentRenderer,
    }
    NilPage *nopPage
)
```

<a name="DefaultPageSort"></a>

```go
var (

    // DefaultPageSort is the default sort func for pages in Hugo:
    // Order by Ordinal, Weight, Date, LinkTitle and then full file path.
    DefaultPageSort = func(p1, p2 Page) bool {
        o1, o2 := getOrdinals(p1, p2)
        if o1 != o2 && o1 != -1 && o2 != -1 {
            return o1 < o2
        }

        w01, w02 := getWeight0s(p1, p2)
        if w01 != w02 && w01 != -1 && w02 != -1 {
            return w01 < w02
        }

        if p1.Weight() == p2.Weight() {
            if p1.Date().Unix() == p2.Date().Unix() {
                c := collatorStringCompare(func(p Page) string { return p.LinkTitle() }, p1, p2)
                if c == 0 {

                    return compare.LessStrings(p1.PathInfo().Path(), p2.PathInfo().Path())
                }
                return c < 0
            }
            return p1.Date().Unix() > p2.Date().Unix()
        }

        if p2.Weight() == 0 {
            return true
        }

        if p1.Weight() == 0 {
            return false
        }

        return p1.Weight() < p2.Weight()
    }
)
```


<a name="AlternativeOutputFormatsProvider"></a>
## type [AlternativeOutputFormatsProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L48-L54>)

AlternativeOutputFormatsProvider provides alternative output formats for a Page.

```go
type AlternativeOutputFormatsProvider interface {
    // AlternativeOutputFormats gives the alternative output formats for the
    // current output.
    // Note that we use the term "alternative" and not "alternate" here, as it
    // does not necessarily replace the other format, it is an alternative representation.
    AlternativeOutputFormats() OutputFormats
}
```

<a name="Author"></a>
## type [Author](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_author.go#L22-L32>)

Author contains details about the author of a page. Deprecated: Use taxonomies instead.

```go
type Author struct {
    GivenName   string
    FamilyName  string
    DisplayName string
    Thumbnail   string
    Image       string
    ShortBio    string
    LongBio     string
    Email       string
    Social      AuthorSocial
}
```

<a name="AuthorList"></a>
## type [AuthorList](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_author.go#L18>)

AuthorList is a list of all authors and their metadata. Deprecated: Use taxonomies instead.

```go
type AuthorList map[string]Author
```

<a name="AuthorSocial"></a>
## type [AuthorSocial](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_author.go#L47>)

AuthorSocial is a place to put social details per author. These are the standard keys that themes will expect to have available, but can be expanded to any others on a per site basis \- website \- github \- facebook \- twitter \- pinterest \- instagram \- youtube \- linkedin \- skype Deprecated: Use taxonomies instead.

```go
type AuthorSocial map[string]string
```

<a name="ChildCareProvider"></a>
## type [ChildCareProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L57-L69>)

ChildCareProvider provides accessors to child resources.

```go
type ChildCareProvider interface {
    // Pages returns a list of pages of all kinds.
    Pages() Pages

    // RegularPages returns a list of pages of kind 'Page'.
    RegularPages() Pages

    // RegularPagesRecursive returns all regular pages below the current
    // section.
    RegularPagesRecursive() Pages

    resource.ResourcesProvider
}
```

<a name="Content"></a>
## type [Content](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_markup.go#L30-L40>)



```go
type Content interface {
    Content(context.Context) (template.HTML, error)
    ContentWithoutSummary(context.Context) (template.HTML, error)
    Summary(context.Context) (Summary, error)
    Plain(context.Context) string
    PlainWords(context.Context) []string
    WordCount(context.Context) int
    FuzzyWordCount(context.Context) int
    ReadingTime(context.Context) int
    Len(context.Context) int
}
```

<a name="ContentProvider"></a>
## type [ContentProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L76-L107>)

ContentProvider provides the content related values for a Page.

```go
type ContentProvider interface {
    Content(context.Context) (any, error)

    // ContentWithoutSummary returns the Page Content stripped of the summary.
    ContentWithoutSummary(ctx context.Context) (template.HTML, error)

    // Plain returns the Page Content stripped of HTML markup.
    Plain(context.Context) string

    // PlainWords returns a string slice from splitting Plain using https://pkg.go.dev/strings#Fields.
    PlainWords(context.Context) []string

    // Summary returns a generated summary of the content.
    // The breakpoint can be set manually by inserting a summary separator in the source file.
    Summary(context.Context) template.HTML

    // Truncated returns whether the Summary  is truncated or not.
    Truncated(context.Context) bool

    // FuzzyWordCount returns the approximate number of words in the content.
    FuzzyWordCount(context.Context) int

    // WordCount returns the number of words in the content.
    WordCount(context.Context) int

    // ReadingTime returns the reading time based on the length of plain text.
    ReadingTime(context.Context) int

    // Len returns the length of the content.
    // This is for internal use only.
    Len(context.Context) int
}
```

<a name="ContentRenderer"></a>
## type [ContentRenderer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L110-L118>)

ContentRenderer provides the content rendering methods for some content.

```go
type ContentRenderer interface {
    // ParseAndRenderContent renders the given content.
    // For internal use only.
    ParseAndRenderContent(ctx context.Context, content []byte, enableTOC bool) (converter.ResultRender, error)
    // For internal use only.
    ParseContent(ctx context.Context, content []byte) (converter.ResultParse, bool, error)
    // For internal use only.
    RenderContent(ctx context.Context, content []byte, doc any) (converter.ResultRender, bool, error)
}
```

<a name="Data"></a>
## type [Data](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_data.go#L24>)

Data represents the .Data element in a Page in Hugo. We make this a type so we can do lazy loading of .Data.Pages

```go
type Data map[string]any
```

<a name="Data.Pages"></a>
### func \(Data\) [Pages](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_data.go#L28>)

```go
func (d Data) Pages() Pages
```

Pages returns the pages stored with key "pages". If this is a func, it will be invoked.

<a name="DataProvider"></a>
## type [DataProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L474-L476>)

DataProvider provides access to the data directory.

```go
type DataProvider interface {
    Data() map[string]any
}
```

<a name="FileProvider"></a>
## type [FileProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L121-L125>)

FileProvider provides the source file.

```go
type FileProvider interface {
    // File returns the source file for this Page,
    // or a zero File if this Page is not backed by a file.
    File() *source.File
}
```

<a name="GetPageProvider"></a>
## type [GetPageProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L128-L135>)

GetPageProvider provides the GetPage method.

```go
type GetPageProvider interface {
    // GetPage looks up a page for the given ref.
    //    {{ with .GetPage "blog" }}{{ .Title }}{{ end }}
    //
    // This will return nil when no page could be found, and will return
    // an error if the ref is ambiguous.
    GetPage(ref string) (Page, error)
}
```

<a name="GitInfoProvider"></a>
## type [GitInfoProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L138-L143>)

GitInfoProvider provides Git info.

```go
type GitInfoProvider interface {
    // GitInfo returns the Git info for this object.
    GitInfo() *source.GitInfo
    // CodeOwners returns the code owners for this object.
    CodeOwners() []string
}
```

```go
    SummaryEndTag  types.LowHigh[string]
    WrapperStart   types.LowHigh[string]
    WrapperEnd     types.LowHigh[string]
    Divider        types.LowHigh[string]

}
```

ExtractSummaryFromHTML extracts a summary from the given HTML content.


ExtractSummaryFromHTMLWithDivider extracts a summary from the given HTML content with a manual summary divider.

<a name="HugoInfo"></a>
## type [HugoInfo](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/hugoinfo.go#L32-L47>)

HugoInfo contains information about the current Hugo environment.

```go
type HugoInfo struct {
    CommitHash string
    BuildDate  string

    // Version of Go that the Hugo binary was built with.
    GoVersion string

    // Context gives access to some of the context scoped variables.
    Context hugo.Context
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewHugoInfo"></a>
### func [NewHugoInfo](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/hugoinfo.go#L144>)

```go
func NewHugoInfo(opts HugoInfoOptions) HugoInfo
```

NewHugoInfo creates a new Hugo Info object.

<a name="HugoInfo.Deps"></a>
### func \(HugoInfo\) [Deps](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/hugoinfo.go#L93>)

```go
func (i HugoInfo) Deps() []*hugo.Dependency
```

Deps gets a list of dependencies for this Hugo build.

<a name="HugoInfo.Environment"></a>
### func \(HugoInfo\) [Environment](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/hugoinfo.go#L63>)

```go
func (i HugoInfo) Environment() string
```

Environment returns the build environment. Defaults are "production" \(hugo\) and "development" \(hugo server\). This can also be set by the user. It can be any string, but it will be all lower case.

<a name="HugoInfo.Generator"></a>
### func \(HugoInfo\) [Generator](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/hugoinfo.go#L55>)

```go
func (i HugoInfo) Generator() template.HTML
```

Generator returns a Hugo meta generator HTML tag.

<a name="HugoInfo.IsDevelopment"></a>
### func \(HugoInfo\) [IsDevelopment](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/hugoinfo.go#L68>)

```go
func (i HugoInfo) IsDevelopment() bool
```

IsDevelopment reports whether the current running environment is "development".

<a name="HugoInfo.IsExtended"></a>
### func \(HugoInfo\) [IsExtended](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/hugoinfo.go#L83>)

```go
func (i HugoInfo) IsExtended() bool
```

IsExtended reports whether the Hugo binary is the extended version.

<a name="HugoInfo.IsMultiHost"></a>
### func \(HugoInfo\) [IsMultiHost](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/hugoinfo.go#L102>)

```go
func (i HugoInfo) IsMultiHost() bool
```

Deprecated: Use hugo.IsMultihost instead.

<a name="HugoInfo.IsMultihost"></a>
### func \(HugoInfo\) [IsMultihost](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/hugoinfo.go#L108>)

```go
func (i HugoInfo) IsMultihost() bool
```

IsMultihost reports whether each configured language has a unique baseURL.

<a name="HugoInfo.IsMultilingual"></a>
### func \(HugoInfo\) [IsMultilingual](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/hugoinfo.go#L113>)

```go
func (i HugoInfo) IsMultilingual() bool
```

IsMultilingual reports whether there are two or more configured languages.

<a name="HugoInfo.IsProduction"></a>
### func \(HugoInfo\) [IsProduction](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/hugoinfo.go#L73>)

```go
func (i HugoInfo) IsProduction() bool
```

IsProduction reports whether the current running environment is "production".

<a name="HugoInfo.IsServer"></a>
### func \(HugoInfo\) [IsServer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/hugoinfo.go#L78>)

```go
func (i HugoInfo) IsServer() bool
```

IsServer reports whether the built\-in server is running.

```go

```

```go

```

Version returns the current version as a comparable version string.

HugoInfoConfigProvider represents the config options that are relevant for HugoInfo.

```go
type HugoInfoConfigProvider interface {
    Environment() string

    WorkingDir() string
    IsMultihost() bool
    IsMultilingual() bool
}
```

```go
    DataProvider
}
```

<a name="HugoInfoOptions"></a>
## type [HugoInfoOptions](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/hugoinfo.go#L133-L141>)

HugoInfoOptions defines the providers required to initialize HugoInfo.

```go
type HugoInfoOptions struct {
    Conf                      HugoInfoConfigProvider
    HugoInfoHugoSitesProvider HugoInfoHugoSitesProvider

    Deps       []*hugo.Dependency
    CommitHash string
    BuildDate  string
    GoVersion  string
}
```

<a name="InSectionPositioner"></a>
## type [InSectionPositioner](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L146-L151>)

InSectionPositioner provides section navigation.

```go
type InSectionPositioner interface {
    // NextInSection returns the next page in the same section.
    NextInSection() Page
    // PrevInSection returns the previous page in the same section.
    PrevInSection() Page
}
```

<a name="LazyContentProvider"></a>
## type [LazyContentProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_lazy_contentprovider.go#L50-L52>)

LazyContentProvider initializes itself when read. Each method of the ContentProvider interface initializes a content provider and shares it with other methods.

Used in cases where we cannot guarantee whether the content provider will be needed. Must create via NewLazyContentProvider.

```go
type LazyContentProvider struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewLazyContentProvider"></a>
### func [NewLazyContentProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_lazy_contentprovider.go#L57>)

```go
func NewLazyContentProvider(f func(ctx context.Context) OutputFormatContentProvider) *LazyContentProvider
```

NewLazyContentProvider returns a LazyContentProvider initialized with function f. The resulting LazyContentProvider calls f in order to retrieve a ContentProvider

<a name="LazyContentProvider.Content"></a>
### func \(\*LazyContentProvider\) [Content](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_lazy_contentprovider.go#L80>)

```go
func (lcp *LazyContentProvider) Content(ctx context.Context) (any, error)
```

### func \(\*LazyContentProvider\) [ContentWithoutSummary](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_lazy_contentprovider.go#L84>)

```go
func (lcp *LazyContentProvider) ContentWithoutSummary(ctx context.Context) (template.HTML, error)
```

```go
func (lcp *LazyContentProvider) Fragments(ctx context.Context) *tableofcontents.Fragments
```



<a name="LazyContentProvider.FuzzyWordCount"></a>

```go
func (lcp *LazyContentProvider) FuzzyWordCount(ctx context.Context) int
```

```go
func (lcp *LazyContentProvider) Len(ctx context.Context) int
```

<a name="OrderedTaxonomy"></a>
## type [OrderedTaxonomy](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L42>)

OrderedTaxonomy is another representation of an Taxonomy using an array rather than a map. Important because you can't order a map.

```go
type OrderedTaxonomy []OrderedTaxonomyEntry
```

<a name="OrderedTaxonomy.Reverse"></a>
### func \(OrderedTaxonomy\) [Reverse](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L139>)

```go
func (t OrderedTaxonomy) Reverse() OrderedTaxonomy
```

Reverse reverses the order of the entries in this taxonomy.

<a name="OrderedTaxonomyEntry"></a>
## type [OrderedTaxonomyEntry](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L55-L58>)

OrderedTaxonomyEntry is similar to an element of a Taxonomy, but with the key embedded \(as name\) e.g: \{Name: Technology, WeightedPages: TaxonomyPages\}

```go
type OrderedTaxonomyEntry struct {
    Name string
    WeightedPages
}
```

<a name="OrderedTaxonomyEntry.Count"></a>
### func \(OrderedTaxonomyEntry\) [Count](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L129>)

```go
func (ie OrderedTaxonomyEntry) Count() int
```

Count returns the count the pages in this taxonomy.

<a name="OrderedTaxonomyEntry.Pages"></a>
### func \(OrderedTaxonomyEntry\) [Pages](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L124>)

```go
func (ie OrderedTaxonomyEntry) Pages() Pages
```

Pages returns the Pages for this taxonomy.

<a name="OrderedTaxonomyEntry.Term"></a>
### func \(OrderedTaxonomyEntry\) [Term](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L134>)

```go
func (ie OrderedTaxonomyEntry) Term() string
```

Term returns the name given to this taxonomy.

<a name="OutputFormat"></a>
## type [OutputFormat](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_outputformat.go#L32-L49>)

OutputFormat links to a representation of a resource.

```go
type OutputFormat struct {
    // Rel contains a value that can be used to construct a rel link.
    // This is value is fetched from the output format definition.
    // Note that for pages with only one output format,
    // this method will always return "canonical".
    // As an example, the AMP output format will, by default, return "amphtml".
    //
    // See:
    // https://www.ampproject.org/docs/guides/deploy/discovery
    //
    // Most other output formats will have "alternate" as value for this.
    Rel string

    Format output.Format
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewOutputFormat"></a>
### func [NewOutputFormat](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_outputformat.go#L76>)

```go
func NewOutputFormat(relPermalink, permalink string, isCanonical bool, f output.Format) OutputFormat
```



<a name="OutputFormat.IsZero"></a>
### func \(OutputFormat\) [IsZero](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_outputformat.go#L72>)

```go
func (o OutputFormat) IsZero() bool
```

IsZero checks whether this OutputFormat is the zero value.

<a name="OutputFormat.MediaType"></a>
### func \(OutputFormat\) [MediaType](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_outputformat.go#L57>)

```go
func (o OutputFormat) MediaType() media.Type
```

MediaType returns this OutputFormat's MediaType \(MIME type\).

<a name="OutputFormat.Name"></a>
### func \(OutputFormat\) [Name](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_outputformat.go#L52>)

```go
func (o OutputFormat) Name() string
```

Name returns this OutputFormat's name, i.e. HTML, AMP, JSON etc.

<a name="OutputFormat.Permalink"></a>
### func \(OutputFormat\) [Permalink](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_outputformat.go#L62>)

```go
func (o OutputFormat) Permalink() string
```

Permalink returns the absolute permalink to this output format.

<a name="OutputFormat.RelPermalink"></a>
### func \(OutputFormat\) [RelPermalink](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_outputformat.go#L67>)

```go
func (o OutputFormat) RelPermalink() string
```

RelPermalink returns the relative permalink to this output format.

<a name="OutputFormatContentProvider"></a>
## type [OutputFormatContentProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_lazy_contentprovider.go#L29-L34>)

OutputFormatContentProvider represents the method set that is "outputFormat aware" and that we provide lazy initialization for in case they get invoked outside of their normal rendering context, e.g. via .Translations. Note that this set is currently not complete, but should cover the most common use cases. For the others, the implementation will be from the page.NoopPage.

```go
type OutputFormatContentProvider interface {
    OutputFormatPageContentProvider

    // for internal use.
    ContentRenderer
}
```

<a name="OutputFormatPageContentProvider"></a>
## type [OutputFormatPageContentProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_lazy_contentprovider.go#L37-L42>)

OutputFormatPageContentProvider holds the exported methods from Page that are "outputFormat aware".

```go
type OutputFormatPageContentProvider interface {
    MarkupProvider
    ContentProvider
    TableOfContentsProvider
    PageRenderProvider
}
```

<a name="OutputFormats"></a>
## type [OutputFormats](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_outputformat.go#L27>)

OutputFormats holds a list of the relevant output formats for a given page.

```go
type OutputFormats []OutputFormat
```

<a name="OutputFormats.Canonical"></a>
### func \(OutputFormats\) [Canonical](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_outputformat.go#L107>)

```go
func (o OutputFormats) Canonical() OutputFormat
```

Canonical returns the first canonical OutputFormat for this page, or a zero OutputFormat if not found.

<a name="OutputFormats.Get"></a>
### func \(OutputFormats\) [Get](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_outputformat.go#L96>)

```go
func (o OutputFormats) Get(name string) OutputFormat
```

Get gets a OutputFormat given its name, i.e. json, html etc. It returns a zero OutputFormat if not found.

OutputFormatsProvider provides the OutputFormats of a Page.

```go
type OutputFormatsProvider interface {

    OutputFormats() OutputFormats
}
```

<a name="Page"></a>
## type [Page](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L172-L178>)

Page is the core interface in Hugo and what you get as the top level data context in your templates.

```go
type Page interface {
    MarkupProvider
    ContentProvider
    TableOfContentsProvider
    PageWithoutContent
    fmt.Stringer
}
```

<a name="PageFragment"></a>
## type [PageFragment](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L180-L183>)



```go
type PageFragment interface {
    resource.ResourceLinksProvider
    resource.ResourceNameTitleProvider
}
```

<a name="PageGenealogist"></a>
## type [PageGenealogist](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages_related.go#L37-L51>)

A PageGenealogist finds related pages in a page collection. This interface is implemented by Pages and PageGroup, which makes it available as \`\{\{ .RegularRelated . \}\}\` etc.

```go
type PageGenealogist interface {
    // Template example:
    // {{ $related := .RegularPages.Related . }}
    Related(ctx context.Context, opts any) (Pages, error)

    // Template example:
    // {{ $related := .RegularPages.RelatedIndices . "tags" "date" }}
    // Deprecated: Use Related instead.
    RelatedIndices(ctx context.Context, doc related.Document, indices ...any) (Pages, error)

    // Template example:
    // {{ $related := .RegularPages.RelatedTo ( keyVals "tags" "hugo", "rocks")  ( keyVals "date" .Date ) }}
    // Deprecated: Use Related instead.
    RelatedTo(ctx context.Context, args ...types.KeyValues) (Pages, error)
}
```

<a name="PageGroup"></a>
## type [PageGroup](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagegroup.go#L43-L49>)

PageGroup represents a group of pages, grouped by the key. The key is typically a year or similar.

```go
type PageGroup struct {
    // The key, typically a year or similar.
    Key any

    // The Pages in this group.
    Pages
}
```

<a name="PageGroup.ProbablyEq"></a>
### func \(PageGroup\) [ProbablyEq](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagegroup.go#L369>)

```go
func (p PageGroup) ProbablyEq(other any) bool
```

ProbablyEq wraps compare.ProbablyEqer For internal use.

<a name="PageGroup.Slice"></a>
### func \(PageGroup\) [Slice](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagegroup.go#L384>)

```go
func (p PageGroup) Slice(in any) (any, error)
```

Slice is for internal use. for the template functions. See collections.Slice.

<a name="PageMatcher"></a>
## type [PageMatcher](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_matcher.go#L37-L61>)

A PageMatcher can be used to match a Page with Glob patterns. Note that the pattern matching is case insensitive.

```go
type PageMatcher struct {
    // A Glob pattern matching the content path below /content.
    // Expects Unix-styled slashes.
    // Note that this is the virtual path, so it starts at the mount root
    // with a leading "/".
    Path string

    // A Glob pattern matching the Page's Kind(s), e.g. "{home,section}"
    Kind string

    // A Glob pattern matching the Page's language, e.g. "{en,sv}".
    // Deprecated: use Sites.Matrix instead.
    Lang string

    // The sites to apply this to.
    // Note that we currently only use the Matrix field for cascade matching.
    Sites sitesmatrix.Sites

    // A Glob pattern matching the Page's Environment, e.g. "{production,development}".
    Environment string

    // Compiled values.
    // The site vectors to apply this to.
    SitesMatrixCompiled sitesmatrix.VectorProvider `mapstructure:"-"`
}
```

<a name="PageMatcher.Match"></a>
### func \(PageMatcher\) [Match](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_matcher.go#L67>)

```go
func (m PageMatcher) Match(kind, path, environment string, sitesMatrix sitesmatrix.VectorProvider) bool
```



<a name="PageMatcher.Matches"></a>
### func \(PageMatcher\) [Matches](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_matcher.go#L63>)

```go
func (m PageMatcher) Matches(p Page) bool
```

```go
type PageMatcherParamsConfig struct {
    // Apply Params to all Pages matching Target.
    Params hmaps.Params
    // Fields holds all fields but Params.
    Fields hmaps.Params
    // Target is the PageMatcher that this config applies to.
    Target PageMatcher
}
```

PageMetaInternalProvider provides internal page metadata.

```go
type PageMetaInternalProvider interface {
    // This is for internal use only.
    PathInfo() *paths.Path
}
```

<a name="PageMetaLanguageResource"></a>
## type [PageMetaLanguageResource](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L190-L193>)

<a name="PageMetaProvider"></a>
## type [PageMetaProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L196-L255>)

PageMetaProvider provides page metadata, typically provided via front matter.

```go
type PageMetaProvider interface {
    // The 4 page dates
    resource.Dated

    // BundleType returns the bundle type: `leaf`, `branch` or an empty string.
    BundleType() string

    // A configured description.
    Description() string

    // Whether this is a draft. Will only be true if run with the --buildDrafts (-D) flag.
    Draft() bool

    // IsHome returns whether this is the home page.
    IsHome() bool

    // Configured keywords.
    Keywords() []string

    // The Page Kind. One of page, home, section, taxonomy, term.
    Kind() string

    // The configured layout to use to render this page. Typically set in front matter.
    Layout() string

    // The title used for links.
    LinkTitle() string

    // IsNode returns whether this is an item of one of the list types in Hugo,
    // i.e. not a regular content
    IsNode() bool

    // IsPage returns whether this is a regular content
    IsPage() bool

    // Path gets the relative path, including file name and extension if relevant,
    // to the source of this Page. It will be relative to any content root.
    Path() string

    // The slug, typically defined in front matter.
    Slug() string

    // IsSection returns whether this is a section
    IsSection() bool

    // Section returns the first path element below the content root.
    Section() string

    // Sitemap returns the sitemap configuration for this page.
    // This is for internal use only.
    Sitemap() config.SitemapConfig

    // Type is a discriminator used to select layouts etc. It is typically set
    // in front matter, but will fall back to the root section.
    Type() string

    // The configured weight, used as the first sort value in the default
    // page sort if non-zero.
    Weight() int
}
```

<a name="PageMetaResource"></a>
## type [PageMetaResource](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L185-L188>)



```go
type PageMetaResource interface {
    PageMetaProvider
    resource.Resource
}
```

<a name="PageProvider"></a>
## type [PageProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L167-L169>)

PageProvider provides access to a Page. Implemented by shortcodes and others.

```go
type PageProvider interface {
    Page() Page
}
```

<a name="PageRenderProvider"></a>
## type [PageRenderProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L324-L335>)

PageRenderProvider provides a way for a Page to render content.

```go
type PageRenderProvider interface {
    // Render renders the given layout with this Page as context.
    Render(ctx context.Context, layout ...string) (template.HTML, error)
    // RenderString renders the first value in args with the content renderer defined
    // for this Page.
    // It takes an optional map as a second argument:
    //
    // display (“inline”):
    // - inline or block. If inline (default), surrounding <p></p> on short snippets will be trimmed.
    // markup (defaults to the Page’s markup)
    RenderString(ctx context.Context, args ...any) (template.HTML, error)
}
```

<a name="PageWithoutContent"></a>
## type [PageWithoutContent](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L338-L402>)

PageWithoutContent is the Page without any of the content methods.

```go
type PageWithoutContent interface {
    RawContentProvider
    RenderShortcodesProvider
    resource.Resource
    PageMetaProvider

    Param(key any) (any, error)
    Aliases() []string
    PageMetaInternalProvider

    resource.LanguageProvider

    // For pages backed by a file.
    FileProvider

    GitInfoProvider

    // Output formats
    OutputFormatsProvider
    AlternativeOutputFormatsProvider

    // Tree navigation
    ChildCareProvider
    TreeProvider

    // Horizontal navigation
    InSectionPositioner
    PageRenderProvider
    PaginatorProvider
    Positioner
    navigation.PageMenusProvider

    // Page lookups/refs
    GetPageProvider
    RefProvider

    resource.TranslationKeyProvider
    TranslationsProvider

    SiteProvider
    SitesProvider

    // Helper methods
    ShortcodeInfoProvider
    compare.Eqer

    // Scratch returns a Scratch that can be used to store temporary state.
    // Note that this Scratch gets reset on server rebuilds. See Store() for a variant that survives.
    // Scratch returns a "scratch pad" that can be used to store state.
    // Deprecated: From Hugo v0.138.0 this is just an alias for Store.
    Scratch() *hstore.Scratch

    hstore.StoreProvider

    RelatedKeywordsProvider

    // GetTerms gets the terms of a given taxonomy,
    // e.g. GetTerms("categories")
    GetTerms(taxonomy string) Pages

    // HeadingsFiltered returns the headings for this page when a filter is set.
    // This is currently only triggered with the Related content feature
    // and the "fragments" type of index.
    HeadingsFiltered(context.Context) tableofcontents.Headings
}
```

<a name="Pager"></a>
## type [Pager](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagination.go#L49-L52>)

Pager represents one of the elements in a paginator. The number, starting on 1, represents its place.

```go
type Pager struct {
    *Paginator
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Pager.First"></a>
### func \(\*Pager\) [First](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagination.go#L182>)

```go
func (p *Pager) First() *Pager
```

First returns the pager for the first page.

<a name="Pager.HasNext"></a>
### func \(\*Pager\) [HasNext](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagination.go#L169>)

```go
func (p *Pager) HasNext() bool
```

HasNext tests whether there are page\(s\) after the current.

<a name="Pager.HasPrev"></a>
### func \(\*Pager\) [HasPrev](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagination.go#L156>)

```go
func (p *Pager) HasPrev() bool
```

HasPrev tests whether there are page\(s\) before the current.

### func \(\*Pager\) [Last](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagination.go#L187>)

```go
func (p *Pager) Last() *Pager
```

Last returns the pager for the last page.

<a name="Pager.Next"></a>
### func \(\*Pager\) [Next](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagination.go#L174>)

```go
func (p *Pager) Next() *Pager
```

Next returns the pager for the next page.

<a name="Pager.NumberOfElements"></a>

```go
func (p *Pager) NumberOfElements() int
```

NumberOfElements gets the number of elements on this page.

<a name="Pager.PageGroups"></a>
### func \(\*Pager\) [PageGroups](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagination.go#L105>)

```go
func (p *Pager) PageGroups() PagesGroup
```

PageGroups return Page groups for this page. Note: If this return non\-empty result, then Pages\(\) will return empty.

<a name="Pager.PageNumber"></a>
### func \(\*Pager\) [PageNumber](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagination.go#L80>)

```go
func (p *Pager) PageNumber() int
```

PageNumber returns the current page's number in the pager sequence.

<a name="Pager.Pages"></a>
### func \(\*Pager\) [Pages](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagination.go#L91>)

```go
func (p *Pager) Pages() Pages
```

Pages returns the Pages on this page. Note: If this return a non\-empty result, then PageGroups\(\) will return empty.

<a name="Pager.Prev"></a>
### func \(\*Pager\) [Prev](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagination.go#L161>)

```go
func (p *Pager) Prev() *Pager
```

Prev returns the pager for the previous page.

<a name="Pager.String"></a>
### func \(Pager\) [String](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagination.go#L54>)

```go
func (p Pager) String() string
```

```go
func (p *Pager) URL() string
```

URL returns the URL to the current page.

<a name="Pages"></a>
## type [Pages](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages.go#L26>)

Pages is a slice of Page objects. This is the most common list type in Hugo.

```go
type Pages []Page
```

<a name="ToPages"></a>
### func [ToPages](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages.go#L53>)

```go
func ToPages(seq any) (Pages, error)
```

ToPages tries to convert seq into Pages.

<a name="Pages.ByDate"></a>
### func \(Pages\) [ByDate](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages_sort.go#L273>)

```go
func (p Pages) ByDate() Pages
```

ByDate sorts the Pages by date and returns a copy.

Adjacent invocations on the same receiver will return a cached result.

This may safely be executed in parallel.

<a name="Pages.ByExpiryDate"></a>
### func \(Pages\) [ByExpiryDate](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages_sort.go#L299>)

```go
func (p Pages) ByExpiryDate() Pages
```

ByExpiryDate sorts the Pages by publish date and returns a copy.

Adjacent invocations on the same receiver will return a cached result.

This may safely be executed in parallel.

<a name="Pages.ByLanguage"></a>
### func \(Pages\) [ByLanguage](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages_sort.go#L361>)

```go
func (p Pages) ByLanguage() Pages
```

ByLanguage sorts the Pages by the language's Weight.

Adjacent invocations on the same receiver will return a cached result.

This may safely be executed in parallel.

<a name="Pages.ByLastmod"></a>
### func \(Pages\) [ByLastmod](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages_sort.go#L316>)

```go
func (p Pages) ByLastmod() Pages
```

ByLastmod sorts the Pages by the last modification date and returns a copy.

Adjacent invocations on the same receiver will return a cached result.

This may safely be executed in parallel.

<a name="Pages.ByLength"></a>
### func \(Pages\) [ByLength](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages_sort.go#L333>)

```go
func (p Pages) ByLength(ctx context.Context) Pages
```

ByLength sorts the Pages by length and returns a copy.

Adjacent invocations on the same receiver will return a cached result.

This may safely be executed in parallel.

<a name="Pages.ByLinkTitle"></a>
### func \(Pages\) [ByLinkTitle](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages_sort.go#L260>)

```go
func (p Pages) ByLinkTitle() Pages
```

ByLinkTitle sorts the Pages by link title and returns a copy.

Adjacent invocations on the same receiver will return a cached result.

This may safely be executed in parallel.

<a name="Pages.ByParam"></a>
### func \(Pages\) [ByParam](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages_sort.go#L403>)

```go
func (p Pages) ByParam(paramsKey any) Pages
```

ByParam sorts the pages according to the given page Params key.

Adjacent invocations on the same receiver with the same paramsKey will return a cached result.

This may safely be executed in parallel.

<a name="Pages.ByPublishDate"></a>
### func \(Pages\) [ByPublishDate](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages_sort.go#L286>)

```go
func (p Pages) ByPublishDate() Pages
```

ByPublishDate sorts the Pages by publish date and returns a copy.

Adjacent invocations on the same receiver will return a cached result.

This may safely be executed in parallel.

<a name="Pages.ByTitle"></a>
### func \(Pages\) [ByTitle](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages_sort.go#L247>)

```go
func (p Pages) ByTitle() Pages
```

ByTitle sorts the Pages by title and returns a copy.

Adjacent invocations on the same receiver will return a cached result.

This may safely be executed in parallel.

<a name="Pages.ByWeight"></a>
### func \(Pages\) [ByWeight](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages_sort.go#L231>)

```go
func (p Pages) ByWeight() Pages
```

ByWeight sorts the Pages by weight and returns a copy.

Adjacent invocations on the same receiver will return a cached result.

This may safely be executed in parallel.

<a name="Pages.Group"></a>
### func \(Pages\) [Group](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pages.go#L92>)

```go
func (p Pages) Group(key any, in any) (any, error)
```

Group groups the pages in in by key. This implements collections.Grouper.

<a name="Pages.GroupBy"></a>
### func \(Pages\) [GroupBy](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagegroup.go#L114>)

```go
func (p Pages) GroupBy(ctx context.Context, key string, order ...string) (PagesGroup, error)
```

GroupBy groups by the value in the given field or method name and with the given order. Valid values for order is asc, desc, rev and reverse.

<a name="Pages.GroupByDate"></a>
### func \(Pages\) [GroupByDate](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagegroup.go#L276>)

```go
func (p Pages) GroupByDate(format string, order ...string) (PagesGroup, error)
```

GroupByDate groups by the given page's Date value in the given format and with the given order. Valid values for order is asc, desc, rev and reverse. For valid format strings, see https://golang.org/pkg/time/#Time.Format


Adjacent invocations on the same receiver will return a cached result.

This may safely be executed in parallel.

<a name="PaginatorProvider"></a>
## type [PaginatorProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/pagination.go#L28-L33>)

PaginatorProvider provides two ways to create a page paginator.

```go
type PaginatorProvider interface {
    // Paginator creates a paginator with the default page set.
    Paginator(options ...any) (*Pager, error)
    // Paginate creates a paginator with the given page set in pages.
    Paginate(pages any, options ...any) (*Pager, error)
}
```

<a name="Positioner"></a>
## type [Positioner](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L409-L414>)

Positioner provides next/prev navigation.

```go
type Positioner interface {
    // Next points up to the next regular page (sorted by Hugo’s default sort).
    Next() Page
    // Prev points down to the previous regular page (sorted by Hugo’s default sort).
    Prev() Page
}
```

<a name="RawContentProvider"></a>

RawContentProvider provides the raw, unprocessed content of the page.

```go
type RawContentProvider interface {

    RawContent() string
}
```

<a name="RefProvider"></a>
## type [RefProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L428-L440>)

RefProvider provides the methods needed to create reflinks to pages.

```go
type RefProvider interface {
    // Ref returns an absolute URl to a page.
    Ref(argsm map[string]any) (string, error)

    // RefFrom is for internal use only.
    RefFrom(argsm map[string]any, source any) (string, error)

    // RelRef returns a relative URL to a page.
    RelRef(argsm map[string]any) (string, error)

    // RelRefFrom is for internal use only.
    RelRefFrom(argsm map[string]any, source any) (string, error)
}
```

ShortcodeInfoProvider provides info about the shortcodes in a Page.

```go
type ShortcodeInfoProvider interface {
    // HasShortcode return whether the page has a shortcode with the given name.
    // This method is mainly motivated with the Hugo Docs site's need for a list
    // of pages with the `todo` shortcode in it.
```
