```go
}
```

<a name="Site"></a>
## type [Site](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/site.go#L37-L138>)

Site represents a site. There can be multiple sites in a multilingual setup.

```go
type Site interface {
    // Returns the Language configured for this Site.
    Language() *langs.Language

    // Returns the Site in one of dimensions language, version or role.
    Dimension(string) SiteDimension

    // Returns the role configured for this Site.
    Role() roles.Role

    // Returns the version configured for this Site.
    Version() versions.Version

    // Returns all the languages configured for all sites.
    Languages() langs.Languages

    GetPage(ref ...string) (Page, error)

    // AllPages returns all pages for all languages.
    AllPages() Pages

    // Returns all the regular Pages in this Site.
    RegularPages() Pages

    // Returns all Pages in this Site.
    Pages() Pages

    // Returns all the top level sections.
    Sections() Pages

    // A shortcut to the home
    Home() Page

    // Returns the server port.
    ServerPort() int

    // Returns the configured title for this Site.
    Title() string

    // Deprecated: Use .Language.Locale instead.
    LanguageCode() string

    // Returns the configured copyright information for this Site.
    Copyright() string

    // Returns all sites for all dimensions.
    Sites() Sites

    // Returns Site currently rendering.
    Current() Site

    // Reports whether this site is the default across all dimensions.
    IsDefault() bool

    // Returns a struct with some information about the build.
    Hugo() HugoInfo

    // Returns the BaseURL for this Site.
    BaseURL() string

    // Returns a taxonomy map.
    Taxonomies() TaxonomyList

    // Returns the last modification date of the content.
    Lastmod() time.Time

    // Returns the Menus for this site.
    Menus() navigation.Menus

    // The main sections in the site.
    MainSections() []string

    // Returns the Params configured for this site.
    Params() hmaps.Params

    // Param is a convenience method to do lookups in Params.
    Param(key any) (any, error)

    // Returns a map of all the data inside /data.
    Data() map[string]any

    // Returns the project config.
    Config() SiteConfig

    // BuildDrafts is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.
    BuildDrafts() bool

    // LanguagePrefix returns the language prefix for this site.
    LanguagePrefix() string

    hstore.StoreProvider

    // String returns a string representation of the site.
    // Note that this representation may change in the future.
    String() string

    // For internal use only.
    // This will panic if the site is not fully initialized.
    // This is typically used to inform the user in the content adapter templates,
    // as these are executed before all the page collections etc. are ready to use.
    CheckReady()
}
```

## type [TableOfContentsProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L484-L490>)

TableOfContentsProvider provides the table of contents for a Page.

```go
type TableOfContentsProvider interface {
    // TableOfContents returns the table of contents for the page rendered as HTML.
    TableOfContents(context.Context) template.HTML
```

<a name="TargetPathDescriptor"></a>
## type [TargetPathDescriptor](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_paths.go#L39-L82>)



```go
type TargetPathDescriptor struct {
    PathSpec *helpers.PathSpec

    Type output.Format
    Kind string

    Path    *paths.Path
    Section *paths.Path

    // For regular content pages this is either
    // 1) the Slug, if set,
    // 2) the file base name (TranslationBaseName).
    BaseName string

    // PrefixFilePath contains zero or more content dimensions used as a file
    // path prefix. Dimensions are ordered role/version/language for
    // single-host and language/role/version for multihost.
    // Example (single-host): guest/v1.0.0/en
    // Example (multihost):   en/guest/v1.0.0
    PrefixFilePath string

    // PrefixLink contains zero or more content dimensions used as a link
    // prefix. Dimensions are ordered role/version/language for single-host,
    // but the language dimension is excluded for multihost.
    // Example (single-host): guest/v1.0.0/en
    // Example (multihost):   guest/v1.0.0
    PrefixLink string

    // If in multihost mode etc., every link/path needs to be prefixed, even
    // if set in URL.
    ForcePrefix bool

    // URL from front matter if set. Will override any Slug etc.
    URL string

    // Used to create paginator links.
    Addends string

    // The expanded permalink if defined for the section, ready to use.
    ExpandedPermalink string

    // Some types cannot have uglyURLs, even if globally enabled, RSS being one example.
    UglyURLs bool
}
```

<a name="TargetPaths"></a>
## type [TargetPaths](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page_paths.go#L85-L97>)

TODO\(bep\) move this type.

```go
type TargetPaths struct {
    // Where to store the file on disk relative to the publish dir. OS slashes.
    TargetFilename string

    // The directory to write sub-resources of the above.
    SubResourceBaseTarget string

    // The base for creating links to sub-resources of the above.
    SubResourceBaseLink string

    // The relative permalink to this resources. Unix slashes.
    Link string
}
```

<a name="Taxonomy"></a>
## type [Taxonomy](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L38>)

A Taxonomy is a map of keywords to a list of pages. For example

```
TagTaxonomy['technology'] = WeightedPages
TagTaxonomy['go']  =  WeightedPages
```

```go
type Taxonomy map[string]WeightedPages
```

<a name="Taxonomy.Alphabetical"></a>
### func \(Taxonomy\) [Alphabetical](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L80>)

```go
func (i Taxonomy) Alphabetical() OrderedTaxonomy
```

Alphabetical returns an ordered taxonomy sorted by key name.

<a name="Taxonomy.ByCount"></a>
### func \(Taxonomy\) [ByCount](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L99>)

```go
func (i Taxonomy) ByCount() OrderedTaxonomy
```

ByCount returns an ordered taxonomy sorted by \# of pages per key. If taxonomies have the same \# of pages, sort them alphabetical

<a name="Taxonomy.Count"></a>
### func \(Taxonomy\) [Count](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L66>)

```go
func (i Taxonomy) Count(key string) int
```

Count the weighted pages for the given key.

<a name="Taxonomy.Get"></a>
### func \(Taxonomy\) [Get](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L61>)

```go
func (i Taxonomy) Get(key string) WeightedPages
```

Get the weighted pages for the given key.

<a name="Taxonomy.Page"></a>
### func \(Taxonomy\) [Page](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L116>)

```go
func (i Taxonomy) Page() Page
```

Page returns the taxonomy page or nil if the taxonomy has no terms.

<a name="Taxonomy.TaxonomyArray"></a>
### func \(Taxonomy\) [TaxonomyArray](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L69>)

```go
func (i Taxonomy) TaxonomyArray() OrderedTaxonomy
```

TaxonomyArray returns an ordered taxonomy with a non defined order.

<a name="TaxonomyList"></a>
## type [TaxonomyList](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L27>)

The TaxonomyList is a list of all taxonomies and their values e.g. List\['tags'\] =\> TagTaxonomy \(from above\)

```go
type TaxonomyList map[string]Taxonomy
```

<a name="TaxonomyList.String"></a>
### func \(TaxonomyList\) [String](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/taxonomy.go#L29>)

```go
func (tl TaxonomyList) String() string
```



<a name="TranslationsProvider"></a>
## type [TranslationsProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L493-L503>)

TranslationsProvider provides access to any translations.

```go
type TranslationsProvider interface {
    // IsTranslated returns whether this content file is translated to
    // other language(s).
    IsTranslated() bool

    // AllTranslations returns all translations, including the current Page.
    AllTranslations() Pages

    // Translations returns the translations excluding the current Page.
    Translations() Pages
}
```

<a name="TreeProvider"></a>
## type [TreeProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/page.go#L506-L549>)

TreeProvider provides section tree navigation.

```go
type TreeProvider interface {
    // IsAncestor returns whether the current page is an ancestor of other.
    // Note that this method is not relevant for taxonomy lists and taxonomy terms pages.
    IsAncestor(other any) bool

    // CurrentSection returns the page's current section or the page itself if home or a section.
    // Note that this will return nil for pages that is not regular, home or section pages.
    CurrentSection() Page

    // IsDescendant returns whether the current page is a descendant of other.
    // Note that this method is not relevant for taxonomy lists and taxonomy terms pages.
    IsDescendant(other any) bool

    // FirstSection returns the section on level 1 below home, e.g. "/docs".
    // For the home page, this will return itself.
    FirstSection() Page

    // InSection returns whether other is in the current section.
    // Note that this will always return false for pages that are
    // not either regular, home or section pages.
    InSection(other any) bool

    // Parent returns a section's parent section or a page's section.
    // To get a section's subsections, see Page's Sections method.
    Parent() Page

    // Ancestors returns the ancestors of each page
    Ancestors() Pages

    // Sections returns this section's subsections, if any.
    // Note that for non-sections, this method will always return an empty list.
    Sections() Pages

    // Page returns a reference to the Page itself, kept here mostly
    // for legacy reasons.
    Page() Page

    // Returns a slice of sections (directories if it's a file) to this
    // Page.
    SectionsEntries() []string

    // SectionsPath is SectionsEntries joined with a /.
    SectionsPath() string
}
```

<a name="WeightedPages"></a>
## type [WeightedPages](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/weighted.go#L27>)

WeightedPages is a list of Pages with their corresponding \(and relative\) weight \[\{Weight: 30, Page: \*1\}, \{Weight: 40, Page: \*2\}\]

```go
type WeightedPages []WeightedPage
```

<a name="WeightedPages.Count"></a>
### func \(WeightedPages\) [Count](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/weighted.go#L131>)

```go
func (wp WeightedPages) Count() int
```

Count returns the number of pages in this weighted page set.

```go

```

```go

```

```go

```

Next returns the next Page relative to the given Page in this weighted page set.

<a name="WeightedPages.Page"></a>
### func \(WeightedPages\) [Page](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/weighted.go#L31>)

```go
func (p WeightedPages) Page() Page
```

Page will return the Page \(of Kind taxonomyList\) that represents this set of pages. This method will panic if p is empty, as that should never happen.

<a name="WeightedPages.Pages"></a>
### func \(WeightedPages\) [Pages](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/weighted.go#L88>)

```go
func (wp WeightedPages) Pages() Pages
```

Pages returns the Pages in this weighted page set.

```go
func (wp WeightedPages) Prev(cur Page) Page
```

Prev returns the previous Page relative to the given Page in this weighted page set.

### func \(WeightedPages\) [Sort](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/page/weighted.go#L128>)

```go
func (wp WeightedPages) Sort()
```

Sort stable sorts this weighted page set.

```go

```

<a name="PostProcessPrefix"></a>

```go
const (
    PostProcessPrefix = "__h_pp_l1"

    // The suffix has an '=' in it to prevent the minifier to remove any enclosing
    // quoutes around the attribute values.
    // See issue #8884.
    PostProcessSuffix = "__e="
)
```

```go
)
```

<a name="Cloner"></a>
## type [Cloner](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resourcetypes.go#L33-L35>)

Cloner is for internal use.

```go
type Cloner interface {
    Clone() Resource
}
```

<a name="ContentProvider"></a>
## type [ContentProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resourcetypes.go#L186-L194>)

ContentProvider provides Content. This should be used with care, as it will read the file content into memory, but it should be cached as effectively as possible by the implementation.

```go
type ContentProvider interface {
    // Content returns this resource's content. It will be equivalent to reading the content
    // that RelPermalink points to in the published folder.
    // The return type will be contextual, and should be what you would expect:
    // * Page: template.HTML
    // * JSON: String
    // * Etc.
    Content(context.Context) (any, error)
}
```

<a name="ContentResource"></a>
## type [ContentResource](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resourcetypes.go#L178-L181>)

ContentResource represents a Resource that provides a way to get to its content. Most Resource types in Hugo implements this interface, including Page.

```go
type ContentResource interface {
    MediaType() media.Type
    ContentProvider
}
```

<a name="Dated"></a>
## type [Dated](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/dates.go#L24-L36>)

Dated wraps a "dated resource". These are the 4 dates that makes the date logic in Hugo.

```go
type Dated interface {
    // Date returns the date of the resource.
    Date() time.Time

    // Lastmod returns the last modification date of the resource.
    Lastmod() time.Time

    // PublishDate returns the publish date of the resource.
    PublishDate() time.Time

    // ExpiryDate returns the expiration date of the resource.
    ExpiryDate() time.Time
}
```

<a name="Identifier"></a>
## type [Identifier](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resourcetypes.go#L162-L166>)

Identifier identifies a resource.

```go
type Identifier interface {
    // Key is mostly for internal use and should be considered opaque.
    // This value may change between Hugo versions.
    Key() string
}
```

```go
type IsProbablySameResourceGetter interface {
    IsProbablySameResourceGetter(other ResourceGetter) bool
}
```

```go
}
```

<a name="ResourceFinder"></a>
## type [ResourceFinder](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resources.go#L288-L319>)

ResourceFinder provides methods to find Resources. Note that GetRemote \(as found in resources.GetRemote\) is not covered by this interface, as this is only available as a global template function.

```go
type ResourceFinder interface {
    ResourceGetter

    // GetMatch finds the first Resource matching the given pattern, or nil if none found.
    //
    // See Match for a more complete explanation about the rules used.
    //
    // It returns nil if no Resource could found, panics if pattern is invalid.
    GetMatch(pattern any) Resource

    // Match gets all resources matching the given base path prefix, e.g
    // "*.png" will match all png files. The "*" does not match path delimiters (/),
    // so if you organize your resources in sub-folders, you need to be explicit about it, e.g.:
    // "images/*.png". To match any PNG image anywhere in the bundle you can do "**.png", and
    // to match all PNG images below the images folder, use "images/**.jpg".
    //
    // The matching is case insensitive.
    //
    // Match matches by using a relative pathwith Unix style slashes (/) and no
    // leading slash, e.g. "images/logo.png".
    //
    // See https://github.com/gobwas/glob for the full rules set.
    //
    // See Match for a more complete explanation about the rules used.
    //
    // It returns nil if no Resources could found, panics if pattern is invalid.
    Match(pattern any) Resources

    // ByType returns resources of a given resource type (e.g. "image").
    // It returns nil if no Resources could found, panics if typ is invalid.
    ByType(typ any) Resources
}
```

<a name="ResourceGetter"></a>
## type [ResourceGetter](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resources.go#L261-L266>)



```go
type ResourceGetter interface {
    // Get locates the Resource with the given name in the current context (e.g. in .Page.Resources).
    //
    // It returns nil if no Resource could found, panics if name is invalid.
    Get(name any) Resource
}
```

<a name="ResourceLinksProvider"></a>
## type [ResourceLinksProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resourcetypes.go#L101-L107>)



```go
type ResourceLinksProvider interface {
    // Permalink represents the absolute link to this resource.
    Permalink() string

    // RelPermalink represents the host relative link to this resource.
    RelPermalink() string
}
```

<a name="ResourceMetaProvider"></a>
## type [ResourceMetaProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resourcetypes.go#L110-L113>)

ResourceMetaProvider provides metadata about a resource.

```go

    ResourceNameTitleProvider
    ResourceParamsProvider
}
```

<a name="ResourceNameTitleProvider"></a>
## type [ResourceNameTitleProvider](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resourcetypes.go#L121-L132>)



```go
type ResourceNameTitleProvider interface {
    // Name is the logical name of this resource. This can be set in the front matter
    // metadata for this resource. If not set, Hugo will assign a value.
    // This will in most cases be the base filename.
    // So, for the image "/some/path/sunset.jpg" this will be "sunset.jpg".
    // The value returned by this method will be used in the GetByPrefix and ByPrefix methods
    // on Resources.
    Name() string

    // Title returns the title if set in front matter. For content pages, this will be the expected value.
    Title() string
}
```

```go
    // Params set in front matter for this resource.

}
```

```go
    // ResourceType is the resource type. For most file types, this is the main
    // part of the MIME type, e.g. "image", "application", "text" etc.
```

```go
}
```

<a name="Resources"></a>
## type [Resources](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resources.go#L34>)

Resources represents a slice of resources, which can be a mix of different types. I.e. both pages and images etc.

```go
type Resources []Resource
```

<a name="Resources.ByType"></a>
### func \(Resources\) [ByType](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resources.go#L89>)

```go
func (r Resources) ByType(typ any) Resources
```

ByType returns resources of a given resource type \(e.g. "image"\).

<a name="Resources.Get"></a>
### func \(Resources\) [Get](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resources.go#L106>)

```go
func (r Resources) Get(name any) Resource
```

Get locates the name given in Resources. The search is case insensitive.

<a name="Resources.GetMatch"></a>
### func \(Resources\) [GetMatch](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resources.go#L152>)

```go
func (r Resources) GetMatch(pattern any) Resource
```

GetMatch finds the first Resource matching the given pattern, or nil if none found. See Match for a more complete explanation about the rules used.

<a name="Resources.Match"></a>
### func \(Resources\) [Match](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resources.go#L190>)

```go
func (r Resources) Match(pattern any) Resources
```

Match gets all resources matching the given base filename prefix, e.g "\*.png" will match all png files. The "\*" does not match path delimiters \(/\), so if you organize your resources in sub\-folders, you need to be explicit about it, e.g.: "images/\*.png". To match any PNG image anywhere in the bundle you can do "\*\*.png", and to match all PNG images below the images folder, use "images/\*\*.jpg". The matching is case insensitive. Match matches by using the value of Resource.Name, which, by default, is a filename with path relative to the bundle root with Unix style slashes \(/\) and no leading slash, e.g. "images/logo.png". See https://github.com/gobwas/glob for the full rules set.

<a name="Resources.MergeByLanguage"></a>
### func \(Resources\) [MergeByLanguage](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resources.go#L225>)

```go
func (r Resources) MergeByLanguage(r2 Resources) Resources
```

MergeByLanguage adds missing translations in r1 from r2.

<a name="Resources.MergeByLanguageInterface"></a>
### func \(Resources\) [MergeByLanguageInterface](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resources.go#L247>)

```go
func (r Resources) MergeByLanguageInterface(in any) (any, error)
```

MergeByLanguageInterface is the generic version of MergeByLanguage. It is here just so it can be called from the tpl package. This is for internal use.

<a name="Resources.Mount"></a>
### func \(Resources\) [Mount](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/resources/resource/resources.go#L39>)

```go
func (r Resources) Mount(base, target string) ResourceGetter
```

Mount mounts the given resources from base to the given target path. Note that leading slashes in target marks an absolute path. This method is currently only useful in js.Batch.

<a name="Namespace"></a>
## type [Namespace](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L57-L62>)

Namespace provides template functions for the "collections" namespace.

```go
type Namespace struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L39>)

```go
func New(deps *deps.Deps) *Namespace
```

New returns a new instance of the collections\-namespaced template functions.

<a name="Namespace.After"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [After](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L65>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) After(n any, l any) (any, error)
```

After returns all the items after the first n items in list l.

<a name="Namespace.Append"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Append](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/append.go#L31>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Append(args ...any) (any, error)
```

Append appends args up to the last one to the slice in the last argument. This construct allows template constructs like this:

```
{{ $pages = $pages | append $p2 $p1 }}
```

Note that with 2 arguments where both are slices of the same type, the first slice will be appended to the second:

```
{{ $pages = $pages | append .Site.RegularPages }}
```

<a name="Namespace.Apply"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Apply](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/apply.go#L27>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Apply(ctx context.Context, c any, fname string, args ...any) (any, error)
```

Apply takes an array or slice c and returns a new slice with the function fname applied over it.

<a name="Namespace.Complement"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Complement](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/complement.go#L32>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Complement(ls ...any) (any, error)
```

Complement gives the elements in the last element of ls that are not in any of the others.

All elements of ls must be slices or arrays of comparable types.

The reasoning behind this rather clumsy API is so we can do this in the templates:

```
{{ $c := .Pages | complement $last4 }}
```

<a name="Namespace.D"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [D](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L555>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) D(seed, n, hi any) ([]int, error)
```

D returns a sorted slice of unique random integers in the half\-open interval \[0, hi\) using the provided seed value. The number of elements in the resulting slice is n or hi, whichever is less.

If n \<= hi, it returns a sorted random sample of size n using J. S. Vitter’s Method D for sequential random sampling.

If n \> hi, it returns the full, sorted range \[0, hi\) of size hi.

If n == 0 or hi == 0, it returns an empty slice.

Reference:

```
J. S. Vitter, "An efficient algorithm for sequential random sampling," ACM Trans. Math. Softw., vol. 11, no. 1, pp. 37–57, 1985.
See also: https://getkerf.wordpress.com/2016/03/30/the-best-algorithm-no-one-knows-about/
```

<a name="Namespace.Delimit"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Delimit](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L101>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Delimit(ctx context.Context, l, sep any, last ...any) (string, error)
```

Delimit takes a given list l and returns a string delimited by sep. If last is passed to the function, it will be used as the final delimiter.

<a name="Namespace.Dictionary"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Dictionary](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L160>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Dictionary(values ...any) (map[string]any, error)
```

Dictionary creates a new map from the given parameters by treating values as key\-value pairs. The number of values must be even. The keys can be string slices, which will create the needed nested structure.

<a name="Namespace.First"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [First](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L197>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) First(limit any, l any) (any, error)
```

First returns the first limit items in list l.

<a name="Namespace.Group"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Group](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L312>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Group(key any, items any) (any, error)
```

Group groups a set of items by the given key. This is currently only supported for Pages.

<a name="Namespace.In"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [In](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L232>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) In(l any, v any) (bool, error)
```

In returns whether v is in the list l. l may be an array or slice.

<a name="Namespace.Index"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Index](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/index.go#L34>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Index(item any, args ...any) (any, error)
```

Index returns the result of indexing its first argument by the following arguments. Thus "index x 1 2 3" is, in Go syntax, x\[1\]\[2\]\[3\]. Each indexed item must be a map, slice, or array.

Adapted from Go stdlib src/text/template/funcs.go.

We deviate from the stdlib mostly because of https://github.com/golang/go/issues/14751.

<a name="Namespace.Intersect"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Intersect](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L271>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Intersect(l1, l2 any) (any, error)
```

Intersect returns the common elements in the given sets, l1 and l2. l1 and l2 must be of the same type and may be either arrays or slices.

<a name="Namespace.IsSet"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [IsSet](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L332>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) IsSet(c any, key any) (bool, error)
```

IsSet returns whether a given array, channel, slice, or map in c has the given key defined.

<a name="Namespace.KeyVals"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [KeyVals](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L755>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) KeyVals(key any, values ...any) (types.KeyValues, error)
```

KeyVals creates a key and values wrapper.

<a name="Namespace.Last"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Last](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L357>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Last(limit any, l any) (any, error)
```

Last returns the last limit items in the list l.

<a name="Namespace.Merge"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Merge](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/merge.go#L30>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Merge(params ...any) (any, error)
```

Merge creates a copy of the final parameter in params and merges the preceding parameters into it in reverse order.

Currently only maps are supported. Key handling is case insensitive.

<a name="Namespace.NewScratch"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [NewScratch](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L761>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) NewScratch() *hstore.Scratch
```

NewScratch creates a new Scratch which can be used to store values in a thread safe way.

<a name="Namespace.Querify"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Querify](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/querify.go#L31>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Querify(params ...any) (string, error)
```

Querify returns a URL query string composed of the given key\-value pairs, encoded and sorted by key.

<a name="Namespace.Reverse"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Reverse](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L392>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Reverse(l any) (any, error)
```

Reverse creates a copy of the list l and reverses it.

<a name="Namespace.Seq"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Seq](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L428>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Seq(args ...any) ([]int, error)
```

Seq creates a sequence of integers from args. It's named and used as GNU's seq.

Examples:

```
3 => 1, 2, 3
1 2 4 => 1, 3
-3 => -1, -2, -3
1 4 => 1, 2, 3, 4
1 -2 => 1, 0, -1, -2
```

<a name="Namespace.Shuffle"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Shuffle](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L496>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Shuffle(l any) (any, error)
```

Shuffle returns list l in a randomized order.

<a name="Namespace.Slice"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Slice](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L526>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Slice(args ...any) any
```

Slice returns a slice of all passed arguments.

<a name="Namespace.Sort"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Sort](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/sort.go#L31>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Sort(ctx context.Context, l any, args ...any) (any, error)
```

Sort returns a sorted copy of the list l.

<a name="Namespace.SymDiff"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [SymDiff](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/symdiff.go#L25>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) SymDiff(s2, s1 any) (any, error)
```

SymDiff returns the symmetric difference of s1 and s2. Arguments must be either a slice or an array of comparable types.

<a name="Namespace.Union"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Union](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L637>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Union(l1, l2 any) (any, error)
```

Union returns the union of the given sets, l1 and l2. l1 and l2 must be of the same type and may be either arrays or slices. If l1 and l2 aren't of the same type then l1 will be returned. If either l1 or l2 is nil then the non\-nil list will be returned.

<a name="Namespace.Uniq"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Uniq](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/collections.go#L720>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Uniq(l any) (any, error)
```

Uniq returns a new list with duplicate elements in the list l removed.

<a name="Namespace.Where"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Where](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/collections/where.go#L29>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Where(ctx context.Context, c, key any, args ...any) (any, error)
```

Where returns a filtered subset of collection c.

# compare

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/tpl/compare"
```

Package compare provides template functions for comparing values.

## Index

- [type Namespace](<#Namespace>)
  - [func New\(loc \*time.Location, caseInsensitive bool\) \*Namespace](<#New>)
  - [func \(n \*Namespace\) Conditional\(cond any, v1, v2 any\) any](<#Namespace.Conditional>)
  - [func \(\*Namespace\) Default\(defaultv any, givenv ...any\) \(any, error\)](<#Namespace.Default>)
  - [func \(n \*Namespace\) Eq\(first any, others ...any\) bool](<#Namespace.Eq>)
  - [func \(n \*Namespace\) Ge\(first any, others ...any\) bool](<#Namespace.Ge>)
  - [func \(n \*Namespace\) Gt\(first any, others ...any\) bool](<#Namespace.Gt>)
  - [func \(n \*Namespace\) Le\(first any, others ...any\) bool](<#Namespace.Le>)
  - [func \(n \*Namespace\) Lt\(first any, others ...any\) bool](<#Namespace.Lt>)
  - [func \(n \*Namespace\) LtCollate\(collator \*langs.Collator, first any, others ...any\) bool](<#Namespace.LtCollate>)
  - [func \(n \*Namespace\) Ne\(first any, others ...any\) bool](<#Namespace.Ne>)


<a name="Namespace"></a>
## type [Namespace](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/compare/compare.go#L38-L42>)

Namespace provides template functions for the "compare" namespace.

```go
type Namespace struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/compare/compare.go#L33>)

```go
func New(loc *time.Location, caseInsensitive bool) *Namespace
```

New returns a new instance of the compare\-namespaced template functions.

<a name="Namespace.Conditional"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Conditional](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/compare/compare.go#L234>)

```go
func (n *Namespace) Conditional(cond any, v1, v2 any) any
```

Conditional can be used as a ternary operator.

It returns v1 if cond is true, else v2.

<a name="Namespace.Default"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Default](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/compare/compare.go#L48>)

```go
func (*Namespace) Default(defaultv any, givenv ...any) (any, error)
```

Default checks whether a givenv is set and returns the default value defaultv if it is not. "Set" in this context means non\-zero for numeric types and times; non\-zero length for strings, arrays, slices, and maps; any boolean or struct value; or non\-nil for any other types.

<a name="Namespace.Eq"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Eq](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/compare/compare.go#L99>)

```go
func (n *Namespace) Eq(first any, others ...any) bool
```

Eq returns the boolean truth of arg1 == arg2 || arg1 == arg3 || arg1 == arg4.

<a name="Namespace.Ge"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Ge](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/compare/compare.go#L170>)

```go
func (n *Namespace) Ge(first any, others ...any) bool
```

Ge returns the boolean truth of arg1 \>= arg2 && arg1 \>= arg3 && arg1 \>= arg4.

<a name="Namespace.Gt"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Gt](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/compare/compare.go#L182>)

```go
func (n *Namespace) Gt(first any, others ...any) bool
```

Gt returns the boolean truth of arg1 \> arg2 && arg1 \> arg3 && arg1 \> arg4.

<a name="Namespace.Le"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Le](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/compare/compare.go#L194>)

```go
func (n *Namespace) Le(first any, others ...any) bool
```

Le returns the boolean truth of arg1 \<= arg2 && arg1 \<= arg3 && arg1 \<= arg4.

<a name="Namespace.Lt"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Lt](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/compare/compare.go#L220>)

```go
func (n *Namespace) Lt(first any, others ...any) bool
```

Lt returns the boolean truth of arg1 \< arg2 && arg1 \< arg3 && arg1 \< arg4.

<a name="Namespace.LtCollate"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [LtCollate](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/compare/compare.go#L208>)

```go
func (n *Namespace) LtCollate(collator *langs.Collator, first any, others ...any) bool
```

LtCollate returns the boolean truth of arg1 \< arg2 && arg1 \< arg3 && arg1 \< arg4. The provided collator will be used for string comparisons. This is for internal use.

<a name="Namespace.Ne"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Ne](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/compare/compare.go#L159>)

```go
func (n *Namespace) Ne(first any, others ...any) bool
```

Ne returns the boolean truth of arg1 \!= arg2 && arg1 \!= arg3 && arg1 \!= arg4.

<a name="New"></a>

```go

```

<a name="Namespace.Dump"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Dump](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/debug/debug.go#L109>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Dump(val any) string
```

Dump returns a object dump of val as a string. Note that not every value passed to Dump will print so nicely, but we'll improve on that.

We recommend using the "go" Chroma lexer to format the output nicely.

Also note that the output from Dump may change from Hugo version to the next, so don't depend on a specific output.

<a name="Timer"></a>
## type [Timer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/debug/debug.go#L143-L148>)

Timer is a timer that can be stopped.

```go
type Timer interface {
    // Stop stops the timer and returns an empty string.
    // Stop can be called multiple times, but only the first call will stop the timer.
    // If Stop is not called, the timer will be stopped when the build ends.
    Stop() string
}
```

<a name="SVGDiagram"></a>
## type [SVGDiagram](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/diagrams/diagrams.go#L20-L33>)



```go
type SVGDiagram interface {
    // Wrapped returns the diagram as an SVG, including the <svg> container.
    Wrapped() template.HTML

    // Inner returns the inner markup of the SVG.
    // This allows for the <svg> container to be created manually.
    Inner() template.HTML

    // Width returns the width of the SVG.
    Width() int

    // Height returns the height of the SVG.
    Height() int
}
```

<a name="Namespace.Base64Decode"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Base64Decode](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/encoding/encoding.go#L39>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Base64Decode(content any) (string, error)
```

<a name="Namespace.Base64Encode"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Base64Encode](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/encoding/encoding.go#L50>)


<a name="Namespace.Jsonify"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Jsonify](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/encoding/encoding.go#L64>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Jsonify(args ...any) (template.HTML, error)
```

Jsonify encodes a given object to JSON. To pretty print the JSON, pass a map or dictionary of options as the first value in args. Supported options are "prefix" and "indent". Each JSON element in the output will begin on a new line beginning with prefix followed by one or more copies of indent according to the indentation nesting.


## Variables

<a name="TemplateFuncsNamespaceRegistry"></a>TemplateFuncsNamespaceRegistry describes a registry of functions that provide namespaces.

```go
var TemplateFuncsNamespaceRegistry []func(d *deps.Deps) *TemplateFuncsNamespace
```

<a name="AddTemplateFuncsNamespace"></a>
## func [AddTemplateFuncsNamespace](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/internal/templatefuncsRegistry.go#L43>)

```go
func AddTemplateFuncsNamespace(ns func(d *deps.Deps) *TemplateFuncsNamespace)
```

AddTemplateFuncsNamespace adds a given function to a registry.

<a name="TemplateFuncMethodMapping"></a>
## type [TemplateFuncMethodMapping](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/internal/templatefuncsRegistry.go#L99-L114>)

TemplateFuncMethodMapping represents a mapping of functions to methods for a given namespace.

```go
type TemplateFuncMethodMapping struct {
    Method any

    // Any template funcs aliases. This is mainly motivated by keeping
    // backwards compatibility, but some new template funcs may also make
    // sense to give short and snappy aliases.
    // Note that these aliases are global and will be merged, so the last
    // key will win.
    Aliases []string

    // A slice of input/expected examples.
    // We keep it a the namespace level for now, but may find a way to keep track
    // of the single template func, for documentation purposes.
    // Some of these, hopefully just a few, may depend on some test data to run.
    Examples [][2]string
}
```

<a name="TemplateFuncsNamespace"></a>
## type [TemplateFuncsNamespace](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/internal/templatefuncsRegistry.go#L48-L60>)

TemplateFuncsNamespace represents a template function namespace.

```go
type TemplateFuncsNamespace struct {
    // The namespace name, "strings", "lang", etc.
    Name string

    // This is the method receiver.
    Context func(ctx context.Context, v ...any) (any, error)

    // OnCreated is called when all the namespaces are ready.
    OnCreated func(namespaces map[string]any)

    // Additional info, aliases and examples, per method name.
    MethodMappings map[string]TemplateFuncMethodMapping
}
```

<a name="TemplateFuncsNamespace.AddMethodMapping"></a>
### func \(\*TemplateFuncsNamespace\) [AddMethodMapping](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/internal/templatefuncsRegistry.go#L66>)

```go
func (t *TemplateFuncsNamespace) AddMethodMapping(m any, aliases []string, examples [][2]string)
```

AddMethodMapping adds a method to a template function namespace.

<a name="TemplateFuncsNamespaces"></a>
## type [TemplateFuncsNamespaces](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/internal/templatefuncsRegistry.go#L63>)

TemplateFuncsNamespaces is a slice of TemplateFuncsNamespace.

```go
type TemplateFuncsNamespaces []*TemplateFuncsNamespace
```

<a name="TemplateFuncsNamespaces.MarshalJSON"></a>
### func \(TemplateFuncsNamespaces\) [MarshalJSON](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/internal/templatefuncsRegistry.go#L170>)

```go
func (namespaces TemplateFuncsNamespaces) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error)
```

MarshalJSON returns the JSON encoding of namespaces.

<a name="TemplateFuncsNamespaces.ToMap"></a>
### func \(TemplateFuncsNamespaces\) [ToMap](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/internal/templatefuncsRegistry.go#L154>)

```go
func (namespaces TemplateFuncsNamespaces) ToMap() map[string]any
```


<a name="Namespace.FormatAccounting"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [FormatAccounting](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/lang/lang.go#L99>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) FormatAccounting(precision, currency, number any) (string, error)
```

FormatAccounting returns the currency representation of number for the given currency and precision for the current language in accounting notation.

The return value is formatted with at least two decimal places.

<a name="Namespace.FormatCurrency"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [FormatCurrency](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/lang/lang.go#L87>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) FormatCurrency(precision, currency, number any) (string, error)
```

FormatCurrency returns the currency representation of number for the given currency and precision for the current language.

The return value is formatted with at least two decimal places.

<a name="Namespace.FormatNumber"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [FormatNumber](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/lang/lang.go#L65>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) FormatNumber(precision, number any) (string, error)
```

FormatNumber formats number with the given precision for the current language.

<a name="Namespace.FormatNumberCustom"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [FormatNumberCustom](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/lang/lang.go#L134>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) FormatNumberCustom(precision, number any, options ...any) (string, error)
```

FormatNumberCustom formats a number with the given precision. The first options parameter is a space\-delimited string of characters to represent negativity, the decimal point, and grouping. The default value is \`\- . ,\`. The second options parameter defines an alternate delimiting character.

Note that numbers are rounded up at 5 or greater. So, with precision set to 0, 1.5 becomes \`2\`, and 1.4 becomes \`1\`.

For a simpler function that adapts to the current language, see FormatNumber.

<a name="Namespace.FormatPercent"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [FormatPercent](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/lang/lang.go#L75>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) FormatPercent(precision, number any) (string, error)
```

FormatPercent formats number with the given precision for the current language. Note that the number is assumed to be a percentage.

<a name="Namespace.Merge"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Merge](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/lang/lang.go#L237>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Merge(p2, p1 any) (any, error)
```

Merge creates a union of pages from two languages.



```go
func (ns *Namespace) Atan2(n, m any) (float64, error)
```

Atan2 returns the arc tangent of n/m, using the signs of the two to determine the quadrant of the return value.


```go
func (ns *Namespace) Cos(n any) (float64, error)
```

<a name="Namespace.Counter"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Counter](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/math/math.go#L363>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Counter() uint64
```

Counter increments and returns a global counter. This was originally added to be used in tests where now.UnixNano did not have the needed precision \(especially on Windows\). Note that given the parallel nature of Hugo, you cannot use this to get sequences of numbers, and the counter will reset on new builds. \<docsmeta\>\{"identifiers": \["now.UnixNano"\] \}\</docsmeta\>

Div divides n1 by n2.

```go
type Namespace struct {
```

<a name="Namespace.FileExists"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [FileExists](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/os/os.go#L128>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) FileExists(i any) (bool, error)
```

FileExists checks whether a file exists under the given path.

<a name="Namespace.Getenv"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Getenv](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/os/os.go#L63>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Getenv(key any) (string, error)
```

Getenv retrieves the value of the environment variable named by the key. It returns the value, which will be empty if the variable is not present.

<a name="Namespace.ReadDir"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [ReadDir](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/os/os.go#L113>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) ReadDir(i any) ([]_os.FileInfo, error)
```

ReadDir lists the directory contents relative to the configured WorkingDir.

<a name="Namespace.ReadFile"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [ReadFile](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/os/os.go#L95>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) ReadFile(i any) (string, error)
```

ReadFile reads the file named by filename relative to the configured WorkingDir. It returns the contents as a string. There is an upper size limit set at 1 megabytes.

<a name="Namespace.Stat"></a>

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Stat(i any) (_os.FileInfo, error)
```

Stat returns the os.FileInfo structure describing file.

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/tpl/partials"
```

Package partials provides template functions for working with reusable templates.

## type [Namespace](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/partials/partials.go#L89-L92>)

Namespace provides template functions for the "templates" namespace.

```go
type Namespace struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/partials/partials.go#L69>)

```go
func New(deps *deps.Deps) *Namespace
```

New returns a new instance of the templates\-namespaced template functions.

<a name="Namespace.Include"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Include](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/partials/partials.go#L111>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Include(ctx context.Context, name string, contextList ...any) (any, error)
```

Include executes the named partial. If the partial contains a return statement, that value will be returned. Else, the rendered output will be returned: A string if the partial is a text/template, or template.HTML when html/template. Note that ctx is provided by Hugo, not the end user.

<a name="Namespace.IncludeCached"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [IncludeCached](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/partials/partials.go#L205>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) IncludeCached(ctx context.Context, name string, context any, variants ...any) (any, error)
```

IncludeCached executes and caches partial templates. The cache is created with name\+variants as the key. Note that ctx is provided by Hugo, not the end user.

# path

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/tpl/path"
```

Package path provides template functions for manipulating paths.

## Index

- [type Namespace](<#Namespace>)
  - [func New\(deps \*deps.Deps\) \*Namespace](<#New>)
  - [func \(ns \*Namespace\) Base\(path any\) \(string, error\)](<#Namespace.Base>)
  - [func \(ns \*Namespace\) BaseName\(path any\) \(string, error\)](<#Namespace.BaseName>)
  - [func \(ns \*Namespace\) Clean\(path any\) \(string, error\)](<#Namespace.Clean>)
  - [func \(ns \*Namespace\) Dir\(path any\) \(string, error\)](<#Namespace.Dir>)
  - [func \(ns \*Namespace\) Ext\(path any\) \(string, error\)](<#Namespace.Ext>)
  - [func \(ns \*Namespace\) Join\(elements ...any\) \(string, error\)](<#Namespace.Join>)
  - [func \(ns \*Namespace\) Split\(path any\) \(paths.DirFile, error\)](<#Namespace.Split>)


<a name="Namespace"></a>
## type [Namespace](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/path/path.go#L35-L37>)

Namespace provides template functions for the "os" namespace.

```go
type Namespace struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/path/path.go#L28>)

```go
func New(deps *deps.Deps) *Namespace
```

New returns a new instance of the path\-namespaced template functions.

<a name="Namespace.Base"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Base](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/path/path.go#L78>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Base(path any) (string, error)
```

Base returns the last element of path. Trailing slashes are removed before extracting the last element. If the path is empty, Base returns ".". If the path consists entirely of slashes, Base returns "/". The input path is passed into filepath.ToSlash converting any Windows slashes to forward slashes.

<a name="Namespace.BaseName"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [BaseName](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/path/path.go#L93>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) BaseName(path any) (string, error)
```

BaseName returns the last element of path, removing the extension if present. Trailing slashes are removed before extracting the last element. If the path is empty, Base returns ".". If the path consists entirely of slashes, Base returns "/". The input path is passed into filepath.ToSlash converting any Windows slashes to forward slashes.

<a name="Namespace.Clean"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Clean](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/path/path.go#L155>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Clean(path any) (string, error)
```

Clean replaces the separators used with standard slashes and then extraneous slashes are removed.

<a name="Namespace.Dir"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Dir](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/path/path.go#L63>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Dir(path any) (string, error)
```

Dir returns all but the last element of path, typically the path's directory. After dropping the final element using Split, the path is Cleaned and trailing slashes are removed. If the path is empty, Dir returns ".". If the path consists entirely of slashes followed by non\-slash bytes, Dir returns a single slash. In any other case, the returned path does not end in a slash. The input path is passed into filepath.ToSlash converting any Windows slashes to forward slashes.

<a name="Namespace.Ext"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Ext](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/path/path.go#L45>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Ext(path any) (string, error)
```

Ext returns the file name extension used by path. The extension is the suffix beginning at the final dot in the final slash\-separated element of path; it is empty if there is no dot. The input path is passed into filepath.ToSlash converting any Windows slashes to forward slashes.

<a name="Namespace.Join"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Join](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/path/path.go#L126>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Join(elements ...any) (string, error)
```

Join joins any number of path elements into a single path, adding a separating slash if necessary. All the input path elements are passed into filepath.ToSlash converting any Windows slashes to forward slashes. The result is Cleaned; in particular, all empty strings are ignored.

<a name="Namespace.Split"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Split](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/path/path.go#L109>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Split(path any) (paths.DirFile, error)
```

Split splits path immediately following the final slash, separating it into a directory and file name component. If there is no slash in path, Split returns an empty dir and file set to path. The input path is passed into filepath.ToSlash converting any Windows slashes to forward slashes. The returned values have the property that path = dir\+file.

<a name="Namespace.IsImageResource"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [IsImageResource](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/reflect/reflect.go#L61>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) IsImageResource(v any) bool
```

IsImageResource reports whether v is a Image Resource.

<a name="Namespace.IsImageResourceProcessable"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [IsImageResourceProcessable](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/reflect/reflect.go#L72>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) IsImageResourceProcessable(v any) bool
```

IsImageResourceProcessable reports whether v is a Image Resource that supports all image processing operations like Resize, Crop, etc. in addition to the metadata operations.

<a name="Namespace.IsImageResourceWithMeta"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [IsImageResourceWithMeta](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/reflect/reflect.go#L67>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) IsImageResourceWithMeta(v any) bool
```

IsImageResourceWithMeta reports whether v is a Image Resource that supports at least the image metadata operations Width and Height and Meta \(for e.g. Exif\). This will return true for AVIF, HEIF and HEIC image resources, even if we don't yet support image operations like Resize, Crop, etc. on these formats.


IsMap reports whether v is a map.

IsPage reports whether v is a Hugo Page.

### func \(\*Namespace\) [ByType](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/resources/resources.go#L164>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) ByType(typ any) resource.Resources
```

ByType returns resources of a given resource type \(e.g. "image"\).

<a name="Namespace.Concat"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Concat](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/resources/resources.go#L200>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Concat(targetPathIn any, r any) (resource.Resource, error)
```

Concat concatenates a slice of Resource objects. These resources must \(currently\) be of the same Media Type.

<a name="Namespace.Copy"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Copy](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/resources/resources.go#L74>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Copy(s any, r resource.Resource) (resource.Resource, error)
```

Copy copies r to the new targetPath in s.

<a name="Namespace.ExecuteAsTemplate"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [ExecuteAsTemplate](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/resources/resources.go#L240>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) ExecuteAsTemplate(ctx context.Context, args ...any) (resource.Resource, error)
```

ExecuteAsTemplate creates a Resource from a Go template, parsed and executed with the given data, and published to the relative target path.

<a name="Namespace.Fingerprint"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Fingerprint](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/resources/resources.go#L260>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Fingerprint(args ...any) (resource.Resource, error)
```

Fingerprint transforms the given Resource with a MD5 hash of the content in the RelPermalink and Permalink.

<a name="Namespace.FromString"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [FromString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/resources/resources.go#L225>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) FromString(targetPathIn, contentIn any) (resource.Resource, error)
```

FromString creates a Resource from a string published to the relative target path.

<a name="Namespace.Get"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Get](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/resources/resources.go#L84>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Get(filename any) resource.Resource
```

Get locates the filename given in Hugo's assets filesystem and creates a Resource object that can be used for further transformations.

<a name="Namespace.GetMatch"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [GetMatch](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/resources/resources.go#L149>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) GetMatch(pattern any) resource.Resource
```

GetMatch finds the first Resource matching the given pattern, or nil if none found.

It looks for files in the assets file system.

See Match for a more complete explanation about the rules used.

<a name="Namespace.GetRemote"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [GetRemote](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/resources/resources.go#L109>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) GetRemote(args ...any) (resource.Resource, error)
```

GetRemote gets the URL \(via HTTP\(s\)\) in the first argument in args and creates Resource object that can be used for further transformations.

A second argument may be provided with an option map.

Note: This method does not return any error as a second return value, for any error situations the error can be checked in .Err.

<a name="Namespace.Match"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Match](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/resources/resources.go#L184>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Match(pattern any) resource.Resources
```

Match gets all resources matching the given base path prefix, e.g "\*.png" will match all png files. The "\*" does not match path delimiters \(/\), so if you organize your resources in sub\-folders, you need to be explicit about it, e.g.: "images/\*.png". To match any PNG image anywhere in the bundle you can do "\*\*.png", and to match all PNG images below the images folder, use "images/\*\*.jpg".

The matching is case insensitive.

Match matches by using the files name with path relative to the file system root with Unix style slashes \(/\) and no leading slash, e.g. "images/logo.png".

See https://github.com/gobwas/glob for the full rules set.

It looks for files in the assets file system.

See Match for a more complete explanation about the rules used.

<a name="Namespace.Minify"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Minify](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/resources/resources.go#L291>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Minify(r resources.ResourceTransformer) (resource.Resource, error)
```

Minify minifies the given Resource using the MediaType to pick the correct minifier.

<a name="Namespace.PostProcess"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [PostProcess](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/resources/resources.go#L296>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) PostProcess(r resource.Resource) (postpub.PostPublishedResource, error)
```

PostProcess processes r after the build.

<a name="Namespace"></a>
## type [Namespace](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L48-L51>)

Namespace provides template functions for the "strings" namespace. Most functions mimic the Go stdlib, but the order of the parameters may be different to ease their use in the Go template system.

```go
type Namespace struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L38>)

```go
func New(d *deps.Deps) *Namespace
```

New returns a new instance of the strings\-namespaced template functions.

<a name="Namespace.Chomp"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Chomp](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L123>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Chomp(s any) (any, error)
```

Chomp returns a copy of s with all trailing newline characters removed.

<a name="Namespace.Contains"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Contains](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L139>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Contains(s, substr any) (bool, error)
```

Contains reports whether substr is in s.

<a name="Namespace.ContainsAny"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [ContainsAny](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L154>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) ContainsAny(s, chars any) (bool, error)
```

ContainsAny reports whether any Unicode code points in chars are within s.

<a name="Namespace.ContainsNonSpace"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [ContainsNonSpace](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L171>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) ContainsNonSpace(s any) (bool, error)
```

ContainsNonSpace reports whether s contains any non\-space characters as defined by Unicode's White Space property, \<docsmeta\>\{"newIn": "0.111.0" \}\</docsmeta\>

<a name="Namespace.Count"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Count](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L110>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Count(substr, s any) (int, error)
```

Count counts the number of non\-overlapping instances of substr in s. If substr is an empty string, Count returns 1 \+ the number of Unicode code points in s.

<a name="Namespace.CountRunes"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [CountRunes](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L54>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) CountRunes(s any) (int, error)
```

CountRunes returns the number of runes in s, excluding whitespace.

<a name="Namespace.CountWords"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [CountWords](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L80>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) CountWords(s any) (int, error)
```

CountWords returns the approximate word count in s.

<a name="Namespace.Diff"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Diff](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L187>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Diff(oldname string, old any, newname string, new any) (string, error)
```

Diff returns an anchored diff of the two texts old and new in the “unified diff” format. If old and new are identical, Diff returns an empty string.

<a name="Namespace.FindRE"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [FindRE](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/regexp.go#L23>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) FindRE(expr string, content any, limit ...any) ([]string, error)
```

FindRE returns a list of strings that match the regular expression. By default all matches will be included. The number of matches can be limited with an optional third parameter.

<a name="Namespace.FindRESubmatch"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [FindRESubmatch](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/regexp.go#L54>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) FindRESubmatch(expr string, content any, limit ...any) ([][]string, error)
```

FindRESubmatch returns a slice of all successive matches of the regular expression in content. Each element is a slice of strings holding the text of the leftmost match of the regular expression and the matches, if any, of its subexpressions.

By default all matches will be included. The number of matches can be limited with the optional limit parameter. A return value of nil indicates no match.

<a name="Namespace.FirstUpper"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [FirstUpper](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L467>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) FirstUpper(s any) (string, error)
```

FirstUpper converts s making the first character upper case.

<a name="Namespace.HasPrefix"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [HasPrefix](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L200>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) HasPrefix(s, prefix any) (bool, error)
```

HasPrefix tests whether the input s begins with prefix.

<a name="Namespace.HasSuffix"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [HasSuffix](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L215>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) HasSuffix(s, suffix any) (bool, error)
```

HasSuffix tests whether the input s begins with suffix.

<a name="Namespace.Repeat"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Repeat](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L590>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Repeat(n, s any) (string, error)
```

Repeat returns a new string consisting of n copies of the string s.

<a name="Namespace.Replace"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Replace](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L232>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Replace(s, old, new any, limit ...any) (string, error)
```

Replace returns a copy of the string s with all occurrences of old replaced with new. The number of replacements can be limited with an optional fourth parameter.

<a name="Namespace.ReplacePairs"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [ReplacePairs](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L263>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) ReplacePairs(args ...any) (string, error)
```

ReplacePairs returns a copy of a string with multiple replacements performed in a single pass. The last argument is the source string. Preceding arguments are old/new string pairs, either as a slice or as individual arguments.

<a name="Namespace.ReplaceRE"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [ReplaceRE](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/regexp.go#L78>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) ReplaceRE(pattern, repl, s any, n ...any) (_ string, err error)
```

ReplaceRE returns a copy of s, replacing all matches of the regular expression pattern with the replacement text repl. The number of replacements can be limited with an optional fourth parameter.

<a name="Namespace.RuneCount"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [RuneCount](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L71>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) RuneCount(s any) (int, error)
```

RuneCount returns the number of runes in s.

<a name="Namespace.SliceString"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [SliceString](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L318>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) SliceString(a any, startEnd ...any) (string, error)
```

SliceString slices a string by specifying a half\-open range with two indices, start and end. 1 and 4 creates a slice including elements 1 through 3. The end index can be omitted, it defaults to the string's length.

<a name="Namespace.Split"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Split](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L362>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Split(a any, delimiter string) ([]string, error)
```

Split slices an input string into all substrings separated by delimiter.

<a name="Namespace.Substr"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Substr](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L383>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Substr(a any, nums ...any) (string, error)
```

Substr extracts parts of a string, beginning at the character at the specified position, and returns the specified number of characters.

It normally takes two parameters: start and length. It can also take one parameter: start, i.e. length is omitted, in which case the substring starting from start until the end of the string will be returned.

To extract characters from the end of the string, use a negative start number.

In addition, borrowing from the extended behavior described at http://php.net/substr, if length is given and is negative, then that many characters will be omitted from the end of string.

<a name="Namespace.Title"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Title](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L458>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Title(s any) (string, error)
```

Title returns a copy of the input s with all Unicode letters that begin words mapped to their title case.

<a name="Namespace.ToLower"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [ToLower](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L478>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) ToLower(s any) (string, error)
```

ToLower returns a copy of the input s with all Unicode letters mapped to their lower case.

<a name="Namespace.ToUpper"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [ToUpper](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L489>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) ToUpper(s any) (string, error)
```

ToUpper returns a copy of the input s with all Unicode letters mapped to their upper case.

<a name="Namespace.Trim"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Trim](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L500>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Trim(s, cutset any) (string, error)
```

Trim returns converts the strings s removing all leading and trailing characters defined contained.

<a name="Namespace.TrimLeft"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [TrimLeft](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L527>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) TrimLeft(cutset, s any) (string, error)
```

TrimLeft returns a slice of the string s with all leading characters contained in cutset removed.

<a name="Namespace.TrimPrefix"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [TrimPrefix](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L543>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) TrimPrefix(prefix, s any) (string, error)
```

TrimPrefix returns s without the provided leading prefix string. If s doesn't start with prefix, s is returned unchanged.

<a name="Namespace.TrimRight"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [TrimRight](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L559>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) TrimRight(cutset, s any) (string, error)
```

TrimRight returns a slice of the string s with all trailing characters contained in cutset removed.

<a name="Namespace.TrimSpace"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [TrimSpace](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L516>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) TrimSpace(s any) (string, error)
```

TrimSpace returns the given string, removing leading and trailing whitespace as defined by Unicode.

<a name="Namespace.TrimSuffix"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [TrimSuffix](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/strings.go#L575>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) TrimSuffix(suffix, s any) (string, error)
```

TrimSuffix returns s without the provided trailing suffix string. If s doesn't end with suffix, s is returned unchanged.

<a name="Namespace.Truncate"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Truncate](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/strings/truncate.go#L44>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Truncate(s any, options ...any) (template.HTML, error)
```

Truncate truncates the string in s to the specified length.


<a name="Namespace.DoDefer"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [DoDefer](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/templates/templates.go#L123>)

```go

```

<a name="Namespace.Exists"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Exists](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/templates/templates.go#L50>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Exists(name string) bool
```

Exists returns whether the template with the given name exists. Note that this is the Unix\-styled relative path including filename suffix, e.g. partials/header.html

<a name="Namespace.Inner"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Inner](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/templates/templates.go#L79>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Inner(ctx context.Context, data any) (any, error)
```

Inner executes the inner content of a partial decorator. Note that there is only one inner block per partial decorator, but inner may be called multiple times with, typically, different data.

# time

```go
import "github.com/gohugoio/hugo/tpl/time"
```

Package time provides template functions for measuring and displaying time.

## Index

- [type Namespace](<#Namespace>)
  - [func New\(timeFormatter htime.TimeFormatter, location \*time.Location, deps \*deps.Deps\) \*Namespace](<#New>)
  - [func \(ns \*Namespace\) AsTime\(v any, args ...any\) \(any, error\)](<#Namespace.AsTime>)
  - [func \(ns \*Namespace\) Duration\(unit any, number any\) \(time.Duration, error\)](<#Namespace.Duration>)
  - [func \(ns \*Namespace\) Format\(layout string, v any\) \(string, error\)](<#Namespace.Format>)
  - [func \(ns \*Namespace\) In\(timeZoneName string, t time.Time\) \(time.Time, error\)](<#Namespace.In>)
  - [func \(ns \*Namespace\) Now\(\) time.Time](<#Namespace.Now>)
  - [func \(ns \*Namespace\) ParseDuration\(s any\) \(time.Duration, error\)](<#Namespace.ParseDuration>)


<a name="Namespace"></a>
## type [Namespace](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/time/time.go#L47-L52>)

Namespace provides template functions for the "time" namespace.

```go
type Namespace struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/time/time.go#L29>)

```go
func New(timeFormatter htime.TimeFormatter, location *time.Location, deps *deps.Deps) *Namespace
```

New returns a new instance of the time\-namespaced template functions.

<a name="Namespace.AsTime"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [AsTime](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/time/time.go#L56>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) AsTime(v any, args ...any) (any, error)
```

AsTime converts the textual representation of the datetime string into a time.Time interface.

<a name="Namespace.Duration"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Duration](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/time/time.go#L136>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Duration(unit any, number any) (time.Duration, error)
```

Duration converts the given number to a time.Duration. Unit is one of nanosecond/ns, microsecond/us/µs, millisecond/ms, second/s, minute/m or hour/h.

<a name="Namespace.Format"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Format](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/time/time.go#L74>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Format(layout string, v any) (string, error)
```

Format converts the textual representation of the datetime string in v into time.Time if needed and formats it with the given layout.

<a name="Namespace.In"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [In](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/time/time.go#L92>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) In(timeZoneName string, t time.Time) (time.Time, error)
```

In returns the time t in the IANA time zone specified by timeZoneName. If timeZoneName is "" or "UTC", the time is returned in UTC. If timeZoneName is "Local", the time is returned in the system's local time zone. Otherwise, timeZoneName must be a valid IANA location name \(e.g., "Europe/Oslo"\).

<a name="Namespace.Now"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [Now](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/time/time.go#L84>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) Now() time.Time
```

Now returns the current local time or \`clock\` time

<a name="Namespace.ParseDuration"></a>
### func \(\*Namespace\) [ParseDuration](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/time/time.go#L109>)

```go
func (ns *Namespace) ParseDuration(s any) (time.Duration, error)
```

ParseDuration parses the duration string s. A duration string is a possibly signed sequence of decimal numbers, each with optional fraction and a unit suffix, such as "300ms", "\-1.5h" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "ns", "us" \(or "µs"\), "ms", "s", "m", "h". See https://golang.org/pkg/time/#ParseDuration

```go
type StoreOptions struct {
    // The filesystem to use.
    Fs  afero.Fs

    // The logger to use.
    Log loggers.Logger

    // The path handler to use.
    PathParser *paths.PathParser

    // Set when --enableTemplateMetrics is set.
    Metrics metrics.Provider

    // All configured output formats.
    OutputFormats output.Formats

    // All configured media types.
    MediaTypes media.Types

    // The default output format.
    DefaultOutputFormat string

    // Taxonomy config.
    TaxonomySingularPlural map[string]string

    // Whether we are in watch or server mode.
    Watching bool

    // The render hook configuration.
    RenderHooks gc.RenderHooks
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

```go
}
```

```go
type TemplWithBaseApplied struct {
    // The template that's overlaid on top of the base template.
    Overlay *TemplInfo

    Base *TemplInfo
    // This is the final template that can be used to render a page.
```

<a name="TemplateDescriptor"></a>
## type [TemplateDescriptor](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/tplimpl/templatedescriptor.go#L25-L44>)

This is used both as a key and in lookups.

```go
type TemplateDescriptor struct {
    // Group 1.
    Kind               string // page, home, section, taxonomy, term (and only those)
    LayoutFromTemplate string // list, single, all,mycustomlayout
    LayoutFromUser     string //  custom layout set in front matter, e.g. list, single, all, mycustomlayout

    // Group 2.
    OutputFormat string // rss, csv ...
    MediaType    string // text/html, text/plain, ...

    SitesHash uint64

    Variant1 string // contextual variant, e.g. "link" in render hooks."
    Variant2 string // contextual variant, e.g. "id" in render.

    // Misc.
    LayoutFromUserMustMatch bool // If set, we only look for the exact layout.
    IsPlainText             bool // Whether this is a plain text template.
    AlwaysAllowPlainText    bool // Whether to e.g. allow plain text templates to be rendered in HTML.
}
```




<a name="TemplateQuery"></a>
## type [TemplateQuery](<https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/blob/master/tpl/tplimpl/templatestore.go#L387-L405>)

TemplateQuery is used in LookupPagesLayout to find the best matching template.

```go
type TemplateQuery struct {
    // The path to walk down to.
    Path string

    // The name to look for. Used for shortcode queries.
    Name string

    // The category to look in.
    Category Category

    // The sites variants to consider.
    Sites sitesmatrix.VectorProvider

    // The template descriptor to match against.
    Desc TemplateDescriptor

    // Whether to even consider this candidate.
    Consider func(candidate *TemplInfo) bool
}
```
